longbow.name: longbow
longbow.description: A tall, recurved bow crafted from flexible Sylvera wood. It is favored for its range and accuracy. Dmg Range: 1 to 6.

steel blade.name: steel blade
steel blade.description: A standard-issue, razor-sharp straight sword used by the Sylveran Guard. Its weight is perfectly balanced for agile strikes. Dmg Range: 1 to 6.

elven longbow.name: elven longbow
elven longbow.description: A masterwork bow infused with forest magic, allowing for unparalleled precision. It hums slightly when drawn. Dmg Range: 1 to 6. Item Bonus: +2.

repeating rifle.name: repeating rifle
repeating rifle.description: A complex firearm with a rotating chamber for multiple shots. It represents the pinnacle of Republic industrial might. Dmg Range: 2 to 12.

ceremonial glaive.name: ceremonial glaive
ceremonial glaive.description: An ornate polearm carried by high-ranking Crown Glaives. Its gleaming blade is polished to a mirror finish. Dmg Range: 1 to 8.

shadow cloak.name: shadow cloak
shadow cloak.description: A tattered-looking garment that seems to absorb ambient light. It helps the wearer vanish into the night. Resistance: shadow 1.

greatspear.name: greatspear
greatspear.description: A long, heavy spear tipped with dragon-glass, designed for aerial combat and mounted charges. It is the signature weapon of the Queensguard Dragoons. Dmg Range: 1 to 8.

dragon scale.name: dragon scale
dragon scale.description: A shimmering, iridescent scale shed by a dragon, known for its incredible durability. It is a prized trade good of the Empire of Drakona.

crown signet.name: crown signet
crown signet.description: A gold-plated ring bearing the royal seal of the Holy Crown. It identifies the wearer as a high-ranking official of the kingdom.

valen gear.name: valen gear
valen gear.description: A complex set of iron cogs and springs essential for maintaining clockwork machinery. These precision components are a major export of the Republic of Valen.

scale armor.name: scale armor
scale armor.description: Drakona dragon-scale plate with layered scales over the torso, built to turn blades while still allowing aerial movement. Defense Bonus: +2. Damage Reduction: 3.

leather coat.name: leather coat
leather coat.description: A basic reinforced leather jacket for road wear, weather, and light scrapes. Defense Bonus: +1. Protection: travel wear, minor cuts, rough weather.

travel cloak.name: travel cloak
travel cloak.description: A durable traveler's cloak for rain, wind, dust, and long roads. Protection: weather and road wear.

tool kit.name: tool kit
tool kit.description: Set of machinist and crafting tools

holy symbol.name: holy symbol
holy symbol.description: A small sacred symbol of Balen’s fourfold faith, used by priests as a visible sign of ordination and as a focus for blessings, wards, and rites.

elven bow.name: elven bow
elven bow.description: A finely crafted Sylvera recurve bow shaped for quiet draw and clean release. Dmg Range: 1 to 6.

silver blade.name: silver blade
silver blade.description: A Sylvera silver-steel shortsword with a bright edge and light, balanced grip. Dmg Range: 1 to 6. Item Bonus: +1.

plate armor.name: plate armor
plate armor.description: Heavy steel plate armor covering the torso with rigid overlapping plates. Defense Bonus: +1. Damage Reduction: 4.

silver dagger.name: silver dagger
silver dagger.description: An ornate Sylvera silver dagger suited for quick cuts, ritual use, and hidden defense. Dmg Range: 1 to 4. Item Bonus: +1.

diplomatic seal.name: diplomatic seal
diplomatic seal.description: An ornate seal representing your nation's authority to negotiate on behalf of your sovereign.

fine robes.name: fine robes
fine robes.description: Elegant garments befitting a person of political standing, embroidered with your nation's symbols.

senatorial seal.name: senatorial seal
senatorial seal.description: The official seal of a Republic Senator, granting authority to speak for the people in matters of state.

signet ring.name: signet ring
signet ring.description: A ring bearing your personal or familial crest, used to authenticate documents and mark your authority.

basic handling whistle.name: basic handling whistle
basic handling whistle.description: A simple whistle used by handlers for trained pets, hounds, horses, pack animals, and other obedient creatures. It is not tied to a specific companion. +1 Tamer checks for ordinary trained animals when appropriate

companion call item.name: companion call item
companion call item.description: A named whistle, bell, charm, horn, scent token, carved bone, or other trinket tied to one specific tamed pet or companion. The narrator should create it immediately when the creature is successfully tamed or purchased. Name format: [Pet Name]'s [item type], such as Kaethin's bone whistle. Calls that creature if nearby and physically able; does not teleport. Allows recall of a specific Companion or Loyal creature when conditions allow

wyvern bone whistle.name: wyvern bone whistle
wyvern bone whistle.description: A generic wyvern call-item type. When assigned to a specific wyvern, rename it using the pet's name, such as Kaethin's bone whistle. Used to call that specific wyvern when nearby and physically able to answer. Calls a specific wyvern Companion if nearby and able to answer

reinforced handling gloves.name: reinforced handling gloves
reinforced handling gloves.description: Thick gloves reinforced with leather and small plates, used for feeding, calming, or handling biting, clawing, or taloned creatures. Defense Bonus: +1. Protection: bites, claws, handling dangerous tameable creatures.

named companion call item template.name: named companion call item template
named companion call item template.description: Template for a named pet call item. Format: [Pet Name]'s [item type]. Example: Kaethin's bone whistle. Flavor text should state that the item calls that specific pet when nearby and physically able to answer. Calls one specific tamed pet or companion when conditions allow

Kaethin's bone whistle.name: Kaethin's bone whistle
Kaethin's bone whistle.description: Example named companion call item. A rasping bone whistle tuned to Kaethin's hearing and training. Used to call Kaethin when the wyvern is nearby and physically able to answer. Calls Kaethin if nearby and able to answer

Balen Slayer's Bestiary.name: Balen Slayer's Bestiary
Balen Slayer's Bestiary.description: A field-worn reference book used by Slayers, hunters, scholars, and monster harvesters across Balen. It contains public creature lore, Slayer annotations, track sketches, weak-point diagrams, harvest notes, regional warnings, and blank pages for new observations. When used, the player may look up known creatures, compare evidence to identify unknown threats, review weaknesses and harvestable materials, check taming status, and add new notes after encounters, study, harvesting, taming, or research. This is the canonical normal bestiary item; ordinary bestiaries, monster books, field guides, and Slayer guides should resolve to this item unless a special variant is explicitly intended.

Damaged Balen Slayer's Bestiary.name: Damaged Balen Slayer's Bestiary
Damaged Balen Slayer's Bestiary.description: A torn, stained, or partially ruined copy of the canonical Balen Slayer's Bestiary. It functions like the standard bestiary, but some entries may be missing, water-damaged, burned, outdated, or illegible.

Annotated Balen Slayer's Bestiary.name: Annotated Balen Slayer's Bestiary
Annotated Balen Slayer's Bestiary.description: A copy of the canonical Balen Slayer's Bestiary filled with personal field notes, sketches, corrections, regional observations, or warnings from a previous owner.

Kaelith's enchanted silver collar.name: Kaelith's enchanted silver collar
Kaelith's enchanted silver collar.description: A small enchanted silver collar crafted by Silas for Kaelith, the hatchling Skysunderer wyvern. The enchantment can either mute Kaelith's natural screeches to silence or enhance them into a loud sonic blast when needed. Sized for a young wyvern and made to shift with his growth.

enchantment.name: Screech Modulation
effects: Can mute Kaelith's chirps, trills, and screeches when silence is needed.
Can enhance Kaelith's screech into a loud sonic blast when commanded or threatened.
enchantment.activation: Responds to Kaelith's need and commands from Silas or Sylvareth.

Pyrrhos' enchanted silver collar.name: Pyrrhos' enchanted silver collar
Pyrrhos' enchanted silver collar.description: A small enchanted silver collar crafted by Silas for Pyrrhos, the hatchling Skysunderer wyvern. The enchantment keeps Pyrrhos warm in cold weather and can give him a faint incorporeal, ghostlike appearance that helps with stealth. Sized for a young wyvern and made to shift with his growth.

enchantment.name: Warmth and Wraithglimmer
effects: Keeps Pyrrhos comfortably warm in cold conditions.
Can make Pyrrhos appear faintly incorporeal or ghostlike, improving stealth and making his outline harder to read.
enchantment.activation: Passive warmth; stealth effect responds to command, danger, or Pyrrhos' need to hide.

Kaelith's bone whistle.name: Kaelith's bone whistle
Kaelith's bone whistle.description: A small bone whistle tuned to Kaelith's hearing and training. Used to call Kaelith when he is nearby and physically able to answer.

Pyrrhos' bone whistle.name: Pyrrhos' bone whistle
Pyrrhos' bone whistle.description: A small bone whistle tuned to Pyrrhos' hearing and training. Used to call Pyrrhos when he is nearby and physically able to answer.

Balen's Slayer's Bestiary.name: Balen's Slayer's Bestiary
Balen's Slayer's Bestiary.description: A field bestiary used by Slayers to identify monsters, track signs, and compare wounds, tracks, and behavior against known threats.

Ifrit Bane Bladedancer's Twinblade.name: Ifrit Bane Bladedancer's Twinblade
Ifrit Bane Bladedancer's Twinblade.description: Masterwork twin longswords forged by Silas using the horns of a slain Ifrit. The blades are fluted down the middle and almost sing when swung through the air. Fire makes the blades take on the sheen of burning obsidian. Dmg Range: 2 to 12. Item Bonus: +2. Extra Damage: 1 to 4 fire.

Furred Leg Plates.name: Furred Leg Plates
Furred Leg Plates.description: Leg plates made from the furs of a Ravager strapped to the shins of leather boots, keeping warm even in the cold of the north. Protection: leg strikes, caltrops, rough terrain. Resistance: cold 2.

Ninefold Silverwing Chain-Plate.name: Ninefold Silverwing Chain-Plate
Ninefold Silverwing Chain-Plate.description: Masterwork breastplate and scaled chainmail hybrid armor made by Silas as proof of his mastery. The Ninefold Sublime Lotus is engraved on the back with enchanted gemstones taking the place of the runes on the lotus petals. The scale chain is made of Sylvareth's molted silver scales and enchanted to be as lightweight as leather. Defense Bonus: +4. Damage Reduction: 2. Resistance: magic 2.

Polished Drakken Bracers.name: Polished Drakken Bracers
Polished Drakken Bracers.description: Steel bracers forged in Drakona with drakken silk lining. Protection: forearm strikes, disarms, weapon binds. Resistance: heat 1.

Mana Potions.name: Mana Potions
Mana Potions.description:
Mana Potions:
A small bundle of basic blue mana restorative vials sold under a generic apothecary label.
Effect: Each vial restores 10 to 20 Mana. Treat individual vials as Herbal Mana Potions when exact tier matters. Ineffective for characters with no personal mana pool.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks per vial.

Healing Potions.name: Healing Potions
Healing Potions.description:
Healing Potions:
A small bundle of basic red healing vials sold under a generic apothecary label.
Effect: Each vial restores 4 to 8 HP. Treat individual vials as Herbal Healing Potions when exact tier matters. Cannot repair shattered bones, lost tissue, or severe internal damage.
Value: 5 to 10 Copper Marks per vial.

Clean Bandages.name: Clean Bandages
Clean Bandages.description:
Clean Bandages:
Fresh boiled linen strips kept dry for field care.
Effect: Restores 1 to 2 HP when binding minor wounds, slowing bleeding, or stabilizing light injuries.
Value: 2 to 5 Copper Marks.

Silver Marks.name: Silver Marks
Silver Marks.description: Universal Balen currency. Silver Marks are the middle-tier coin; 1 Silver Mark equals 10 Copper Marks and 10 Silver Marks equal 1 Gold Mark. Currency item: stack this item instead of creating new money items.

Gold Marks.name: Gold Marks
Gold Marks.description: Universal Balen currency. Gold Marks are the high-tier coin; 1 Gold Mark equals 10 Silver Marks or 100 Copper Marks. Currency item: stack this item instead of creating new money items.

Copper Marks.name: Copper Marks
Copper Marks.description: Universal Balen currency. Copper Marks are the low-tier coin; 10 Copper Marks equal 1 Silver Mark. Currency item: stack this item instead of creating new money items.

greataxe.name: greataxe
greataxe.description: A heavy two-handed axe built for brutal chops, shield-breaking strikes, and Ravager battlefield charges. It rewards strength, commitment, and proper spacing. Dmg Range: 2 to 12.

traveling kit.name: traveling kit
traveling kit.description: A compact bundle of road essentials: bedroll straps, a tin cup, firestarter, needle and thread, soap scrap, cord, and small repair odds and ends for life between settlements.

Slayer certification card.name: Slayer certification card
Slayer certification card.description: An official Slayer Guild certification card identifying the bearer as a recognized member of the Slayers Guild and authorizing them to take legal contracts across Balen.

prayer token.name: prayer token
prayer token.description: A small holy token, charm, prayer bead, or stamped symbol marking a priest's faith and service.

simple vestments.name: simple vestments
simple vestments.description: Plain travel-worn religious clothing suitable for rites, temple service, and road ministry.

small scripture.name: small scripture
small scripture.description: A compact book or folded set of sacred teachings, prayers, rites, and moral instruction used by clergy while traveling.

prayer book.name: prayer book
prayer book.description: A compact traveling book of pantheon prayers, rites, funeral words, blessings, sanctuary customs, and temple doctrine used by ordained priests across Balen.

traveling vestments.name: traveling vestments
traveling vestments.description: Durable road-worn priestly clothing suitable for travel, temple service, field rites, funerals, sanctuary work, and public blessing without looking like court finery.

dagger.name: dagger
dagger.description: A simple short blade useful as a tool, last-resort weapon, and close-quarters sidearm. Dmg Range: 1 to 4.

shortsword.name: shortsword
shortsword.description: A modest one-handed sword suited for travelers, priests, guards, and anyone who needs a practical defensive sidearm. Dmg Range: 1 to 6.

alchemist's kit.name: alchemist's kit
alchemist's kit.description: A compact field kit of small burners, mixing rods, droppers, clamps, powders, salves, and sealed compartments for preparing medicines, stabilizers, poultices, and basic alchemical treatments on the road.

empty vial.name: empty vial
empty vial.description: A small clean glass vial used to hold medicines, blood samples, toxins, tinctures, monster fluids, alchemical reagents, or emergency doses. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving careful alchemical storage, sampling, measuring, or preserving small liquid reagents.

Trolls Blood Potion.name: Trolls Blood Potion
Trolls Blood Potion.description:
Trolls Blood Potion:
A legacy name for a pinnacle-grade troll-blood healing draught.
Effect: Restores 14 to 24 HP; rapidly seals deep wounds, knits flesh, and supports severe trauma recovery.
Value: 5 to 10 Silver Marks.

hunting sword.name: hunting sword
hunting sword.description: A practical one-handed field sword carried by Slayers, scouts, and road hunters. It is sturdy enough for camp work and balanced enough for close combat against beasts and monsters. Dmg Range: 1 to 6.

harvesting knife.name: harvesting knife
harvesting knife.description: A sturdy Slayer Guild knife used for skinning, cutting sinew, removing glands, cleaning hides, and harvesting useful monster parts without ruining valuable materials.

Slayer contract seal.name: Slayer contract seal
Slayer contract seal.description: A small stamped guild seal used to mark accepted Slayer contracts, confirm completed work, and identify paperwork handled through a recognized Slayer Guild branch.

field kit.name: field kit
field kit.description: A compact kit of cord, chalk, waxed cloth, simple stakes, specimen wraps, tinder, hooks, and small utility tools for wilderness investigation, camp setup, and fieldwork.

Twin Sabers.name: Twin Sabers
Twin Sabers.description: A matched pair of curved dueling blades used by aggressive Bladesingers who favor sweeping, flame-building arcs. This item represents both sabers as one paired fighting style. Dmg Range: 2 to 12. Bonus: +1 to Pyromantic Bladesinging checks while wielded as a pair.

Dueling Saber.name: Dueling Saber
Dueling Saber.description: A balanced single saber favored by fluid duelists and Hydromantic Bladesingers. Its curve supports smooth cuts, defensive feints, and flowing reversals. Dmg Range: 1 to 8. Bonus: +1 to Hydromantic Bladesinging checks and defensive feints.

Dual Daggers.name: Dual Daggers
Dual Daggers.description: A matched pair of light fighting daggers balanced for rapid footwork, close-angle cuts, and sudden Aeromantic changes of direction. This item represents both daggers as one paired fighting style. Dmg Range: 2 to 8. Bonus: +1 to Aeromantic Bladesinging checks and evasive footwork.

Estoc.name: Estoc
Estoc.description: A narrow thrusting sword built to pierce gaps, drive clean lines, and reward precise Fulmantic timing. Dmg Range: 1 to 8. Bonus: +1 to Fulmantic Bladesinging checks and precise thrusting attacks.

Duelist's Jacket.name: Duelist's Jacket
Duelist's Jacket.description: A fitted, reinforced jacket favored by Bladesingers, duelists, and traveling blade instructors. Flexible enough for dance-like footwork and blade forms, but sturdy enough to protect against scrapes, glancing cuts, and rough travel. Bonus: +1 to Bladesinging/Sublime Arts checks involving footwork, dueling, rhythm, dodging, or maintaining movement.

Twin Axes.name: Twin Axes
Twin Axes.description: A matched pair of brutal one-handed axes favored by Ravagers who prefer hacking momentum, alternating chops, and relentless close-range pressure. This item represents both axes as one paired fighting style. Dmg Range: 2 to 12. Bonus: +1 to Ravager Sublime Arts checks involving aggressive charges, guard breaks, or rending defenses.

warhammer.name: warhammer
warhammer.description: A heavy battlefield hammer built for crushing armor, breaking guards, staggering foes, and driving force through shields. It rewards strength, timing, and commitment. Dmg Range: 1 to 10. Bonus: +1 to Ravager Sublime Arts checks involving shoves, guard breaks, armor-crushing blows, or battlefield charges.

longsword.name: longsword
longsword.description: A standard knightly one-handed sword balanced for cuts, thrusts, guards, and shield work. Dmg Range: 1 to 8.

Knight's Shield.name: Knight's Shield
Knight's Shield.description: A sturdy knightly shield built for blocks, shield-wall discipline, and close-quarters control. When equipped in the secondary slot, it grants +1 physical Damage Resistance through its shield rule rather than a normal item bonus. It can also be used for Shield Bash attacks. Shield Bash Dmg Range: 1 to 2. A successful shield bash may stun, stagger, interrupt, or knock an enemy off-balance.

Chain Shirt.name: Chain Shirt
Chain Shirt.description: A practical mail shirt worn beneath or over clothing, favored by traveling knights who need protection without full battlefield plate. Defense Bonus: +1. Damage Reduction: 2.

Knight's Tabard.name: Knight's Tabard
Knight's Tabard.description: A formal tabard bearing house, order, or oathbound colors. It marks the wearer as a knight or knightly retainer and carries social expectation as much as decoration. Bonus: +1 to formal knightly persuasion, oath, heraldry, or recognized-station checks where the tabard matters.

Arming Sword.name: Arming Sword
Arming Sword.description: A practical one-handed sword favored by professional soldiers, caravan guards, and mercenaries. Less ceremonial than a knightly blade and more reliable than a cheap camp weapon. Dmg Range: 1 to 8.

Brigandine Vest.name: Brigandine Vest
Brigandine Vest.description: A sturdy vest of layered cloth or leather reinforced with small metal plates, favored by mercenaries who need protection without looking like formal knights. Defense Bonus: +1. Damage Reduction: 1. Bonus: +1 to athletics checks involving bracing, guarding, or enduring rough combat movement.

Folded Mercenary Banner.name: Folded Mercenary Banner
Folded Mercenary Banner.description: A carefully folded banner bearing the sigil of the mercenary band you once marched with. It may represent pride, loss, exile, loyalty, betrayal, or simply proof that you once belonged to a professional company of blades. Bonus: +1 to checks involving mercenary reputation, company history, military recognition, or intimidating those who know the banner.

whetstone.name: whetstone
whetstone.description: A small sharpening stone used to maintain blades, axes, knives, and field tools during travel. Useful for keeping a weapon serviceable after rough fighting or hard road use. Bonus: +1 to blade maintenance, sharpening, weapon appraisal, or preparing a blade before combat or travel.

Rune-Etched Arming Sword.name: Rune-Etched Arming Sword
Rune-Etched Arming Sword.description: A practical arming sword etched with simple rune channels along the fuller and guard, built to accept quick combat inscriptions and controlled mana release. Dmg Range: 1 to 8. Bonus: +1 to Runic Magic and +1 to bladed arts when used for Spellsword techniques.

Reinforced Coat.name: Reinforced Coat
Reinforced Coat.description: A sturdy travel coat reinforced with hidden plates, leather panels, and rune-safe seams. It protects without interfering with blade work or inscription movement. Defense Bonus: +1. Damage Reduction: 1. Bonus: +1 to Runic Magic checks involving safe inscription movement or maintaining rune-safe seams under pressure.

Engraving Stylus.name: Engraving Stylus
Engraving Stylus.description: A fine metal stylus used to carve small runes, sigils, guide marks, and magic-circle details into wax, wood, leather, soft metal, chalked surfaces, or prepared equipment.

Inscription Chalk.name: Inscription Chalk
Inscription Chalk.description:
Inscription Chalk:
Mana-reactive chalk used for temporary runes, circles, and teaching marks.
Effect: Enables basic runic drawing on stone, wood, metal, or prepared surfaces.
Value: 5 to 12 Copper Marks.

shortbow.name: shortbow
shortbow.description: A compact hunting and scouting bow built for quiet draw, short-range accuracy, and use in woods, ruins, alleys, or broken terrain. Dmg Range: 1 to 6. Bonus: +1 to archery checks made from cover, ambush, or careful positioning.

Scout's Map Case.name: Scout's Map Case
Scout's Map Case.description: A weatherproof case containing charcoal, folded route notes, waxed scraps, and simple mapping tools used to mark trails, patrol routes, campsites, ambush points, and hazards. Bonus: +1 to survival and investigation checks involving routes, terrain, hazards, patrol paths, or safe approaches.

Arcane Focus.name: Arcane Focus
Arcane Focus.description: A hand-held focus such as a crystal, wand, carved charm, ringed rod, or polished lens used to stabilize personal spellcasting and shape raw mana with better control. Bonus: +1 to Arcanist spellcasting checks.

Arcanist's Spellbook.name: Arcanist's Spellbook
Arcanist's Spellbook.description: A bound working spellbook filled with formulae, diagrams, spell notes, corrected theories, mana-flow sketches, and personal experiments. It is a living record of the Arcanist’s study rather than a fixed list of memorized spells. Bonus: +1 to Arcane Analysis and Book Casting where the book can be consulted.

Ink Vial.name: Ink Vial
Ink Vial.description: A small sealed vial of dark ink suitable for spell notes, corrections, diagrams, labels, formula work, and quick arcane sketches. Bonus: +1 to Runic Magic or scholarly writing checks involving clean spell notes, formula correction, copied sigils, or precise diagrams.

Traveling Robe.name: Traveling Robe
Traveling Robe.description: A practical robe cut for travel, study, and spellcasting, with reinforced hems, inner pockets, and loose sleeves that do not interfere with gestures. Slot: clothes. Bonus: +1 to performance checks involving controlled spellcasting gestures, ritual posture, or formal presentation.

Runescribe Kit.name: Runescribe Kit
Runescribe Kit.description: A compact ritual kit containing measuring cord, guide rings, wax tabs, pigment cups, cleaning cloths, seal needles, fine brushes, and small tools for preparing runes, body-circles, parchment seals, and temporary magic-circle work. Bonus: +1 to Runic Magic when preparing or repairing runes and circles.

Ritual Ink.name: Ritual Ink
Ritual Ink.description:
Ritual Ink:
Dark ink mixed with binding salts and mana-reactive pigment.
Effect: Enables durable runes, circle work, scroll notes, and ritual diagrams.
Value: 1 to 3 Silver Marks.

Blank Parchment.name: Blank Parchment
Blank Parchment.description: Folded sheets of prepared parchment suitable for notes, temporary seals, simple scrolls, copied runes, circle diagrams, and emergency written spell structures. Bonus: +1 to Runic Magic or research checks involving temporary seals, copied inscriptions, field notes, scroll drafts, or diagram storage.

rune chalk.name: rune chalk
rune chalk.description: A bundle of chalk used for temporary runes and magic circles. Prefer Inscription Chalk as the canonical item name.

ink set.name: ink set
ink set.description: A compact ink set used for runes, notes, and magical diagrams. Prefer Ritual Ink or Ink Vial as canonical item names.

copper stylus.name: copper stylus
copper stylus.description: A copper stylus for etching runes into soft materials. Prefer Engraving Stylus as canonical item name.

blank seals.name: blank seals
blank seals.description: Prepared blank seal papers for simple rune work. Prefer Blank Parchment as canonical item name.

Tamer's Whistle.name: Tamer's Whistle
Tamer's Whistle.description: A simple whistle tuned for clear field signals, calming calls, recall patterns, and short command notes. Bonus: +1 to Tamer checks involving commands, recall, calming, or Wild Call to Aid.

Feed Pouch.name: Feed Pouch
Feed Pouch.description: A small waterproof pouch carrying feed pellets, dried meat scraps, seed mix, scent lures, and simple creature treats for calming or rewarding tameable animals and monsters. Bonus: +1 to Tamer checks involving feeding, baiting, calming, or trust-building.

Handling Gloves.name: Handling Gloves
Handling Gloves.description: Thick but flexible gloves for handling claws, beaks, teeth, spines, rough scales, nervous bites, and rope burns without losing fine control. Bonus: +1 to Tamer checks involving safe handling, restraint, feeding, or first contact.

Training Lead.name: Training Lead
Training Lead.description: A durable lead, loop-line, and soft restraint set for guiding, grounding, calming, or beginning command training with tameable creatures. Bonus: +1 to Tamer checks involving restraint, relocation, walking, or early command work.

Snare Kit.name: Snare Kit
Snare Kit.description: A compact set of cord, soft loops, stakes, padded catches, spring pegs, scent ties, and small weights used to build humane snares that restrain or slow without maiming. Bonus: +1 to Tamer checks involving Set Humane Snare, capture, rescue, or relocation.

Scholar's Journal.name: Scholar's Journal
Scholar's Journal.description: A sturdy field journal filled with notes, copied inscriptions, references, sketches, questions, corrections, and blank pages for future discoveries. Bonus: +1 to Cross-Reference when notes can be consulted.

Writing Kit.name: Writing Kit
Writing Kit.description: A compact scholar's writing bundle with quills, charcoal sticks, wax tabs, scraping knife, folded note slips, and small tools for copying records, labels, maps, and inscriptions.

Reference Notes.name: Reference Notes
Reference Notes.description: A bundle of condensed academic references: dates, symbols, legal marks, religious formulas, translation fragments, names, dynastic notes, ruins shorthand, and useful research prompts. Bonus: +1 to Broad Education checks when relevant.

Crowbar.name: Crowbar
Crowbar.description: A compact iron pry bar used to lift stuck lids, wedge doors, test loose stones, force old hinges, and apply leverage in ruins. Bonus: +1 to Dungeoneer checks involving prying, forcing, wedging, or testing mechanisms.

Lockpicks.name: Lockpicks
Lockpicks.description: A small roll of picks, hooks, tension bars, shims, and fine probes used to open locks and manipulate delicate mechanisms. Bonus: +1 to Disarm Mechanism when dealing with locks, latches, pins, or fine mechanical traps.

Rope.name: Rope
Rope.description: A coil of sturdy travel rope useful for climbing, lowering gear, tying anchors, crossing gaps, restraining objects, and surviving vertical dungeon spaces. Bonus: +1 to survival checks involving climbing support, anchoring, lowering gear, binding loads, or safe traversal.

Lantern.name: Lantern
Lantern.description:
Lantern:
A metal-framed oil lantern with glass panes and a carrying loop.
Effect: Provides steady light when filled with lantern oil; safer than an open torch.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Chalk.name: Chalk
Chalk.description: A bundle of chalk sticks used to mark explored passages, danger signs, return routes, checked doors, suspect floors, and coded delver marks. Bonus: +1 to Delver's Mark when route marking matters.

Dungeoneer's Pack.name: Dungeoneer's Pack
Dungeoneer's Pack.description: A practical delving bundle with small wedges, twine, waxed cloth, spare hooks, tiny bells, oilcloth scraps, dusting brush, charcoal, and other odds and ends for cautious ruin exploration. Bonus: +1 to Dungeoneer checks involving traps, hazards, and dungeon preparation.

Draconic Breathing Charm.name: Draconic Breathing Charm
Draconic Breathing Charm.description: A small charm, throat cord, carved scale-token, or bead used to steady breathing rhythm during Dracomancy practice. It is not a weapon and does not grant Dracomancy by itself. Bonus: +1 to Dracomancy breath-control checks or recovering composure after breath strain.

Powder Measure.name: Powder Measure
Powder Measure.description: A small calibrated brass measure used to portion blackpowder safely and consistently. Bonus: +1 to gunpowder handling checks involving safe loading, maintenance, misfire prevention, or powder measurement.

Practice Focus.name: Practice Focus
Practice Focus.description: A small inexpensive focus used for basic mana-control exercises, spell-shaping drills, and sensing mana behavior. It is weaker than a proper caster's focus. Bonus: +1 to basic mana-control, spell-shaping, or identifying simple mana behavior.

Softstep Wraps.name: Softstep Wraps
Softstep Wraps.description: Dark cloth wraps, padded bindings, or muffling strips used around boots, buckles, straps, and loose gear to reduce noise while moving. Bonus: +1 to stealth checks involving quiet movement or reducing gear noise.

Unsent Letter.name: Unsent Letter
Unsent Letter.description: A sealed, unfinished, or carefully folded letter tied to something the bearer hopes to make right. Its exact meaning is left open to the character.

Unmarked Map Fragment.name: Unmarked Map Fragment
Unmarked Map Fragment.description: A partial map with no clear origin, suggesting a place, route, ruin, border, or question still waiting to be found.

Oath Cord.name: Oath Cord
Oath Cord.description: A simple cord, ribbon, braid, or charm kept as a reminder of a promise, role, duty, or obligation.

Travel-Worn Token.name: Travel-Worn Token
Travel-Worn Token.description: A small personal token worn smooth by travel, kept as a reminder that the bearer’s path is their own.

Training Tally.name: Training Tally
Training Tally.description: A marked strip, bead chain, carved counter, or practice record used to track improvement, repetition, and personal discipline.

Keepsake Charm.name: Keepsake Charm
Keepsake Charm.description: A small personal keepsake connected to someone, someplace, or something the bearer wants to protect.

Old Challenge Token.name: Old Challenge Token
Old Challenge Token.description: A token, ribbon, marked scrap, or keepsake from a contest, duel, performance, wager, or public ambition.

Marked Name.name: Marked Name
Marked Name.description: A folded note, symbol, sketch, or name tied to someone or something the bearer has not forgotten.

Strange Rubbing.name: Strange Rubbing
Strange Rubbing.description: A charcoal rubbing, copied symbol, or fragment of writing that hints at something dangerous, obscure, or hidden.

Personal Standard.name: Personal Standard
Personal Standard.description: A small emblem, practice mark, token, or written goal representing the standard the bearer is trying to meet.

Basic Smithing Tools.name: Basic Smithing Tools
Basic Smithing Tools.description: A compact apprentice smithing roll with a small hammer, tongs, files, punch, measuring cord, chalk, and repair odds-and-ends. It is enough for basic repairs, fittings, edge touch-ups, and simple field metalwork, but it is not a full forge. Bonus: +1 to Blacksmithing checks involving basic repairs, fittings, maintenance, or simple ironwork.

Basic Alchemy Tools.name: Basic Alchemy Tools
Basic Alchemy Tools.description: A compact beginner alchemy roll with small vials, droppers, mixing rods, corks, labels, a tiny burner, and sample packets. It supports simple field mixtures, poultices, solvents, and stabilizers, but it is not a full laboratory. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving basic alchemy, potion handling, poultices, stabilizers, or sample preparation.

Sewing Kit.name: Sewing Kit
Sewing Kit.description: A small kit of needles, thread, patches, buttons, pins, measuring cord, and folded scrap cloth for mending and fitting garments on the road. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving sewing, cloth repair, garment fitting, seam reinforcement, or fabric salvage.

Leatherworking Tools.name: Leatherworking Tools
Leatherworking Tools.description: A compact set of awls, needles, waxed thread, small punches, oil cloth, edge tools, and scraps for repairing straps, belts, boots, armor ties, and leather gear. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving leather repair, fitting, stitching, oiling, or simple leatherwork.

Jeweler's Tools.name: Jeweler's Tools
Jeweler's Tools.description: A delicate roll of tweezers, tiny files, polishing cloth, setting picks, wire, loupe, and fine clamps for small metalwork and gem handling. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving jewelry repair, stone setting, polishing, fine fittings, or small-value appraisal.

Woodworking Tools.name: Woodworking Tools
Woodworking Tools.description: A compact woodworking bundle with carving knife, small saw, awl, measuring cord, pegs, wedges, sandcloth, and wood glue for field repairs and simple shaping. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving wood repair, carving, handles, pegs, boxes, frames, or simple construction.

Cooking Kit.name: Cooking Kit
Cooking Kit.description: A small travel cook kit with a tin pan, spice twist, knife, spoon, salt pouch, wrapped herbs, cloth strainer, and compact utensils for camp meals and food preservation. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving cooking, ration preparation, preservation, camp meals, or making limited supplies last.

Scribe's Kit.name: Scribe's Kit
Scribe's Kit.description: A compact writing and copying kit with quills, charcoal, wax tabs, spare nibs, sand, folded note slips, a scraping knife, and small containers for ink and sealing. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving copying documents, maintaining ledgers, preparing ink, labels, or preserving written records.

Drakken Service Sabre.name: Drakken Service Sabre
Drakken Service Sabre.description: A standard-issue Drakken naval sabre with a curved single edge, built for shipboard fighting, sky-patrol sidearm use, and disciplined service cuts. Dmg Range: 1 to 6. Bonus: +1 to bladed arts checks while used as a service sidearm.

Drakken Leather Armor.name: Drakken Leather Armor
Drakken Leather Armor.description: Flexible Drakken leather armor issued to standard naval and sky-patrol personnel. It is lighter and more practical than dragon-scale armor while still protecting against blades, weather, and rough aerial service. Defense Bonus: +1. Damage Reduction: 2.

Dracosilk Clothes.name: Dracosilk Clothes
Dracosilk Clothes.description: Fine Drakona clothing woven from Dracosilk, prized for its lightness, sheen, heat tolerance, and graceful drape. Appropriate for artisans, sky-city professionals, and refined civilian wear. Bonus: +1 to Crafting checks involving fine handwork, presentation, or delicate material handling.

musket.name: musket
musket.description: A Republic blackpowder musket built for disciplined line fire, smoke-filled volleys, and industrial-age military service. Slower and simpler than experimental repeaters. Dmg Range: 2 to 12. Bonus: +1 to musketry and +1 to gunpowder handling checks while using or maintaining it.

service pistol.name: service pistol
service pistol.description: A compact Republic blackpowder sidearm carried by officers, guards, and specialists. Useful at close range when a long firearm is impractical. Dmg Range: 1 to 8. Bonus: +1 to gunpowder handling checks with pistols.

Republic Service Sabre.name: Republic Service Sabre
Republic Service Sabre.description: A practical Republic officer and cavalry-style sabre with a curved blade suited for command sidearm use, quick cuts, and close-quarters defense. Dmg Range: 1 to 6. Bonus: +1 to bladed arts checks while used as a service sidearm.

Silver Scimitar.name: Silver Scimitar
Silver Scimitar.description: A curved Sylveran silver-steel blade made for sweeping cuts, forest footwork, and graceful wardens or rangers. Dmg Range: 1 to 6. Item Bonus: +1 to bladed arts checks.

Silver Half Plate.name: Silver Half Plate
Silver Half Plate.description: Sylveran silver-inlaid half plate shaped for forest wardens who need real protection without losing mobility. Defense Bonus: +1. Damage Reduction: 3. Bonus: +1 to survival checks in Sylveran wilds or forest patrol conditions.

Field Dressing Kit.name: Field Dressing Kit
Field Dressing Kit.description:
Field Dressing Kit:
A compact kit of bandages, needle, thread, salve, splint ties, and pressure wraps.
Effect: Restores 2 to 4 HP with proper field treatment; stabilizes moderate bleeding, shock, and travel-threatening wounds.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Surgeon's Field Kit.name: Surgeon's Field Kit
Surgeon's Field Kit.description:
Surgeon's Field Kit:
Professional tools for surgery, suturing, bone-setting, antiseptic care, and severe wound treatment.
Effect: Restores 4 to 6 HP during extended treatment outside immediate danger; requires time and medical skill.
Value: 2 to 5 Silver Marks.

Herbal Healing Potion.name: Herbal Healing Potion
Herbal Healing Potion.description:
Herbal Healing Potion:
A simple green-brown potion brewed from medicinal herbs, roots, and mild stabilizers.
Effect: Restores 4 to 8 HP; eases pain, slows bleeding, and supports minor to moderate wound recovery.
Value: 5 to 10 Copper Marks.

Troll Drop Potion.name: Troll Drop Potion
Troll Drop Potion.description:
Troll Drop Potion:
A stronger restorative made with diluted troll essence, bitter herbs, and binding reagents.
Effect: Restores 8 to 14 HP; closes wounds, reduces bleeding, and knits torn muscle, but cannot regrow limbs.
Value: 1 to 3 Silver Marks.

Troll's Blood Draught.name: Troll's Blood Draught
Troll's Blood Draught.description:
Troll's Blood Draught:
A pinnacle-grade draught brewed from excess troll blood and rare alchemical binders.
Effect: Restores 14 to 24 HP; rapidly seals deep wounds, knits flesh, and supports severe trauma recovery.
Value: 5 to 10 Silver Marks.

Herbal Mana Potion.name: Herbal Mana Potion
Herbal Mana Potion.description:
Herbal Mana Potion:
A pale blue-green potion brewed from mana-sensitive herbs and springwater.
Effect: Restores 10 to 20 Mana and eases minor mana fatigue; ineffective without a personal mana pool.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Condensed Mana Drop.name: Condensed Mana Drop
Condensed Mana Drop.description:
Condensed Mana Drop:
A concentrated bead of refined mana essence in a bitter alchemical solution.
Effect: Restores 25 to 40 Mana and steadies moderate spellcasting strain; ineffective without a personal mana pool.
Value: 2 to 4 Silver Marks.

Condensed Mana Draught.name: Condensed Mana Draught
Condensed Mana Draught.description:
Condensed Mana Draught:
A dense shimmering mana restorative made from condensed essence, stabilizing salts, and rare reagents.
Effect: Restores 50 to 75 Mana; helps severe mana exhaustion but does not undo Mana Burn or overdraw injury.
Value: 6 to 10 Silver Marks.

Ginseng Extract.name: Ginseng Extract
Ginseng Extract.description:
Ginseng Extract:
A sharp earthy stimulant made from boiled ginseng root, bitter herbs, and warming spirits.
Effect: Restores 10 to 20 Stamina; helps light fatigue and minor Sublime Arts strain.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Tigerroot Tonic.name: Tigerroot Tonic
Tigerroot Tonic.description:
Tigerroot Tonic:
A strong amber tonic brewed from tigerroot, pepperbark, honey, and stamina-reactive salts.
Effect: Restores 25 to 40 Stamina; helps combat fatigue, muscle burn, and repeated technique use.
Value: 2 to 4 Silver Marks.

Lotus Extract.name: Lotus Extract
Lotus Extract.description:
Lotus Extract:
A potent martial restorative distilled from rare lotus root, mineral salts, and breath-sharpening stimulants.
Effect: Restores 50 to 75 Stamina; renews combat rhythm and severe martial strain, but does not heal wounds.
Value: 6 to 10 Silver Marks.

Willowbark Antitoxin.name: Willowbark Antitoxin
Willowbark Antitoxin.description:
Willowbark Antitoxin:
A bitter antitoxin brewed from willowbark, charcoal, and cleansing herbs.
Effect: Grants a bonus against common poisons and reduces minor poison symptoms for one scene.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Verdant Antidote.name: Verdant Antidote
Verdant Antidote.description:
Verdant Antidote:
A Sylveran antidote of root extracts, river salts, and medicinal leaf.
Effect: Counters moderate venoms and grants poison resistance for one scene.
Value: 2 to 4 Silver Marks.

Blackroot Counteragent.name: Blackroot Counteragent
Blackroot Counteragent.description:
Blackroot Counteragent:
A rare blackroot antidote prepared for severe monster venom and alchemical toxins.
Effect: Grants strong poison resistance for one scene and may halt lethal venom if used quickly.
Value: 6 to 10 Silver Marks.

Feverfew Tonic.name: Feverfew Tonic
Feverfew Tonic.description:
Feverfew Tonic:
A cooling medicinal tonic of feverfew, mint, and clean spirit.
Effect: Reduces fever, chills, nausea, and minor disease symptoms during recovery.
Value: 5 to 10 Copper Marks.

Roseguard Remedy.name: Roseguard Remedy
Roseguard Remedy.description:
Roseguard Remedy:
A Holy Kingdom remedy of rosehip, honey, temple herbs, and clean spirits.
Effect: Grants a bonus against disease progression and supports recovery over a rest.
Value: 1 to 3 Silver Marks.

Burn Salve.name: Burn Salve
Burn Salve.description:
Burn Salve:
A thick cooling salve of aloe, rendered fat, clay, and soothing herbs.
Effect: Treats burns, reduces pain, and grants minor fire-burn resistance during recovery.
Value: 5 to 12 Copper Marks.

Frostbite Balm.name: Frostbite Balm
Frostbite Balm.description:
Frostbite Balm:
A warming balm of resin, pepper oil, and mountain herbs.
Effect: Treats frostbite, restores feeling to chilled limbs, and grants minor cold exposure resistance.
Value: 5 to 12 Copper Marks.

Wound Poultice.name: Wound Poultice
Wound Poultice.description:
Wound Poultice:
A packed herbal dressing for infection-prone cuts and punctures.
Effect: Supports wound recovery, lowers infection risk, and improves mundane healing checks.
Value: 4 to 9 Copper Marks.

Trail Rations.name: Trail Rations
Trail Rations.description:
Trail Rations:
Dried meat, hard bread, nuts, and preserved fruit packed for travel.
Effect: Feeds one person for one day and prevents hunger penalties during normal travel.
Value: 3 to 6 Copper Marks.

Hearty Travel Meal.name: Hearty Travel Meal
Hearty Travel Meal.description:
Hearty Travel Meal:
A filling packed meal of smoked meat, cheese, bread, and spiced vegetables.
Effect: Improves morale and grants a small recovery bonus during the next short rest.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Stimulant Chew.name: Stimulant Chew
Stimulant Chew.description:
Stimulant Chew:
A bitter chew of tea leaf, pepperbark, and wakeful herbs.
Effect: Delays fatigue and drowsiness for one scene, but does not replace sleep.
Value: 4 to 8 Copper Marks.

Fire Oil.name: Fire Oil
Fire Oil.description:
Fire Oil:
A volatile red oil used to coat blades, arrowheads, or thrown flasks.
Effect: Adds minor fire damage or ignition risk for 3 successful hits or one thrown splash.
Value: 1 to 3 Silver Marks.

Frost Oil.name: Frost Oil
Frost Oil.description:
Frost Oil:
A pale alchemical oil that chills metal and stiffens flesh on contact.
Effect: Adds minor frost damage or slowing effect for 3 successful hits.
Value: 1 to 3 Silver Marks.

Storm Oil.name: Storm Oil
Storm Oil.description:
Storm Oil:
A crackling conductive oil brewed with mineral salts and storm-reactive essence.
Effect: Adds minor lightning shock or interruption risk for 3 successful hits.
Value: 2 to 4 Silver Marks.

Silvering Oil.name: Silvering Oil
Silvering Oil.description:
Silvering Oil:
A temporary silvered coating for blades, arrows, or monster-hunting tools.
Effect: Lets one weapon count as silvered for one scene or 3 successful hits.
Value: 2 to 5 Silver Marks.

Sleep Sap Coating.name: Sleep Sap Coating
Sleep Sap Coating.description:
Sleep Sap Coating:
A sticky Sylveran sap used on darts, needles, or blade edges.
Effect: May cause drowsiness or brief sleep on a failed resistance check; strongest against small or weakened targets.
Value: 3 to 6 Silver Marks.

Bitterfang Venom.name: Bitterfang Venom
Bitterfang Venom.description:
Bitterfang Venom:
A dangerous monster-derived venom stored in a sealed black vial.
Effect: Poisons one weapon for 3 successful hits; may cause pain, weakness, or HP loss on failed resistance.
Value: 5 to 9 Silver Marks.

Smoke Bomb.name: Smoke Bomb
Smoke Bomb.description:
Smoke Bomb:
A clay sphere packed with smoke powder and a pull-cord fuse.
Effect: Creates a small smoke cloud for 2 to 3 rounds, aiding escape, stealth, or repositioning.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Flash Powder.name: Flash Powder
Flash Powder.description:
Flash Powder:
A paper twist of bright alchemical powder ignited by spark or fuse.
Effect: Creates a brief flash that may blind, distract, or interrupt nearby targets.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Fire Pot.name: Fire Pot
Fire Pot.description:
Fire Pot:
A sealed clay pot of sticky burning oil with a rag fuse.
Effect: Ignites a small area on impact and may cause ongoing fire damage until smothered.
Value: 1 to 3 Silver Marks.

Thundercrack Charge.name: Thundercrack Charge
Thundercrack Charge.description:
Thundercrack Charge:
A Republic blackpowder charge built for noise, shock, and disruption.
Effect: Creates a loud concussive blast that can stagger, frighten animals, or interrupt formations.
Value: 2 to 5 Silver Marks.

Tanglefoot Resin Flask.name: Tanglefoot Resin Flask
Tanglefoot Resin Flask.description:
Tanglefoot Resin Flask:
A glass flask of sticky resin that hardens when exposed to air.
Effect: Creates difficult footing in a small area and may slow or briefly restrain targets.
Value: 1 to 3 Silver Marks.

Monster Bait.name: Monster Bait
Monster Bait.description:
Monster Bait:
A foul bundle of bloodmeal, glands, and scent oils used by hunters and Slayers.
Effect: Attracts nearby beasts or monsters when their senses and hunger make it plausible.
Value: 6 to 12 Copper Marks.

Scent-Masking Paste.name: Scent-Masking Paste
Scent-Masking Paste.description:
Scent-Masking Paste:
A pungent paste of mud, herbs, and animal musk.
Effect: Masks humanoid scent for one scene, helping stealth against scent-tracking creatures.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Torch.name: Torch
Torch.description:
Torch:
A pitch-wrapped wooden torch suitable for travel and dungeon work.
Effect: Provides handheld light for one scene or about one hour; can ignite flammable material.
Value: 1 to 3 Copper Marks.

Lantern Oil.name: Lantern Oil
Lantern Oil.description:
Lantern Oil:
A small flask of clean-burning lamp oil.
Effect: Fuels a lantern for several hours and can be poured as an emergency fire starter.
Value: 2 to 5 Copper Marks.

Glowcap Vial.name: Glowcap Vial
Glowcap Vial.description:
Glowcap Vial:
A sealed vial of faintly glowing mushroom pulp.
Effect: Provides dim cold light for one scene without flame or smoke.
Value: 5 to 10 Copper Marks.

Charged Rune Slip.name: Charged Rune Slip
Charged Rune Slip.description:
Charged Rune Slip:
A small prepared paper rune holding a minor charge.
Effect: Releases a simple one-use runic effect such as spark, alarm, latch, or brief ward.
Value: 1 to 3 Silver Marks.

Circle Salt.name: Circle Salt
Circle Salt.description:
Circle Salt:
A pouch of pale salt mixed with mana-conductive powder.
Effect: Helps draw temporary circles, threshold wards, and basic containment lines.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Quickseal Wax.name: Quickseal Wax
Quickseal Wax.description:
Quickseal Wax:
A soft red wax used to seal rune lines, vials, wounds in gear, and field repairs.
Effect: Stabilizes temporary inscriptions or seals small leaks and cracks for one scene.
Value: 5 to 10 Copper Marks.

Gunpowder Pouch.name: Gunpowder Pouch
Gunpowder Pouch.description:
Gunpowder Pouch:
A waxed leather pouch for keeping blackpowder dry and portioned.
Effect: Supports loading and maintaining blackpowder weapons; required when powder supply matters.
Value: 8 to 15 Copper Marks.

Healing Draught.name: Healing Draught
Healing Draught.description:
Healing Draught:
A common medicinal draught used for battlefield and travel wounds.
Effect: Restores 4 to 8 HP and supports minor to moderate wound recovery.
Value: 5 to 10 Copper Marks.

Iron Ration.name: Iron Ration
Iron Ration.description:
Iron Ration:
Dense dried food packed for soldiers and hard travel.
Effect: Feeds one person for one day; unpleasant but durable.
Value: 2 to 4 Copper Marks.

Sylvera Leaf.name: Sylvera Leaf
Sylvera Leaf.description:
Sylvera Leaf:
A broad medicinal leaf from Sylveran herbal practice.
Effect: Supports poultices, antidotes, and minor healing preparations.
Value: 2 to 5 Copper Marks.

Balen.name: Balen
Balen.basicInfo: The mortal surface world and main explorable map of Balen, containing the four nations, Aethelgard neutral lands, roads, skies, cities, wilderness, ruins, and ordinary surface-world locations.
Balen.hiddenInfo: Main world realm. This is the default surface map for ordinary Balen play. Do not treat Vaulted City locations as part of this realm.

The Vaulted City.name: The Vaulted City
The Vaulted City.basicInfo: A collapsed Celestial pocket-space beneath Balen, holding the canted ruins of a dead skyborne metropolis.
The Vaulted City.hiddenInfo: Separate realm-space. The Vaulted City is dead beyond its seal: no bodies, bones, decay, or active inhabitants. Use location paths for plausible movement. The Abyssal Tower Vista is a jagged central chasm-edge, not an entrance to the Tower. The obsidian-black dust is a mystery in narration. Do not explain it or let casual NPCs identify it. No one should call it Shayde-ash, shadow-ash, daemon-ash, or remains unless a specific late-game revelation explicitly unlocks that truth.
The Vaulted City.navigationRule: Use realmRouteGraph for plausible travel between Vaulted City locations. Do not treat all POIs as freely reachable.
The Vaulted City.entranceRule: The Vaulted City is reached from Balen only through opened Celestial breach-site realmGates. Main entrances lead to Vault of Penitent Light. Return travel must use an unlocked entrance gate.
The Vaulted City.dustSecrecyRule: Describe the dust as fine obsidian-black dust only. It may cling, smear, gather in cracks, or dull light, but its origin must not be stated.

Frostfire Tundra.name: Frostfire Tundra
Frostfire Tundra.basicInfo: Frostfire Tundra: A northern frozen Drakonan wasteland of permafrost plateaus, ice-crusted roosts, white mountain shoulders, and blizzard-prone outposts. Travel is harsh, visibility can vanish without warning, and only Drakonan patrols, dragon riders, and hardened mountain settlements endure here reliably.
Frostfire Tundra.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Frostfire Tundra.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Frostfire Tundra, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Northern Watch.name: Northern Watch
Northern Watch.basicInfo: Northern Watch: A subarctic Drakonan mountain region where tundra valleys, snow-dusted ridges, frozen streams, and wind-scoured patrol routes replace the greener highlands to the south. Sky-ship patrols and distant dragon roars carry across cold open air, and rival travelers are watched closely from fortified outposts.
Northern Watch.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Northern Watch.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Northern Watch, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Stormwind Passes.name: Stormwind Passes
Stormwind Passes.basicInfo: Stormwind Passes: North of the lush Drakona Highlands, the plateaus climb into the first hard snowline. Mountain plains, dragon-roost cliffs, and fortified patrol roads remain visible, but frost gathers in the passes, alpine streams run ice-cold, and winter winds begin to dominate travel.
Stormwind Passes.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Stormwind Passes.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Stormwind Passes, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Drakona Highlands.name: Drakona Highlands
Drakona Highlands.basicInfo: Northern region of Drakona at (0, 7). The capital region of Drakona. A large Valley surrounded by the mountain ranges of Drakona, at the center is the floating capital city of Caelum spire, to the north is the ashenforge village and to the south the copperpeak spire. Travelers encounter cold winds, distant dragon roars, sky-ship patrols, fortified mountain outposts. This is a dangerous travel zone.
Drakona Highlands.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Drakona Highlands.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Drakona Highlands, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Aerial Strongholds.name: Aerial Strongholds
Aerial Strongholds.basicInfo: Aerial Strongholds: A northern Drakonan aerial military region north of Caelum Spire, defined by cold winds, fortified mountain outposts, roosts, sky-ship patrols, and dragon-flight defenses. It is dangerous and strategically important, but it is not Drakona's southern border.
Aerial Strongholds.hiddenInfo: Relative Position: Northern/internal Drakonan aerial stronghold region north of Caelum Spire. Border Status: Not Drakona's southern border, not the southern land approach, and not Dragonfall Pass. Military/Security Role: Sky-defense, fortified roosts, patrol bases, and internal/northern aerial control. Narration Rule: Do not describe Aerial Strongholds as Drakona's southern frontier, southern gate, southern pass, or southern border. Foreign presence here may still be suspicious, but frame it as infiltration, espionage, airspace violation, or deep incursion rather than a southern border clash.
Aerial Strongholds.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Aerial Strongholds, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Wyrm Spine Foothills.name: Wyrm Spine Foothills
Wyrm Spine Foothills.basicInfo: Northern region of Drakona at (0, 5). Travelers encounter cold winds, distant dragon roars, sky-ship patrols, fortified mountain outposts. This is a dangerous travel zone.
Wyrm Spine Foothills.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Wyrm Spine Foothills.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Wyrm Spine Foothills, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Wyrm Spine Summit.name: Wyrm Spine Summit
Wyrm Spine Summit.basicInfo: Northern region of Drakona at (0, 4). Travelers encounter cold winds, distant dragon roars, sky-ship patrols, fortified mountain outposts. This is a dangerous travel zone.
Wyrm Spine Summit.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Wyrm Spine Summit.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Wyrm Spine Summit, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Dragonfall Pass.name: Dragonfall Pass
Dragonfall Pass.basicInfo: Dragonfall Pass: Drakona's southernmost land border and primary fortified southern pass. The region is a high-security mountain approach where Drakonan patrols, border fortresses, sky-ship watches, and dragon riders monitor movement from southern or neutral routes.
Dragonfall Pass.hiddenInfo: Relative Position: South/southwest of Caelum Spire and the primary southern land approach into Drakona. Border Status: Drakona's southernmost land border and correct southern frontier/security pass. Military/Security Role: Border fortress zone, southern pass, land-gate, watchtower line, and high-security approach. Narration Rule: If narration refers to Drakona's southern border, southern frontier, southern gate, or southern land approach, use Dragonfall Pass unless the user explicitly changes the map. Any non-Drakona military presence here should feel like a border incident, infiltration, scouting, raid, or invasion pressure.
Dragonfall Pass.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Dragonfall Pass, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Highland Approach.name: Highland Approach
Highland Approach.basicInfo: Northern region of Drakona at (0, 2). Travelers encounter cold winds, distant dragon roars, sky-ship patrols, fortified mountain outposts. This is a dangerous travel zone.
Highland Approach.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Highland Approach.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Highland Approach, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Skyward Peaks.name: Skyward Peaks
Skyward Peaks.basicInfo: Northern region of Drakona at (-1, 7). Travelers encounter cold winds, distant dragon roars, sky-ship patrols, fortified mountain outposts. This is a dangerous travel zone.
Skyward Peaks.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Skyward Peaks.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Skyward Peaks, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Dragon Roost Valleys.name: Dragon Roost Valleys
Dragon Roost Valleys.basicInfo: Northern region of Drakona at (1, 7). Travelers encounter cold winds, distant dragon roars, sky-ship patrols, fortified mountain outposts. This is a dangerous travel zone.
Dragon Roost Valleys.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Any non-Drakona presence (especially Republic) should be treated as potential invasion, sabotage, or deep-cover agents. Any presence of rival nations should feel tense or dangerous.
Dragon Roost Valleys.imagePrompt: A detailed fantasy map of Dragon Roost Valleys, showing terrain, landmarks, and atmospheric lighting in a high fantasy style.

Vaulted City Interior.hiddenInfo: Use routeGraph for travel between Vaulted City locations. The player normally enters through Vault of Penitent Light, follows Canted Promenade, and branches from there. Do not use areas.Main Area.paths for inter-location movement. Access from Balen requires one of the surface Celestial breach realmGates. The first destination is Vault of Penitent Light. The black dust should remain unexplained. NPCs should avoid naming it as Shayde-related, daemon-related, ash, or remains.
Vaulted City Interior.dustSecrecyRule: Use only sensory details for the dust: fine, obsidian-black, powder-like, gathered in cracks and thresholds. Do not explain what it is.

Aethelgard.name: Aethelgard
Aethelgard.basicInfo: Aethelgard is Balen’s neutral circular heart-city, built in rings around the Magi-knight Citadel in the Aetherian Marches. Crossroads Plaza serves as the main gateway and civic hub, while markets, inns, guild offices, archives, embassies.

High Court.description: High Court is the formal summit and judgment chamber area associated with Aethelgard's highest neutral.

Aethelgard City.description: Neutral city district of homes, trade halls, guild offices, embassies, roads, and everyday civic life.

The Ringmarket.description: Circular market district of neutral merchants, supplies, crafts, food, and traveler trade.

The Wayfarer's Accord Inn.description: Huge inn connecting market and city, used by travelers, diplomats, Slayers, and adventurers.
The Wayfarer's Accord Inn.hiddenInfo: Connected Area Note: The Wayfarer's Weight is an attached expedition-supply storefront reached through the inn's common room, side hall, or rear courtyard.

Connected Party Room.description: Secure inn room attached to the Wayfarer’s Accord for party rest, planning, and private scenes.

Slayers Guild Headquarters.description: Central Slayer headquarters for contracts, monster records, registrations, and field coordination.

Crossroads Plaza.description: Primary gateway plaza at the center of travel routes, markets, contracts, and public movement.

Citadel Training Grounds.description: Martial and magical training yards for Magi-Knights and approved trainees.

Magi-knight Citadel.description: Central citadel of all Magi-Knight orders and site of the Four-Nation Summit.

Citadel Archives.description: Protected archive of Magi-Knight records, treaties, monster lore, and restricted histories.

Magi-Knight Citadel: Order Halls.description: The halls of the magi-knight citadel in which many of the officers within the magi-knight are housed

The Wayfarer's Weight.name: The Wayfarer's Weight
The Wayfarer's Weight.basicInfo: An expedition outfitter storefront in Aethelgard, connected directly to The Wayfarer's Accord Inn. Travelers usually reach it by passing through the inn's common room, side hall, or rear courtyard, making it a natural supply stop for adventurers, Slayers, caravan guards, scouts, pilgrims, and mercenaries preparing to leave the city.
The Wayfarer's Weight.hiddenInfo: This is an Aethelgard city area, not a separate regional location and not the inn itself. Use it when characters ask for travel gear, expedition supplies, trail provisions, rope, packs, tents, lanterns, weather gear, harvest containers, mule tack, field tools, or practical road equipment. The route between The Wayfarer's Accord Inn and The Wayfarer's Weight is immediate and internal: common room to side hall, side hall to outfitter wing, or rear courtyard to shop entrance.
The Wayfarer's Weight.description: An expedition outfitter storefront in Aethelgard, connected directly to The Wayfarer's Accord Inn. Travelers usually reach it by passing through the inn's common room, side hall, or rear courtyard, making it a natural supply stop for adventurers, Slayers, caravan guards, scouts, pilgrims, and mercenaries preparing to leave the city.

Crossroads Stable Yard.name: Crossroads Stable Yard
Crossroads Stable Yard.description: A busy Aethelgard stable and mount yard near the city exits, serving travelers, caravans, Slayers, merchants, scouts, and adventurers. Horses, mules, pack animals, cart teams, wagons, and trained tameable mounts are boarded, inspected, bought, sold, rented, and fitted here.
Crossroads Stable Yard.basicInfo: A stable, wagon, and mount yard near Crossroads Plaza and the city exits. Standard stalls and wagon teams are handled by Runa Tackwell, while the reinforced wyvern runs and false-wyrm training pens are handled by Kestrel Vanehook.
Crossroads Stable Yard.hiddenInfo: This is an Aethelgard city area, not a separate regional destination. The ordinary stable stalls, wagon yard, tack racks, feed storage, and cart rentals are overseen by Runa Tackwell. The reinforced wyvern runs, glide-saddle racks, scenting posts, and false-wyrm handling pens are overseen by Kestrel Vanehook. Use this area for ordinary mounts, carts, wagons, boarding, mount purchases, rented animals, Desert Trotter Wyverns, tameable false-wyrm handling, and Tamer-system scenes.

Aethelgard.hiddenInfo:
Neutral circular capital around the Magi-knight Citadel. Crossroads Plaza is the main hub. Keep Aethelgard politically neutral, organized by rings, and centered on Magi-Knight authority.

NPC LEVEL GUIDANCE:
Aethelgard uses parent range 1-20 so different internal areas can generate appropriate NPCs. Choose NPC levels by internal area. Do not expose this guidance to players.

Aethelgard City: civilians, merchants, travelers, minor criminals, ordinary civic guards, and starter hazards level 1-5.
The Wayfarer's Accord Inn: patrons, staff, travelers, rumors, minor trouble, and casual guards level 1-4.
The Ringmarket: merchants, shoppers, guards, pickpockets, apprentices, and market hazards level 1-5.
Citadel Archives: archive guards, restricted scholars, ward hazards, and Magi-Knight record keepers level 6-12.
Citadel Training Grounds: trainees, instructors, sparring opponents, and training hazards level 5-10.
Magi-knight Citadel: sworn Magi-Knights, citadel guards, captains, wards, and serious resistance level 8-15.
Magi-Knight Order Halls: senior knights, order specialists, commanders, elite wards, and crisis defenders level 10-20.
Grandmaster, High Exarch, Knight Commanders, named masters, and boss-grade citadel defenders use curated levels, usually 15-20.

Never spawn level 1 Magi-Knights as standard citadel defenders.

Caelum Spire.name: Caelum Spire
Caelum Spire.basicInfo: Caelum Spire is Drakona’s massive floating capital, a three-level island-city spiraling around a central citadel tower and built for dragons and humanoids to live together.

Lower Thoroughfare.description: Dragon-wide lower street around the spire with raised humanoid walkways, common access, and ramps upward.

Common Residences.description: Lower-city homes, shrines, kitchens, workshops, balconies, and family terraces for ordinary citizens.

Lower Roost Walks.description: Lower roost terraces for smaller dragons, couriers, service riders, and drakken families.

Cloud-Piercer Plaza.description: Huge middle-tier arrival plaza for airships, dragons, flyers, and ground gondolas.

Sky Market District.description: Dragon-scaled market of dracosilk, spices, dragon-glass, roost gear, skyship goods, and aerial tack.

Sky-Ship Docks.description: Reinforced airship docks with mooring towers, customs decks, cranes, bells, and weather offices.

Gondola Tether Station.description: Public gondolas linking Caelum to the ground for those who cannot fly or arrive by airship.

Wyrmforge Atelier.description: Prestigious craft quarter for dragon-glass, scales, dracosilk fittings, armor, saddles.

Central Spire Entrance.description: Formal entrance to the central citadel, with ground gates and aerial openings for dragon access.
paths: Great Hall of the Draconic Council

Upper Thoroughfare.description: Dragon-wide upper road through noble terraces, roosts, gardens, and the Queensguard bridge.

Noble Aeries.description: High estates with private roosts, humanoid villas, balconies, open halls, and dragon terraces.

Floating Gardens of Veyra.description: Upper luxury gardens of silver leaves, cloud lilies, pools, resting ledges, and quiet diplomacy.

Dragon Roost Platforms.description: Civic and noble dragon platforms for landing, resting, receiving guests, and formal flights.

Queensguard Barracks.description: Separate anchored island for Queensguard Dragoons and bonded dragons.

Great Hall of the Draconic Council.description: Colossal council hall with humanoid galleries, dragon platforms.

Sky Throne.description: Queen Veyra’s sky-open throne chamber with landing balconies and space for dragon envoys.
paths: Great Hall of the Draconic Council

Caelum Archives.description: Archives of dragon law, genealogies, treaties, bond records, skyship logs, and sealed conflicts.
paths: Great Hall of the Draconic Council

Drakken Armada Command.description: Military command for skyship routes, aerial patrols, rider deployments, weather, and Armada orders.

Caelum Academy of Dracomancy.description: Academy for breath-casting, aerial control, dragon law, bond ethics, and Dracomancy.

Caelum Spire.hiddenInfo:
Massive dragon-humanoid cohabitation capital. Three city levels spiral around one central spire. Roads and halls are dragon-scale. Dragon Archons and Matriarchs can enter the council hall by flight.

NPC LEVEL GUIDANCE:
Caelum Spire uses parent range 1-20 so different internal areas can generate appropriate NPCs. Choose NPC levels by internal area. Do not expose this guidance to players.

Common terraces, service walks, civic streets, servants, artisans, dockhands, market crowds, and minor trouble level 1-5.
Sky Market District: merchants, travelers, dock guards, pickpockets, and public hazards level 1-5.
Sky-Ship Docks and Gondola Tether Station: crew, patrols, dock security, smugglers, and sky-lane hazards level 2-7.

Sky Throne, Great Hall of the Draconic Council, Drakken Armada Command, noble aeries, and dragon roost command areas level 12-20.
Queen Veyra, Archons, Matriarchs, true dragon leaders, and named sovereign defenders use curated levels, usually 15-20.

Never spawn level 1 Queensguard, Dragoons, Armada officers, or true dragon leaders.

Stormpeak Citadel.name: Stormpeak Citadel
Stormpeak Citadel.basicInfo: A massive mountain fortress and sky-ship dock in the Empire of Drakona, seat of aerial military power.

Sky-Dock.description: Floating platforms for Drakken Armada vessels.

Command Spire.description: The war room of the Queensguard Dragoons.

Dragon Aerie.description: Huge caverns housing the empire's most prized dragons.

Emberdrift.name: Emberdrift
Emberdrift.basicInfo: A smaller floating sky-town in Drakona known for its dragon-glass forges and free-spirited merchants.
Emberdrift.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Describe this specific location.

Wyrmrest Roost.name: Wyrmrest Roost
Wyrmrest Roost.basicInfo: A sacred natural valley in Drakona where wild dragons nest and young Drakken train.

Nesting Crags.description: Steep cliffs dotted with dragon nests.

Training Grounds.description: Open fields used for aerial combat drills.

Wyrmrest Roost.hiddenInfo: AI Instructions: Describe this specific location.

Ashenforge.name: Ashenforge
Ashenforge.basicInfo: A rugged mining settlement in Drakona that extracts raw dragon-glass from volcanic slopes.

Mine Entrance.description: Heavily guarded shaft into the volcano.

Forge Barracks.description: Living quarters for miners and guards.

Ironhaven.name: Ironhaven
Ironhaven.basicInfo: Ironhaven is the Republic of Valen’s early industrial capital, a red-brick and black-iron city of rail lines, foundries, Senate halls, blimp moorings, blackpowder workshops, and marching Musketeers.

Ironmile Avenue.description: Soot-dark main boulevard linking rail gate, market, factories, Senate, inn, and Musketeer district.

Ironhaven Rail Gate.description: Fortified rail-and-road entrance with customs, scales, turnstiles, steam vents, and notices.

Steam Market.description: Red-brick market of food, tools, patents, clothing, ammunition, papers, kitchens, and wagon vendors.

Brass Lantern Inn.description: Worker-and-traveler inn for factory crews, Musketeers, merchants, Slayers, and clerks.

Player Room.description: Compact inn room with iron bed, coal stove, lockbox, thick windows, and brass lamp.

Slayer Guild Branch.description: Paperwork-heavy Slayer branch with ledgers, permits, hazard ranks, inspections, and bounty records.

Gear-Works District.description: Machine shops, gear towers, pipe bridges, foundry offices, small factories, bells, belts, and pulleys.

Blackpowder Foundries.description: Guarded foundries for cannon barrels, rifle parts, rails, engines, and pressure-fitted pieces.

Inventor's Quarter.description: Prototype shops, patent salons, brass labs, engine sheds, test yards, and invention disputes.

Patent Office.description: Ledgers, sealed drawings, working models, clerks, disputes, and locked cabinets of dangerous ideas.

Chief Engineer's Workshop.description: Master Tinker Hix’s watched workshop of rifle parts, gauges, schematics, mechanisms, and assistants.

Industrial Docks and Rail Yards.description: Rail spurs, cranes, canals, dispatch sheds, coal hoppers, wagon lots, and military freight.

Republic Senate Hall.description: Fortified Senate chamber for laws, budgets, labor, production, trade, military policy, and petitions.

Minister's Office.description: First Minister’s austere office with clerks, courier boxes, files, and factory skyline views.

Civic Ledger Court.description: Public record court for budgets, votes, charters, spending, labor disputes, and civic complaints.

Clockwork Tower.description: Civic bell and clock tower regulating shifts, Senate sessions, rail schedules, curfews, and alarms.

Musketeer Bastion.description: Military district for rifle, bayonet, cannon, sapper, field, and urban-defense drills.

Republic Armory.description: Capital armory of rifles, shells, uniforms, powder crates, bayonets, tools, and siege parts.

Blimp Mooring Yard.description: Observation blimps, signal balloons, tether crews, gas inspectors, weather clerks, and dispatch officers.

Ironhaven.hiddenInfo:
Early industrial Republic capital. Keep technology to rail, wagons, blimps, steam hoists, foundries, rifles, presses, signal bells, ledgers, and messenger runners. Ironmile Avenue is the main connector.

NPC LEVEL GUIDANCE:
Ironhaven uses parent range 1-16 so different internal areas can generate appropriate NPCs. Choose NPC levels by internal area. Do not expose this guidance to players.

Steam Market, worker streets, public inns, civic roads, laborers, merchants, minor criminals, and starter industrial hazards level 1-5.
Industrial Docks, Rail Yards, Gear-Works District, factory streets, and ordinary security level 2-7.
Blackpowder Foundries, Inventor's Quarter, Patent Office, and technical hazards level 3-8.

Never spawn level 1 Musketeers, officers, armory guards, or high-security Republic soldiers.

Blackiron Foundry.name: Blackiron Foundry
Blackiron Foundry.basicInfo: Blackiron Foundry is a major Republic city, a smaller Ironhaven-style hub of civic offices, early industry, markets, ledgers, Musketeer order.

Blackiron Avenue.description: Main civic-industrial road for wagons, workers, merchants, Musketeer patrols.

Foundry Worker District.description: Dense local district of homes, workshops, small offices, yards.

Forge Market.description: Local market of food, tools, parts, ledgers, newspapers, blackpowder supplies.

Slayer Ledger Desk.description: Paperwork-heavy Slayer desk for contracts, permits, hazard ranks.

Senator's Foundry Office.description: Local Senate office and government seat for the city’s senator, clerks.

Foundry Guard Post.description: Musketeer or guard post with drill yard, armory store, watch office.

Coalglass Inn.description: Practical Republic inn for workers, clerks, merchants, Slayers.

Blackiron Foundry.hiddenInfo: Republic major city. Keep technology early industrial: rail, wagons, blimps where appropriate, steam hoists.

Cogport.name: Cogport
Cogport.basicInfo: Valen's primary industrial harbor and trade hub where ore arrives and finished weapons depart.

Warehouse Row.description: Huge storage for black powder and iron.

Steamvein Mines.basicInfo: A vast underground mining complex in Valen that supplies the raw materials for the Republic's war machines.

Goldenhaven.name: Goldenhaven
Goldenhaven.basicInfo: The prosperous breadbasket city of the Holy Crown, famous for its golden wheat fields and knightly protection.

Harvest Square.description: Central market overflowing with grain and livestock.

Abbey Gate.description: Fortified entrance guarded by Crown Knights.

Wheatwall.name: Wheatwall
Wheatwall.basicInfo: A heavily fortified agrarian town on the southern plains, serving as the first line of defense for the kingdom.

The Painted Barrel.description: A modest Wheatwall inn with a low common room.

Goldenharvest Abbey.basicInfo: A holy monastery dedicated to the agrarian faith of the Holy Crown, where priests bless the land and train healers.

Sanctuary Garden.description: Lush prayer gardens that yield miraculous crops.

Scriptorium.description: Library of ancient agricultural and knightly texts.

Mill House.description: Water-powered mills that feed the kingdom.
