Canopy's Whisper.name: Canopy's Whisper
Canopy's Whisper.basicInfo: Canopy’s Whisper is Sylvera’s sacred stone-and-silver forest capital, built around the Whispering Heartwood without harming living trees. The surrounding Emerald Deluge makes the capital humid and rain-washed, with raised paths, root-safe foundations, and canopy drainage designed to live with monsoon weather rather than fight it.

Rootgate Hollow.description: Main entrance among sacred roots, with mountain-stone paths, silver-bronze arches.

Lower Rootways.description: Ground-level stone paths, water channels, lower homes, shrines, storage.

Wildcall Paths.description: Wide root-safe routes for worcens, beast-bonded families, companions, wild clans, and animal envoys.

World-Root Shrine.description: Sacred shrine between exposed roots, with fitted stone floors, silver bowls, oath-stones.

Moonwell Sanctuary.description: Healing sanctuary around a moonlit spring, with river-stone basins, lamps, screens.

The Spiral Bough.description: Main stone-and-bronze spiral walkway around the Heartwood, rising from roots to market and royal canopy.

Canopy Market.description: Suspended market of stone decks, metal frames, cloth awnings, rope bridges, herbs, bows, charms.

The Leafwake Inn.description: Traveler inn on a freestanding terrace near the market, screened by cloth, leaves, and wind chimes.

Player Room.description: Quiet Leafwake room with hammock beds, fallen-wood chest, leaf screens, and forest-mist balcony.

Slayer Guild Branch.description: Sylveran Slayer branch focused on ecological review, threat confirmation, corruption signs, and balance.

Speaker's Bough.description: Civic administration terrace for law, disputes, records, bridge care, notices, and city governance.

Heartwood Hall.description: Stone-and-silver council hall beside the Whispering Heartwood, not inside it, for Queen and Verdant Six.

Verdant Court.description: Open-air royal court near the upper branches for audiences, envoys, ceremonies, and petitions.

Royal Canopy.description: Protected upper district of stone terraces, silver platforms, bridges, royal residence.

Floating Gardens of Elowyn.description: Upper gardens of suspended platforms, stone planters, flowering terraces, and quiet diplomacy.

Sylveran Guard Perches.description: Visible ranger posts, guard platforms, and scout perches watching the upper city and approaches.

Hidden Bow Platforms.description: Concealed defensive platforms hidden among leaves, stone outcrops, and freestanding frames.

Canopy's Whisper.hiddenInfo:
Sacred forest capital built around ancestral trees, not from them. Do not make living trees into architecture.

NPC LEVEL GUIDANCE:
Canopy's Whisper uses parent range 1-20 so different internal areas can generate appropriate NPCs. Choose NPC levels by internal area. Do not expose this guidance to players.

Queen Elowyn, Verdant Six, High Warden Thalara, named champions, ancient sacred guardians, and boss-grade defenders use curated levels, usually 12-20.

Never spawn level 1 Sylveran Guard, royal defenders, senior wardens, or sacred Heartwood protectors.

Stoneleaf Walk.description: Main stone-and-bronze walk built around sacred trees and natural features.

Rootsafe District.description: Root-safe homes, terraces, work spaces, shrines, and paths built with stone.

Grove Market.description: Quiet market of herbs, food, crafts, bows, charms, medicine, beast tack.

Slayer Grove-Post.description: Sylveran Slayer post focused on ecological review, threat confirmation.

Rootwarden's Stone Court.description: Council seat and local government terrace for the regional Verdant Six.

Sylveran Warden Perches.description: Sylveran Guard and warden posts watching approaches, paths, groves.

Rootrest Lodge.description: Local lodge for envoys, Slayers, scouts, merchants.

Rootfather Grove.hiddenInfo: Sylvera major city. Build around nature, not from living trees.

Floor 10.loreDrop: A black-glass shard etched with a sunburst failing to reach the bottom of a spiral.
Floor 10.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 1-10 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

Floor 20.loreDrop: A rusted oath-chain marked: 'The light reached the walls, not the root.'
Floor 20.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 11-20 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

Floor 30.loreDrop: A sealed page describing a crown of light breaking against a sea beneath stone.
Floor 30.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 21-30 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

Floor 40.loreDrop: A throne-slate showing four nations above a black gate, with the gate unbroken.
Floor 40.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 31-40 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

Floor 50.loreDrop: A ritual bone tablet warning that blood opens doors where light cannot follow.
Floor 50.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 41-50 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

Floor 60.loreDrop: A cracked crown-symbol stained black at the lower rim: 'When the Crown dims, the buried door wakes.'
Floor 60.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 51-60 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

Floor 80.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 71-80 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

Floor 90.loreDrop: A living shadow-script: 'We were not burned. We were buried.'
Floor 90.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 81-90 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

Floor 100.loreDrop: The Matriarch's memory-core revealing the Tower as a surviving gateway to the Shaydes' realm.
Floor 100.regenerationRule: While this boss lives, floors 91-100 regenerate creatures and hazards every 1-2 days. Killing the boss stops that stratum's regeneration.

towerRules.partyLimit: Maximum five remembered expedition members. Larger parties suffer disappearances and memory loss around extra members.
towerRules.regeneration: Creatures and hazards regenerate every 1-2 days until the boss of that ten-floor stratum is killed.
towerRules.bossFloors: Every 10th floor is a boss floor. Boss death stops regeneration for that stratum and guarantees a lore fragment.
towerRules.publicKnowledge: Only floors 1-10 are publicly documented. No expedition has officially cleared floor 10.
towerRules.creatureTruth: Tower creatures are increasingly guardian-manifestations, locks, jailers, or immune responses, not ordinary wildlife.
towerRules.scaleProgression: Floors expand with depth; floor 100 abruptly contracts into a mockery of a castle throne room.

Forgeheart City.name: Forgeheart City
Forgeheart City.basicInfo: Forgeheart City is a compact Republic manufacturing hub: more mass industrial forge-complex than ordinary city, built around foundries, factory yards, rail spurs, worker rows, and guarded road access between the Foundry Road and eastern Valen.

Forgeheart Main Road.description: The main industrial road through Forgeheart, lined with rail spurs, wagon lanes, soot-black signposts, factory gates, and Republic patrol lamps.

Forgeheart Worker Quarter.description: Dense worker housing, bunkhouses, canteens, washhouses, pay offices, and cramped brick lanes for the laborers who keep the forge-complex alive.

Foundry Market.description: A practical market of tool stalls, coal sellers, food wagons, repair benches, powder-safe storage booths, and merchants serving factory crews and caravan traffic.

Forgeheart Manufacturing Yard.description: The heart of the hub: furnace halls, stamping sheds, cannon boring frames, rail cranes, armored wagon bays, and guarded Republic production lines.

The Smokestack Inn.description: A soot-stained but reliable inn for shift bosses, caravan guards, inspectors, and travelers waiting for clearance through the industrial road network.

Forgeheart City.hiddenInfo: Republic manufacturing hub. Treat it as a working industrial forge-complex rather than a full civic capital or Senate seat. Emphasize smoky factories, rail tracks, black powder smell, guarded production yards, armed patrols, shift bells, wagon traffic, and military manufacturing. Rival-nation presence should create immediate tension.

A northern Drakona dragon-clan spire rising from the Frostfire Tundra, built for dragons and drakken capable of surviving glacial wind, ice-sheathed stone, and brutal altitude.

Unlike the flying cities of Drakona. Frostfire Tundra is carved into the highest mountain of the tundra's its homes and paths carved into spiraling platforms down the mountain

Crownheart.name: Crownheart
Crownheart.basicInfo: Crownheart is the Holy Kingdom’s sacred castle-city, arranged along the Crownway Fairway from the outer gates to the citadel. Markets, inns, chapels, homes, and the Slayer branch line the road before the inner walls.

Crownway Fairway.description: Broad cobblestone ceremonial road from outer gates to citadel; main parade, market, pilgrimage.

Crownheart Market.description: Holy-capital market of food, armor fittings, icons, pilgrim tokens, supplies, and Crown-supervised trade.

Slayer Guild Branch.description: Regulated Slayer office for local contracts, reports, harvest records, and Crown oversight.

Pilgrim's Rest Inn.description: Large Crownway inn for pilgrims, knights, Slayers, merchants, and petitioners.

Player Room.description: Clean rented room in Pilgrim’s Rest Inn with beds, chest, washstand, and Crownway view.

Castle Courtyard.description: Inner military yard where Glaives and Crown Knights drill before the citadel.

Glaive Barracks.description: Severe barracks of the elite Glaives closest to the citadel and Throne-Sanctum.

Crown Knight Barracks.description: Broader knight barracks for Crown Knights, House Dawnmere sworn knights, patrols, and escorts.

Crownheart Citadel.description: Central white-stone castle holding the Throne-Sanctum, archives, council chamber.

Throne-Sanctum.description: Throne room and holy chapel. The Crystal Crown is embedded above the Emperor’s throne and struck by dawn.

Citadel Archives.description: Guarded royal and sacred archives of decrees, oaths, house records, and restricted Crown documents.

Crown Council Chamber.description: Formal chamber for the Emperor, High Priestess, Glaive Commander, House Lords, and House Knights.

Crownheart.hiddenInfo:
Sacred castle-city on the Crownway Fairway. Crystal Crown is embedded above the throne in the Throne-Sanctum and never worn. Glaives guard the Emperor and citadel; Crown Knights serve broader military and house roles.

NPC LEVEL GUIDANCE:
Crownheart uses parent range 1-20 so different internal areas can generate appropriate NPCs. Choose NPC levels by internal area. Do not expose this guidance to players.

Pilgrim roads, public plazas, markets, inns, temple commons, servants, pilgrims, commoners, and minor trouble level 1-5.
Crown Knight posts, patrol offices, and normal watch positions level 4-8.
Temple administrative areas, relic workshops, and priestly restricted halls level 4-10.
Glaive Barracks and elite enforcement areas level 8-14.
Throne-Sanctum, Crystal Crown security, royal court defenses, sacred sealed chambers, and high temple wards level 12-20.
Emperor, High Priestess, Glaive Commander, House Knights, named champions, and boss-grade sacred defenders use curated levels, usually 12-20.

Never spawn level 1 Crown Knights, Glaives, palace guards, or Crystal Crown defenders.

Thornwatch Hold.basicInfo: A small defensive hold woven into rainwood, thorns, and stone, guarding paths through the wet Sylveran frontier.

Thornwall Walk.description: Main stone-and-bronze walk built around sacred trees and natural features.

Briar District.description: Root-safe homes, terraces, work spaces, shrines, and paths built with stone.

Thorn Market.description: Quiet market of herbs, food, crafts, bows, charms, medicine, beast tack.

Slayer Grove-Post.description: Sylveran Slayer post focused on ecological review, threat confirmation.

Thorn Marshal's Hall.description: Council seat and local government terrace for the regional Verdant Six.

Sylveran Warden Perches.description: Sylveran Guard and warden posts watching approaches, paths, groves.

Briar Lantern Lodge.description: Local lodge for envoys, Slayers, scouts, merchants.

Thornwatch Hold.hiddenInfo: Sylvera major city. Build around nature, not from living trees.

Powdergate Arsenal.name: Powdergate Arsenal
Powdergate Arsenal.basicInfo: Powdergate Arsenal is a major Republic city, a smaller Ironhaven-style hub of civic offices, early industry, markets, ledgers, Musketeer order.

Powdergate Road.description: Main civic-industrial road for wagons, workers, merchants, Musketeer patrols.

Arsenal Worker District.description: Dense local district of homes, workshops, small offices, yards.

Powdergate Market.description: Local market of food, tools, parts, ledgers, newspapers, blackpowder supplies.

Slayer Permit Desk.description: Paperwork-heavy Slayer desk for contracts, permits, hazard ranks.

Defense Senate Office.description: Local Senate office and government seat for the city’s senator, clerks.

Arsenal Yard.description: Musketeer or guard post with drill yard, armory store, watch office.

Black Kettle Inn.description: Practical Republic inn for workers, clerks, merchants, Slayers.

Powdergate Arsenal.hiddenInfo: Republic major city. Keep technology early industrial: rail, wagons, blimps where appropriate, steam hoists.

Gearford Exchange.basicInfo: Gearford Exchange is a major Republic city, a smaller Ironhaven-style hub of civic offices, early industry, markets, ledgers, Musketeer order.

Gearford Avenue.description: Main civic-industrial road for wagons, workers, merchants, Musketeer patrols.

Guildhouse District.description: Dense local district of homes, workshops, small offices, yards.

Exchange Market.description: Local market of food, tools, parts, ledgers, newspapers, blackpowder supplies.

Saintless Abbey Breach.name: Saintless Abbey Breach
Saintless Abbey Breach.basicInfo: A ruined mortal abbey-castle whose lower crypt has fused with the exposed crest of a Celestial noble estate from the Vaulted City.

Ruined Abbey Court.description: Surface ruin of broken arches, cracked bells, and mortal stone fused to white-gold Celestial masonry.

Sealed Slayer Camp.description: A dangerous staging camp used by elite Slayers studying the ruin and its hostile wards.

Collision Stair.description: A slanted stair where castle dungeon stone gives way to canted Celestial estate halls.

Canted Noble Hall.description: Grand noble hall tilted from the dimensional collision, scarred by claws, blades, and magic scorch.

Escape Seal.description: Mournful Celestial seal left where survivors fled upward and barred the breach behind them.

Saintless Abbey Breach.hiddenInfo: Midgame Celestial ruin. This was once part of a noble tower-home in the Vaulted City. The surface abbey is mortal damage from the collision. Enemies remain outside/deeper around the seal, but the architecture hints at a larger buried city. This site can serve as a realm entrance to the Vault of Penitent Light once its Celestial seal is opened.

realmGate.description: A sealed Celestial noble-estate breach beneath a ruined abbey-castle. When properly opened, it leads into the Vault of Penitent Light.
requirements: The local Celestial seal must be opened or bypassed.
The route should be treated as dangerous and midgame-or-higher.
Do not allow casual travel until the seal has been solved, broken, or story-unlocked.

Ashen Skycourt Forge.name: Ashen Skycourt Forge
Ashen Skycourt Forge.basicInfo: A shattered skycourt ruin where a Celestial light-forge was driven into mortal stone during the realm collision.

Broken Skycourt.description: Surface court of cracked white stone, fallen columns, and fused mortal masonry.

Slayer Survey Post.description: Heavily guarded survey post tracking constructs, dark mana surges, and relic hazards.

Forge Descent.description: Collapsed stair and hoist shaft descending into radiant machinery half-buried at an angle.

Lumen Forge Floor.description: Divine forge chamber of broken lenses, dead light channels, ward constructs, and scorch-black stone.

Containment Seal.description: A sealed forge gate whose light-runes flicker under pressure from dark mana below.

Ashen Skycourt Forge.hiddenInfo: Mid-to-high Celestial ruin. Shows divine engineering and containment technology. The forge conduits point toward the Vaulted City network. Dangerous constructs and unstable machinery should be common. This site can serve as a realm entrance to the Vault of Penitent Light once its Celestial seal is opened.

realmGate.description: A flickering forge seal at the end of a shattered light-forge. When stabilized, it leads into the Vault of Penitent Light.
requirements: The local Celestial seal must be opened or bypassed.
The route should be treated as dangerous and midgame-or-higher.
Do not allow casual travel until the seal has been solved, broken, or story-unlocked.

Reliquary Archive Breach.name: Reliquary Archive Breach
Reliquary Archive Breach.basicInfo: A reliquary cellar beneath old ruins that opens into a broken Celestial archive wing from the buried city-network.

Mortal Reliquary Cellars.description: Old mortal cellars and chapel vaults cracked open by pale Celestial stone beneath them.

Slayer Chapel Watch.description: Crown-licensed watch post for elite Slayers, priests, and restricted ruin records.

Archive Stair.description: Stairwell descending through fused stone into a silent archive built on a wrong angle.

Crystal Record Hall.description: Rows of shattered crystal records, silent memory basins, hostile echoes, and erased names.

Song-Locked Seal.description: A sealed record gate that responds to balance, shadow, guilt, and remembered names.

Reliquary Archive Breach.hiddenInfo: High Celestial ruin. Publicly it supports the idea that Celestials aided mortals; deeper fragments imply records were redacted and the larger Vaulted City network exists below. This site can serve as a realm entrance to the Vault of Penitent Light once its Celestial seal is opened.

realmGate.description: A sealed archive gate beneath holy reliquary cellars. When answered by the correct doctrine, it leads into the Vault of Penitent Light.
requirements: The local Celestial seal must be opened or bypassed.
The route should be treated as dangerous and midgame-or-higher.
Do not allow casual travel until the seal has been solved, broken, or story-unlocked.

Broken Halo Bastion.name: Broken Halo Bastion
Broken Halo Bastion.basicInfo: A fortress ruin where the upper crown of a Celestial defensive tower punched through a mortal stronghold.

Fallen Bastion Crown.description: Surface fortress crown of white-gold battlements fused into ruined mortal stone.

Slayer Roost Camp.description: Aerial-access camp for high-level Slayers and Drakona scouts watching the unstable ruin.

Ward Descent.description: Narrow descent past inward-facing ward pylons and battle-scarred defensive rings.

Inward Shield Hall.description: Celestial bastion chamber whose defenses face down toward the city rather than outward.

Bastion Escape Seal.description: Old seal over a troop passage leading toward deeper undercity corridors.

Broken Halo Bastion.hiddenInfo: High Celestial ruin. The key clue is that many defenses face inward, implying the threat was inside or below. Use dangerous ward constructs, corrupted sentinels, and aerial access hazards. This site can serve as a realm entrance to the Vault of Penitent Light once its Celestial seal is opened.

realmGate.description: An old troop-passage seal in a Celestial defensive tower. When breached, it leads into the Vault of Penitent Light.
requirements: The local Celestial seal must be opened or bypassed.
The route should be treated as dangerous and midgame-or-higher.
Do not allow casual travel until the seal has been solved, broken, or story-unlocked.

Glasswound Boundary Breach.name: Glasswound Boundary Breach
Glasswound Boundary Breach.basicInfo: A strange breach-site at the edge of wet Sylveran overgrowth, where glasslike scars cut through vines and flooded roots.

Glasswound Surface.description: Cracked surface ruin of glassy stone, broken trees, and pale structures that should not fit the ground.

Warden-Slayer Camp.description: Sylveran and Slayer camp monitoring warped gravity, dark mana pressure, and broken seals.

Folded Passage.description: Passage where floors, walls, and stairs meet at impossible angles from the pocket-realm collapse.

Tilted Observatory.description: Celestial observatory chamber canted sideways, its sky-lenses now pointed into stone and darkness.

Boundary Seal.description: Seal over a fractured transit line toward the collapsed Vaulted City realm.

Glasswound Boundary Breach.hiddenInfo: Very high Celestial ruin. This is one of the clearest hints that the Vaulted City came from a collapsed pocket dimension. Reality is warped; enemies should be severe. This site can serve as a realm entrance to the Vault of Penitent Light once its Celestial seal is opened.

realmGate.description: A fractured boundary seal at a warped collision site. When aligned, it leads into the Vault of Penitent Light.
requirements: The local Celestial seal must be opened or bypassed.
The route should be treated as dangerous and midgame-or-higher.
Do not allow casual travel until the seal has been solved, broken, or story-unlocked.

Vault of Penitent Light.name: Vault of Penitent Light
Vault of Penitent Light.basicInfo: The final sealed gate into the dead city. Its riddles test balance, the shadow cast by light, and atonement/guilt.

Main Area.description: The final sealed gate into the dead city. Its riddles test balance, the shadow cast by light, and atonement/guilt.

Vault of Penitent Light.hiddenInfo: The door should imply a grand treasure room, then open to tomb-cold darkness that stifles breath, cools blood, and suffocates light. The seal is post-fall, not part of the original city. This gate is the hub connecting opened surface Celestial breach sites to the Vaulted City realm. Do not identify the obsidian-black dust or have casual NPCs name it as Shayde, daemon, shadow, ash, or remains.
Vault of Penitent Light.navigationHint: Use realmRoutes for movement to other Vaulted City locations. areas.Main Area.paths is intentionally empty because Voyage validates paths only within the same location.

rules: The player may return only through an entrance seal that has already been opened or story-unlocked.
Leaving the Vaulted City should return the party to the matching sourceArea of the chosen surface ruin.
Do not treat the Vaulted City as connected to normal Balen roads except through these realmGate entrances.

Canted Promenade.name: Canted Promenade
Canted Promenade.basicInfo: A grand white-gold avenue tilted from the pocket-realm collapse, lined with ravaged facades, claw-grooves, scorch marks, and black dust.

Main Area.description: A grand white-gold avenue tilted from the pocket-realm collapse, lined with ravaged facades, claw-grooves, scorch marks, and black dust.

Canted Promenade.hiddenInfo: Make the incline feel wrong: the city was built level but merged crooked. No bodies, bones, or decay. Do not identify the obsidian-black dust or have casual NPCs name it as Shayde, daemon, shadow, ash, or remains.
Canted Promenade.navigationHint: Use realmRoutes for movement to other Vaulted City locations. areas.Main Area.paths is intentionally empty because Voyage validates paths only within the same location.

Thousand-Lamp Plaza.name: Thousand-Lamp Plaza
Thousand-Lamp Plaza.basicInfo: A vast civic square where suspended crystal lamps still glow faintly, their light pressed thin by suffocating dark mana.

Main Area.description: A vast civic square where suspended crystal lamps still glow faintly, their light pressed thin by suffocating dark mana.

Thousand-Lamp Plaza.hiddenInfo: This should feel like a public heart of a once-living civilization, not a shrine or tomb. Do not identify the obsidian-black dust or have casual NPCs name it as Shayde, daemon, shadow, ash, or remains.
Thousand-Lamp Plaza.navigationHint: Use realmRoutes for movement to other Vaulted City locations. areas.Main Area.paths is intentionally empty because Voyage validates paths only within the same location.

Sunspire Noble District.basicInfo: Regal tower-homes and mansions of Celestial nobility, canted and cracked, with upper portions once breaching surface ruins.

Main Area.description: Regal tower-homes and mansions of Celestial nobility, canted and cracked, with upper portions once breaching surface ruins.

Sunspire Noble District.hiddenInfo: These noble homes explain why surface castles can hide stairwells into Celestial interiors. Do not identify the obsidian-black dust or have casual NPCs name it as Shayde, daemon, shadow, ash, or remains.

Main Area.description: Aerial docks and open balconies now facing cavern darkness, proving the city was never meant to be underground.

Lumen Crown Forum.name: Lumen Crown Forum
Lumen Crown Forum.basicInfo: A magnificent forum of law, art, and civic pride, ravaged by ancient battle but still breathtaking in scale.

Main Area.description: A magnificent forum of law, art, and civic pride, ravaged by ancient battle but still breathtaking in scale.

Lumen Crown Forum.hiddenInfo: Shows Celestials as a complete society: law, art, families, public life, pride, and achievement. Do not identify the obsidian-black dust or have casual NPCs name it as Shayde, daemon, shadow, ash, or remains.

Aureate Archive.name: Aureate Archive
Aureate Archive.basicInfo: Shattered archive halls of crystal records and silent memory basins, many darkened or cracked beyond reading.

Main Area.description: Shattered archive halls of crystal records and silent memory basins, many darkened or cracked beyond reading.

Aureate Archive.hiddenInfo: Give fragments, not full truth. Records suggest scale, fear, and contradiction without explaining everything. Do not identify the obsidian-black dust or have casual NPCs name it as Shayde, daemon, shadow, ash, or remains.
