Consecrates a wide area around the priest, cleansing blighted plants or land, curing disease, easing harmful afflictions, stabilizing allies, restoring vitality, sanctifying ground, and repelling or damaging profane corrupting forces. Do not use this ability to reveal forbidden creature lore by name unless the story has already earned that knowledge.

Triage.name: Triage
Triage.description: Rapidly scan nearby injuries, symptoms, breathing, bleeding, posture, shock, poison signs, broken bones, and battlefield risk to determine who needs treatment first. For a short duration, your medical judgment sharpens, letting you prioritize the most severe injuries before wasting time on lesser wounds.
Triage.usageInstructions: Healing and medicine assessment buff. For 5 rounds, improve healing-based skill checks including Stabilize, Field Treatment, potion use, bandaging, surgery, poison treatment, emergency diagnosis, and deciding which patient or wound must be treated first. May cost stamina if performed under combat pressure or while moving.

Stabilize.name: Stabilize
Stabilize.description: Use pressure, bandages, positioning, and practiced triage to keep a dying or badly wounded creature alive. This does not require mana, though it may demand stamina when performed under combat pressure, while moving, or while treating multiple patients quickly.
Stabilize.usageInstructions: Emergency survival technique. Use to stop immediate death, slow severe bleeding, reduce shock, maintain breathing, or keep a patient alive long enough for treatment, magic, alchemy, or evacuation. It is not instant regeneration.

Field Treatment.name: Field Treatment
Field Treatment.description: Treat bleeding, shock, pain, poison symptoms, infection risk, broken bones, and travel-threatening injuries with medicine, bandages, vials, and alchemical supplies. Slower than magic, but reliable when mana is unavailable or when magical wound closure is not enough.
Field Treatment.usageInstructions: Practical medical treatment. Use after or during danger to bind wounds, splint bones, administer remedies, manage shock, reduce infection risk, treat poison symptoms, prepare a patient for travel, or support natural recovery. Requires appropriate supplies when the scene calls for them.

Arcane Suturing.name: Arcane Suturing
Arcane Suturing.description: Spend mana to seal small cuts, slow bleeding, reinforce stitches, or hold torn flesh closed with minor arcane force. This helps keep wounds shut, but it does not regenerate tissue, repair broken bones, restore organs, or replace proper treatment.
Arcane Suturing.usageInstructions: Minor arcane stabilization. Use to help close or hold wounds when bandages and stitching alone are not enough. It supports medicine; it does not replace triage, surgery, bone-setting, recovery care, or Dracomantic regeneration.

Steady Hands.name: Steady Hands
Steady Hands.description: Spend stamina to steady your breath, still your hands, and work through pain, fear, motion, or battlefield chaos. Your body settles into practiced discipline so treatment can continue even when the world is shaking around you.
Steady Hands.usageInstructions: Stamina-based medical focus. For a short duration, improve emergency treatment, surgery, potion administration, bandaging, stitching, stabilizing someone under pressure, or working while injured, frightened, rushed, riding, moving, or in combat.

Monster Read.name: Monster Read
Monster Read.description: Study tracks, wounds, droppings, feeding signs, movement patterns, shed scales, claw marks, territory marks, or battle behavior to identify a creature’s habits, likely weaknesses, danger level, and tactical behavior. This is trained Slayer fieldcraft, not magic. Cost: none. Cooldown: 5 rounds.
Monster Read.usageInstructions: Use survival, investigation, bestiary knowledge, and observed evidence to reveal practical monster information. Do not reveal secret endgame lore, Daemon/Shayde truth, or facts the evidence cannot support.

Hunter's Mark.name: Hunter's Mark
Hunter's Mark.description: Focus on one creature you can see or have clearly tracked. For a short duration, you read its movement, pressure its openings, and exploit what you know about its behavior. Gain a bonus to tracking, striking, predicting movement, and exploiting openings against that target. Cost: none. Duration: 5 rounds. Cooldown: 10 rounds.
Hunter's Mark.usageInstructions: This is a trained combat focus technique, not a supernatural mark. It should not consume mana, stamina, or breathing.

Clean Harvest.name: Clean Harvest
Clean Harvest.description: Use proper Slayer Guild technique to harvest useful monster parts without ruining them. Improves the chance of recovering intact hides, glands, claws, venom sacs, scales, bones, organs, alchemical reagents, or crafting materials. Cost: none.
Clean Harvest.usageInstructions: Use after a legal kill, found carcass, or approved harvest. Respect conservation rules, protected species, Red-Guard audits, and dragon-sentience laws.

Inferno Lotus.name: Inferno Lotus
Inferno Lotus.description: Spin through a dance of whirling blades, guiding fire-mana into a blooming vortex around you. Flame unfurls from your weapon arcs like the petals of a burning lotus, striking nearby enemies and turning close quarters into a furnace.
Inferno Lotus.usageInstructions: Pyromantic signature technique. Best with Twin Sabers. Use for close-range area damage and crowd control; flame damage follows successful blade rhythm/contact rather than automatic hits.

Tidal Miststep.name: Tidal Miststep
Tidal Miststep.description: Flow directly toward an opponent in a smooth, unbroken dance, gathering water-mana as mist around your steps. At the last moment, you slip back just before contact and crash the gathered current into the target like a sudden tide.
Tidal Miststep.usageInstructions: Hydromantic signature technique. Best with Dueling Saber. Use as a feint, displacement, and single-target wave impact after hit/contact resolution.

Highland Jig.name: Highland Jig
Highland Jig.description: Break into a rapid, flowing whirl of slashes and footwork, each blade strike cutting first while wind-mana follows a heartbeat later as a second invisible blow. The technique overwhelms guards through speed, rhythm, and delayed pressure.
Highland Jig.usageInstructions: Aeromantic signature technique. Best with Dual Daggers. Use for rapid multi-hit pressure, delayed wind follow-up strikes, and evasive offense.

Thunderlord's Rite.name: Thunderlord's Rite
Thunderlord's Rite.description: Move through fluid steps broken by rapid, jagged thrusts, guiding lightning-mana through each piercing line. Every strike cracks with blinding flash and deafening thunder, overwhelming the target’s senses as much as their guard.
Thunderlord's Rite.usageInstructions: Fulmantic signature technique. Best with Estoc. Use for piercing burst pressure, sensory disruption, lightning flash-feints, and thunderous thrusts after hit/contact resolution.

Oathguard.name: Oathguard
Oathguard.description: Interpose yourself between an ally and incoming danger, using shield, armor, stance, and trained timing to protect them. This is formal knightly bodyguard discipline: stepping into the line of attack, catching a blow on shield or armor, and forcing danger to break against you first.
Oathguard.usageInstructions: Defensive protection technique. Use to improve blocks, parries, interception, bodyguard actions, and attempts to protect an ally from a direct attack. It is not magic and costs no resource; use cooldown and positioning instead.

Knight's Challenge.name: Knight's Challenge
Knight's Challenge.description: Issue a formal challenge through voice, stance, weapon, shield, and battlefield authority. A foe who understands the threat or respects strength is pressured to face you rather than strike weaker allies.
Knight's Challenge.usageInstructions: Battlefield control technique. Use persuasion, intimidation when appropriate, bladed arts, and social authority to draw one enemy's attention, protect allies, force a duel-like focus, or make a reckless foe answer the knight first. Mindless creatures may ignore the formal meaning but can still react to posture and threat.

Shield Brace.name: Shield Brace
Shield Brace.description: Set your shield and stance against a charge, heavy blow, breath pressure, arrow volley, or forced movement. Your body locks behind the shield with trained knightly discipline, making you harder to knock down or break through.
Shield Brace.usageInstructions: Defensive stance technique. Use to improve block checks, resist being shoved or knocked prone, reduce incoming physical pressure, hold a chokepoint, or protect against volleys and charges when equipped with a shield or similar defensive implement.

Veteran's Eye.name: Veteran's Eye
Veteran's Eye.description: Quickly assess a battlefield, employer, contract site, ambush point, guard rotation, or armed group for danger signs. You can identify weak positions, likely betrayals, poor formations, hidden leverage, and whether a fight is worth the pay.
Veteran's Eye.usageInstructions: Use for tactical assessment, employer judgment, ambush recognition, contract-site danger reads, and practical battlefield survival. In combat, use a 5-round cooldown. Outside combat, use when the scene gives enough evidence to read.

Dirty Opening.name: Dirty Opening
Dirty Opening.description: Exploit a distraction, bad footing, overextension, shield gap, wounded limb, or moment of hesitation with a pragmatic strike. This is not honorable dueling; it is professional survival.
Dirty Opening.usageInstructions: Use as an opportunistic attack when the target is distracted, off-balance, overextended, wounded, poorly positioned, or exposed by an ally. This is trained pragmatism, not magic or Sublime Arts.

Hold the Line.name: Hold the Line
Hold the Line.description: Brace yourself and maintain position under pressure, protecting a route, doorway, caravan wagon, employer, or ally. Your stance and practical battlefield discipline help you resist being shoved, surrounded, intimidated, or forced to give ground.
Hold the Line.usageInstructions: Defensive escort and line-holding technique. For 3 rounds, improve resistance to forced movement, intimidation, being surrounded, and losing a protected position. Useful for caravan defense, door holds, employer protection, and expedition security.

Ignis Brand.name: Ignis Brand
Ignis Brand.description: Part of Runic Branding. After making direct contact with a target through your weapon, hand, tattooed palm, etched focus, or prepared ritual implement, speak “Ignis” to brand a fire rune into them. The sigil flashes hot and erupts in a burst of flame from the point of contact.
Ignis Brand.usageInstructions: The brand only forms after a successful hit, touch, grapple, or other contact. On activation, deal fire damage or ignite a small burst at the branded point.

Aero Brand.name: Aero Brand
Aero Brand.description: Part of Runic Branding. After making direct contact with a target through your weapon, hand, tattooed palm, etched focus, or prepared ritual implement, speak “Aero” to brand a wind rune into them. The sigil releases a violent directional force, pushing the target away from the branded point.
Aero Brand.usageInstructions: The brand only forms after successful contact. Use for knockback, forced movement, staggering, spacing control, or interrupting a target’s footing.

Fulcra Brand.name: Fulcra Brand
Fulcra Brand.description: Part of Runic Branding. After making direct contact with a target through your weapon, hand, tattooed palm, etched focus, or prepared ritual implement, speak “Fulcra” to brand a lightning rune into them. The sigil calls a sharp lightning strike down onto or through the marked point.
Fulcra Brand.usageInstructions: The brand only forms after successful contact. Use for lightning damage, shock, brief sensory disruption, or interrupting a target’s action.

Icis Brand.name: Icis Brand
Icis Brand.description: Part of Runic Branding. After making direct contact with a target through your weapon, hand, tattooed palm, etched focus, or prepared ritual implement, speak “Icis” to brand a frost rune into them. The sigil rapidly freezes the branded area, slowing movement and potentially locking a limb or joint in place.
Icis Brand.usageInstructions: The brand only forms after successful contact. Use for frost damage, slowing movement, stiffening armor joints, numbing a limb, or briefly pinning a small area in ice.

Weapon Rune.name: Weapon Rune
Weapon Rune.description: Charge a prepared rune on your weapon, briefly empowering your next strike with arcane force or a simple prepared effect.
Weapon Rune.usageInstructions: Use when the Spellsword has a prepared weapon rune or can quickly trace one. The next successful weapon strike may add arcane force, a simple prepared effect, or a small rune-triggered advantage.

Guard Circle.name: Guard Circle
Guard Circle.description: Trace or trigger a small defensive circle on armor, scabbard, shield, ground, or air to reduce, deflect, or disrupt incoming harm.
Guard Circle.usageInstructions: Defensive Spellsword circle. Use to reduce, deflect, ward, or disrupt an incoming attack when the Spellsword has a surface, armor mark, scabbard mark, shield mark, ground line, or air-traced circle to complete the pattern.

Sigil Strike.name: Sigil Strike
Sigil Strike.description: Strike with your weapon while releasing a prepared sigil, adding arcane force, disruption, or a limited elemental effect based on the rune used.
Sigil Strike.usageInstructions: Runic weapon attack. Unlike Runic Branding, Sigil Strike releases a prepared sigil through the weapon attack rather than branding the target. Requires a successful hit before damage or disruption applies.

Forward Recon.name: Forward Recon
Forward Recon.description: Move ahead of the party to quietly inspect terrain, roads, doors, camps, ruins, patrol paths, or enemy positions. On success, reveal useful tactical information before the group commits.
Forward Recon.usageInstructions: Reconnaissance utility. Use stealth, survival, investigation, sightlines, terrain reading, and patience to learn what waits ahead before the party enters danger. Outside combat, cooldown may be ignored when time and conditions allow careful scouting.

Ambush Sense.name: Ambush Sense
Ambush Sense.description: Read terrain, silence, broken branches, disturbed dust, unnatural stillness, poor sightlines, or suspicious cover to detect likely ambushes or hidden watchers before they strike.
Ambush Sense.usageInstructions: Danger detection utility. Use to identify likely ambushes, hidden watchers, kill-zones, false quiet, concealed firing lines, or terrain that gives enemies an advantage. This is mundane fieldcraft, not divination.

Silent Step.name: Silent Step
Silent Step.description: Control your breathing, foot placement, clothing movement, and body profile to move quietly through difficult terrain or dangerous sightlines.
Silent Step.usageInstructions: Mundane stealth movement. For 3 rounds or a short tense scene, improve attempts to move quietly, cross noisy ground, hide body profile, avoid sightlines, or reposition without being noticed. Does not grant supernatural invisibility.

Marked Route.name: Marked Route
Marked Route.description: Mark a safe or efficient path for allies using subtle signs, scratches, cloth tags, stone placement, charcoal marks, or memory. Allies following your route gain help avoiding hazards, wrong turns, patrols, or exposed ground.
Marked Route.usageInstructions: Party navigation and route support. Use during travel, infiltration, dungeon movement, wilderness movement, or retreats to guide allies along a safer path. The marks should be subtle enough not to advertise the route to enemies unless the Scout is careless or rushed.

Ghost the Line.name: Ghost the Line
Ghost the Line.description: Slip through a watched route, patrol gap, doorway, treeline, alley, ruin hall, battlefield edge, or enemy perimeter by using cover and timing to break detection. For a short duration, you gain a major bonus to stealth, repositioning, scouting ahead, escaping pursuit, or crossing exposed ground where intermittent cover exists.
Ghost the Line.usageInstructions: Active stealth, infiltration, and escape. Requires cover, broken sightlines, distraction, crowds, terrain, clutter, smoke, darkness, brush, ruins, or another plausible way to break observation. It fails or becomes much harder in fully open ground under direct observation.

Advanced Survivalism.name: Advanced Survivalism
Advanced Survivalism.description: Use the surrounding environment, spare cloth, binding material, mud, herbs, bark, sap, pressure wraps, splints, or improvised fieldcraft to mend your injuries enough to keep moving. This is emergency survival care, not true regeneration.
Advanced Survivalism.usageInstructions: Heavy-cooldown self-sustain. Recover a small amount of health and reduce minor bleeding, pain, or travel-threatening injury effects when suitable materials are available. Cannot restore lost limbs, repair severe internal damage, fix shattered bones, cure disease, or replace proper medical treatment.

Cantrip.name: Cantrip
Cantrip.description: Use tiny, controlled magic for mundane tasks: conjure a simple temporary chair, levitate or nudge a small unattended object, clean dust or stains, warm tea, chill a cup, light a candle, make a small harmless sound, or perform similar prestidigitation. Cantrips are utility magic, not combat spells, and should not replace tools, solve major obstacles, or create lasting valuables.
Cantrip.usageInstructions: Use for minor mundane magic and flavor effects. Normally no tracked mana cost unless repeated, strained, enlarged, used under pressure, or pushed beyond harmless utility. Cantrip cannot deal meaningful damage, bypass serious locks, create permanent matter, or replace full spellcasting.

Magic Missile.name: Magic Missile
Magic Missile.description: Shape raw mana into precise darts of arcane force and launch them at visible targets. Magic Missile is simple, reliable, and favored by Arcanists because it rewards control more than raw power. Each missile costs 10 mana and deals 1 to 2 arcane damage. For every additional 10 mana spent, create one additional missile. The Arcanist may fire all missiles at one target or split them among multiple visible targets, as long as the spell-shape can be maintained. Large mana dumps are possible, but risk exhaustion, mana strain, or dangerous overdraw if pushed too far.
Magic Missile.usageInstructions: Basic scalable offensive spell. Spend 10 mana per missile; each missile deals 1 to 2 arcane damage. Missiles may focus one visible target or split among visible targets. Use normal hit/defense logic unless the scene establishes a special auto-strike rule.

Snap Casting.name: Snap Casting
Snap Casting.description: Immediately cast a second spell after resolving your first spell. This represents an Arcanist's trained ability to hold two spell-shapes in sequence and release them before the first flow fully fades.
Snap Casting.usageInstructions: Burst casting and action-economy technique. The Arcanist still pays the mana cost for both spells. The second spell must be quick and reasonable to cast; large rituals, complex circles, long incantations, or heavily prepared effects cannot be snap-cast.

Book Casting.name: Book Casting
Book Casting.description: Open, recall, or mentally reference spellbook formulae to cast with greater efficiency. For 4 rounds, your spells cost less mana because the spell-shape is stabilized by studied notes, diagrams, and practiced formulae.
Book Casting.usageInstructions: Mana-efficiency casting mode. For 4 rounds, reduce the mana cost of Arcanist spellcasting at narrator discretion, usually by making simple spells cheaper or reducing waste from complex spells. Does not make magic free and does not reduce non-Arcanist resource costs.

Dispel.name: Dispel
Dispel.description: Release a controlled unraveling pulse that attempts to break active magical effects within range. This can weaken or end wards, enchantments, curses, bindings, illusions, lingering spell effects, magical hazards, or unstable spellwork.
Dispel.usageInstructions: Area magical removal. Stronger, older, divine, draconic, circle-bound, heavily anchored, or plot-important magic may resist, weaken only partly, or require a contested Arcanist/Runic Magic/investigation check instead of vanishing automatically.

Counterspell.name: Counterspell
Counterspell.description: React defensively to hostile magic cast at you, identifying the spell-shape as it forms and disrupting it before it fully lands. This can negate, weaken, redirect, or destabilize offensive magic aimed at the Arcanist.
Counterspell.usageInstructions: Defensive anti-magic reaction. Works best against direct offensive magic aimed at the Arcanist. Area effects, breath-casting, rituals, traps, divine miracles, unknown magic, or effects aimed elsewhere may be harder to counter or impossible without preparation.

Arcane Analysis.name: Arcane Analysis
Arcane Analysis.description: Study an active spell, magical object, strange mana behavior, ward, curse, rune, enchantment, or supernatural phenomenon to understand its structure, weakness, purpose, or danger.
Arcane Analysis.usageInstructions: Magical investigation. Costs no mana for observation, but may require low mana if the Arcanist actively probes or tests a structure. In combat or tense scenes, use a 4-round cooldown if repeated analysis would slow action.

Air-Cut Circle.name: Air-Cut Circle
Air-Cut Circle.description: Trace a temporary magic circle into the air using mana, finger motion, tattoo flare, or a ritual implement. The circle hangs for a short time, allowing you to create a ward, trap, binding, alarm, seal, or trigger without needing chalk, ink, parchment, or carved ground.
Air-Cut Circle.usageInstructions: Temporary circle creation. Use for short-lived wards, traps, bindings, alarms, seals, or triggers in the air. The circle still needs correct geometry and can be disrupted by impact, anti-magic, poor concentration, or broken linework.

Mana-Curse Trap Circle.name: Mana-Curse Trap Circle
Mana-Curse Trap Circle.description: Draw a purposefully broken magic circle designed to fail when activated. When a mana-bearing creature touches the circle, the pattern attempts to complete its intended spell, sparks, sputters, and collapses into a draining feedback loop. Instead of releasing a spell, the broken circle continuously leeches mana from the target for as long as contact is maintained. The victim may feel numbness, vertigo, blood-warmth draining from the limbs, nosebleeds, trembling, or spell-shock as their mana is pulled into the failed circuit.
Mana-Curse Trap Circle.usageInstructions: Prepared mana-drain trap. Triggered by physical contact with the circle. Drains mana continuously while contact remains. Ends when contact is broken, the circle is disrupted, the pattern is smudged, or the target is removed from the circle. It drains mana, not stamina or breathing; creatures with no mana may suffer discomfort or shock rather than true mana loss.

Chained Circles.name: Chained Circles
Chained Circles.description: Draw compound magic circles with interlocking effects, allowing multiple circle functions to trigger together, in sequence, or under shared conditions. A Runescribe can use Chained Circles to combine wards, traps, alarms, bindings, seals, barriers, or mana-drain patterns into a single prepared ritual structure.
Chained Circles.usageInstructions: Compound circle construction. Mana cost scales with complexity. More circles mean more instability. Complex chains require time, space, correct geometry, stable surfaces, and a Runic Magic check. If one circle is damaged, smudged, overloaded, or drawn incorrectly, the whole chain may weaken, misfire, or collapse.

Ward Script.name: Ward Script
Ward Script.description: Draw, trace, or activate a defensive rune or circle to reduce harm, block hostile magic, protect a doorway, shield a small area, or stabilize a vulnerable target. Ward Script is slower and more deliberate than a Priest’s shield, but it can be prepared on surfaces, objects, or temporary circle-lines.
Ward Script.usageInstructions: Defensive warding. Use to reduce harm, block hostile magic, protect a doorway or small area, stabilize a vulnerable target, or reinforce a position with a prepared rune or circle.

Break Inscription.name: Break Inscription
Break Inscription.description: Study and disrupt a hostile rune, circle, seal, ward, or magical inscription by smudging, cutting, overwriting, grounding, or interrupting the pattern. This can disable traps, weaken wards, break binding circles, or collapse unstable runic effects.
Break Inscription.usageInstructions: Rune and circle disruption. Costs no mana for mundane disruption, or low mana if actively counter-inscribing. Use Runic Magic and investigation to identify where to break the pattern. Does not automatically defeat divine, ancient, heavily anchored, or secret endgame magic.

Set Humane Snare.name: Set Humane Snare
Set Humane Snare.description: Set a trap intended to restrain or slow a tameable creature without maiming it. Useful for capture, rescue, study, relocation, or preventing a creature from fleeing into danger.
Set Humane Snare.usageInstructions: Requires appropriate snare materials or a Snare Kit. Outside combat or chase scenes, use normal setup time instead of strict cooldown. The snare should restrain or slow, not mutilate.

Read Temperament.name: Read Temperament
Read Temperament.description: Study a creature’s posture, eyes, breath, sound, tail, wings, scent behavior, feeding signs, distance tolerance, and threat display to determine whether it is afraid, hungry, territorial, wounded, curious, protective, confused, or about to attack.
Read Temperament.usageInstructions: Use before taming, trapping, feeding, approaching, or commanding a creature. Reveal practical behavior and risk, not hidden lore beyond what the creature’s signs support.

Wild Call to Aid.name: Wild Call to Aid
Wild Call to Aid.description: Use voice, posture, scent, food, whistle-calls, and trained field signals to appeal to a nearby wild beast or tameable creature. On success, the creature aids you for 5 to 6 rounds before returning to its own instincts, territory, or safety. This does not create a permanent companion, pet, or mount; it is temporary cooperation based on trust, opportunity, instinct, and circumstance.
Wild Call to Aid.usageInstructions: Heavy-cooldown field support. Requires a suitable nearby creature that could plausibly respond. It cannot force loyalty, override sentience, command protected creatures, or summon animals from nowhere.

Cross-Reference.name: Cross-Reference
Cross-Reference.description: Pause to compare what you know against notes, records, memory, language patterns, historical precedent, or visible evidence. On success, reveal a useful connection, contradiction, missing detail, likely translation, or research lead.
Cross-Reference.usageInstructions: Use for research, deduction, clue connection, translation support, document comparison, historical context, and identifying contradictions. Outside combat, no cooldown. In tense scenes, use a 5-round cooldown if repeated analysis would slow action. Do not reveal secret truths without earned evidence.

Dissuade.name: Dissuade
Dissuade.description: Attempt to convince a hostile sentient enemy that killing you is unnecessary, unwise, unprofitable, dishonorable, illegal, politically dangerous, or less useful than keeping you alive. You may appeal to logic, fear of consequences, shared interests, moral hesitation, curiosity, ransom value, future usefulness, or a better bargain.
Dissuade.usageInstructions: Survival negotiation and hostile de-escalation. Dissuade does not mind-control the target. It only works on sentient enemies capable of understanding the Scholar, and is strongest when the argument fits the target's motives, culture, fear, duty, greed, pride, or situation. Does not work on mindless enemies, enemies who cannot understand the Scholar, enemies committed beyond reason, or situations where the target has no believable reason to spare them.

Check for Traps.name: Check for Traps
Check for Traps.description: Slow down and examine a door, chest, floor, wall, statue, hallway, bridge, stairway, corpse, treasure pile, or mechanism for traps and hidden danger.
Check for Traps.usageInstructions: Trap detection technique. Use investigation, Trap Sense, tools, lighting, dust patterns, seams, tension, air flow, sound, and architecture to identify pressure plates, tripwires, rigged chests, false stones, murder holes, hidden darts, unstable floors, and other hazards. No cooldown is needed outside tense scenes when time is available.

Disarm Mechanism.name: Disarm Mechanism
Disarm Mechanism.description: Use tools, wedges, picks, pins, tension control, careful pressure, or brute disabling to disarm a trap, lock, latch, hidden mechanism, tripwire, pressure plate, or rigged object.
Disarm Mechanism.usageInstructions: Trap and lock disarm technique. Requires appropriate tools or a plausible improvised method when the scene calls for them. It can disable mundane mechanisms and simple hazards; magical inscriptions, circles, or wards may require Runic Magic, a Runescribe, or another countermeasure.

Emergency Escape.name: Emergency Escape
Emergency Escape.description: React quickly to a collapsing floor, closing wall, rolling stone, falling ceiling, trap trigger, monster lunge, or sudden dungeon hazard. Gain a bonus to dodge, dive, roll, grab an edge, pull an ally, or escape the immediate danger.
Emergency Escape.usageInstructions: Emergency hazard reaction. Use for sudden dungeon dangers such as collapsing floors, triggered traps, closing walls, falling stone, monster lunges from confined spaces, cave-ins, blade traps, darts, fire vents, or collapsing bridges. This is not teleportation or immunity; it improves the chance to escape, reduce harm, or pull an ally clear.

Loot Appraisal.name: Loot Appraisal
Loot Appraisal.description: Quickly assess valuables, relics, coinage, equipment, crafting materials, suspicious treasure, or dungeon salvage to judge likely value, danger, authenticity, and portability.
Loot Appraisal.usageInstructions: Treasure assessment utility. Use investigation, Treasure Eye, material clues, weight, markings, dust, placement, craftsmanship, and context to judge whether loot is valuable, fake, damaged, trapped, cursed, too dangerous, too heavy, or worth carrying. Does not reveal secret/endgame lore without earned evidence.

Delver's Mark.name: Delver's Mark
Delver's Mark.description: Mark a safe route, dangerous path, checked door, trapped object, explored corridor, or return path using chalk, scratches, twine, dust marks, or coded symbols. Allies following your marks gain help avoiding repeated hazards or getting lost.
Delver's Mark.usageInstructions: Dungeon navigation and party utility. Use to mark cleared rooms, safe routes, return paths, hazards, trapped doors, false floors, explored corridors, bad air, or areas that need later investigation. Helps allies avoid getting lost or repeating already-identified hazards.

Drakona Sky-Patrol Recruit.name: Drakona Sky-Patrol Recruit
Drakona Sky-Patrol Recruit.storyStart: You stand on the Dragon Roost Platforms of Caelum Spire's upper level as a new sky-patrol recruit. Below, the city spirals around the central tower in three tiers; above, dragons and patrol riders cut across the wind. Your first assignment begins near the roost bells while Cloud-Piercer Plaza bustles far below on the middle level.
Drakona Sky-Patrol Recruit.description: Begin as a standard-issue Drakken navy private on Caelum Spire's aerial patrol lines. Expect sky-watch drills, roost duty, sabre-and-armor discipline, dragon flyovers, airship traffic, and the pressure of proving yourself beneath the shadow of Drakona's floating capital.

Drakona Queensguard Dragoon.name: Drakona Queensguard Dragoon
Drakona Queensguard Dragoon.storyStart: You report to the Queensguard Barracks, a separate anchored island chained to Caelum Spire's upper level. Across the wide aerial yards, Queensguard Dragoons drill beside their bonded dragons, launching from ramps and circling through open sky-combat rings. The central spire rises across the bridge, with Queen Veyra's Sky Throne high above.
Drakona Queensguard Dragoon.description: Begin among Drakona's most prestigious aerial warriors, sworn to protect Queen Veyra and the sovereignty of Caelum Spire. Expect elite dragoon protocol, greatspear training, dangerous sky-combat expectations, and the weight of serving in a corps where dragons and riders are watched by all Drakona.

Drakona Skyfarer.name: Drakona Skyfarer
Drakona Skyfarer.storyStart: You step onto the Sky-Ship Docks of Caelum Spire as wind snaps banners along the mooring towers. Cargo cranes swing over the edge of the floating city, airships groan against their lines, and Cloud-Piercer Plaza waits just beyond the customs deck.
Drakona Skyfarer.description: Begin as a freelance skyfarer moving through Caelum Spire's docks, winds, contracts, and airship traffic. Expect aerial travel, smuggling rumors, merchant disputes, sky-lane hazards, and the freedom—and risk—of making your own way above the world.

Drakona Wyrmforge Artisan.name: Drakona Wyrmforge Artisan
Drakona Wyrmforge Artisan.storyStart: You begin your shift inside the Wyrmforge Atelier on Caelum Spire's middle level. Molted scales, dragon-glass, dracosilk fittings, and skyship hardware line the workbenches while the Sky Market District hums outside. Above, the central spire casts its long shadow over the city.
Drakona Wyrmforge Artisan.description: Begin as a Wyrmforge craftsperson surrounded by dracosilk, dragon-glass, skyship fittings, and the exacting standards of Drakona's artisan culture. Expect craft commissions, forge politics, rare materials, and the chance to turn skill at the bench into a wider destiny.

Drakona Councillor.name: Drakona Councillor
Drakona Councillor.storyStart:
You stand within the grand Magi-Knight Citadel of Aethelgard, the air thick with the weight of history. Before you, the Exarch of the Order of the Gryphon steps forward to the center of the chamber. His voice carries across the hall as he addresses the assembled gathering:

"Leaders of Balen, I bid you welcome to this unprecedented summit. To my right stands Queen Veyra of Drakona, sovereign of the skies and protector of the Dragon-Blooded. To my left, Emperor Aureon Dawnmere of the Holy Kingdom, bearer of the Crystal Crown and guardian of the southern plains. Across from me, the honored delegation of the Republic of Valen, led by Minister Gerard, voice of the industrial heartland. And representing the ancient forests of Sylvera, Queen Elowyn and her Councillor, guardians of the World-Roots."

The Exarch's expression grows grave. "But we have not gathered merely to exchange pleasantries. There is a matter of grave concern that has brought all four nations to this table—a matter that threatens the balance of our world. I call upon each of you to speak your truth, for the fate of Balen may well rest upon the words spoken here today."
Drakona Councillor.description: Begin as a Drakona political voice at a tense four-nation summit in Aethelgard. Expect diplomacy, veiled threats, national pride, fragile alliances, and the burden of representing Queen Veyra's interests while every word may shift the balance of Balen.

Republic Line Infantry.name: Republic Line Infantry
Republic Line Infantry.storyStart: You stand at attention in Musketeer Bastion, standard-issue repeating rifle slung across your back while drill bells echo from the Clockwork Tower. As a fresh private in the Republic Musketeers, you can smell coal smoke from the foundries and hear Ironmile Avenue roaring beyond the walls.
Republic Line Infantry.description: Begin as a Republic musketeer private in Ironhaven, drilled under smoke, brass, bells, and blackpowder discipline. Expect military orders, musket lines, industrial unrest, dangerous patrols, and the hard life of a soldier serving a nation that trusts steel more than magic.

Republic Iron Legion Lieutenant.name: Republic Iron Legion Lieutenant
Republic Iron Legion Lieutenant.storyStart: You stand in the Blimp Mooring Yard overlooking Ironhaven's rail lines and tether towers. Signal flags, rail schedules, troop manifests, wind notes, and blimp dispatch boards cover the table before you. As an Iron Legion lieutenant, your orders move by steel track, wagon column, signal balloon, and the Republic's slow but watchful sky.
Republic Iron Legion Lieutenant.description: Begin as a Republic officer responsible for soldiers, dispatches, and hard decisions across rail, road, and blimp routes. Expect command pressure, blackpowder logistics, political oversight, battlefield pragmatism, and the burden of keeping Republic forces effective without igniting open war.

Republic Dockside Scrapper.name: Republic Dockside Scrapper
Republic Dockside Scrapper.storyStart: You wipe grease from your hands beside the Industrial Docks and Rail Yards of Ironhaven. Cargo cranes scream, railcars clatter, blimps groan against their moorings, and discarded machinery waits for someone clever enough to make coin from scrap.
Republic Dockside Scrapper.description: Begin in the soot, noise, and opportunity of Ironhaven's industrial docks. Expect salvage work, black-market whispers, broken machinery, dockside rivalries, dangerous bargains, and the chance to turn scrap, grit, and nerve into survival.

Republic Blackpowder Machinist.name: Republic Blackpowder Machinist
Republic Blackpowder Machinist.storyStart: The smell of gunpowder, hot metal, and machine oil fills your bay in the Blackpowder Foundries of Ironhaven. As a master machinist, you craft the Republic's firearms, pressure parts, and industrial engines beneath strict safety bells and louder political ambition.
Republic Blackpowder Machinist.description: Begin as a technical specialist in Ironhaven's foundries, surrounded by muskets, engines, powder, and pressure parts. Expect invention, accidents, military contracts, factory politics, and the danger of knowing exactly how Republic weapons work.

Republic Senator.name: Republic Senator
Republic Senator.storyStart:
You stand within the grand Magi-Knight Citadel of Aethelgard, the air thick with the weight of history. Before you, the Exarch of the Order of the Gryphon steps forward to the center of the chamber. His voice carries across the hall as he addresses the assembled gathering:

"Leaders of Balen, I bid you welcome to this unprecedented summit. To my right stands Queen Veyra of Drakona, sovereign of the skies and protector of the Dragon-Blooded. To my left, Emperor Aureon Dawnmere of the Holy Kingdom, bearer of the Crystal Crown and guardian of the southern plains. Across from me, the honored delegation of the Republic of Valen, led by Minister Gerard, voice of the industrial heartland. And representing the ancient forests of Sylvera, Queen Elowyn and her Councillor, guardians of the World-Roots."

The Exarch's expression grows grave. "But we have not gathered merely to exchange pleasantries. There is a matter of grave concern that has brought all four nations to this table—a matter that threatens the balance of our world. I call upon each of you to speak your truth, for the fate of Balen may well rest upon the words spoken here today."
Republic Senator.description: Begin as a Republic delegate at a four-nation summit where industrial ambition meets old crowns, dragons, forests, and neutral law. Expect speeches, backroom bargaining, ideological clashes, and the chance to shape history without drawing a blade.

Holy Kingdom Templar Recruit.name: Holy Kingdom Templar Recruit
Holy Kingdom Templar Recruit.storyStart: You kneel in the Throne-Sanctum of Crownheart Citadel as a newly sworn templar recruit. Morning light pours through stained glass behind the Emperor's throne and strikes the Crystal Crown embedded above it. Your oath echoes beneath the holy ceiling while Glaives stand silent along the aisle.
Holy Kingdom Templar Recruit.description: Begin newly sworn beneath the light of Crownheart's Throne-Sanctum. Expect holy vows, plate-and-glaive discipline, temple authority, knightly scrutiny, and the difficult path of proving that devotion can survive fear, politics, and blood.

Knight of the Crystal Crown.name: Knight of the Crystal Crown
Knight of the Crystal Crown.storyStart: You stand in full plate in the Crown Knight Barracks inside Crownheart's castle walls. Beyond the drill yard, Crownheart Citadel rises over the Castle Courtyard, and within it the Throne-Sanctum safeguards the Crystal Crown embedded above the Emperor's throne. Your duty is to protect the Crown, the Emperor, and the people who pass beneath the Crownway banners.
Knight of the Crystal Crown.description: Begin as a sworn protector of Crownheart, armored in the service of the Crystal Crown and the people beneath its light. Expect courtly duty, patrols, noble pressure, sacred law, and the constant question of whether honor can hold when the kingdom trembles.

Faithful Pilgrim.name: Faithful Pilgrim
Faithful Pilgrim.storyStart: You walk the Crownway Fairway of Crownheart with a pilgrim's staff in hand. The straight cobblestone road stretches from the outer gates toward the castle walls and the white citadel beyond. Pilgrims, merchants, knights, and priests move around you, all drawn toward the Throne-Sanctum where the Crystal Crown catches the morning sun.
Faithful Pilgrim.description: Begin on the Crownway among merchants, priests, beggars, knights, and travelers drawn toward the Crystal Crown. Expect faith, rumor, road danger, temple politics, personal revelation, and the freedom to decide whether pilgrimage becomes devotion, discovery, or escape.

Holy Kingdom Lightforge Artisan.name: Holy Kingdom Lightforge Artisan
Holy Kingdom Lightforge Artisan.storyStart: You work in a guarded reliquary-workroom adjoining the Citadel Archives of Crownheart. Ledgers, sacred diagrams, and old Crown-light records lie open beside your tools as you prepare a lightforged relic under priestly supervision. Not far away, the Throne-Sanctum holds the Crystal Crown above the Emperor's throne.
Holy Kingdom Lightforge Artisan.description: Begin as a sacred craftsperson working under Crownheart's watchful eyes. Expect relic commissions, guarded archives, priestly oversight, noble patrons, and the pressure of shaping objects meant to carry faith, power, and public trust.

Holy Kingdom Councillor.name: Holy Kingdom Councillor
Holy Kingdom Councillor.storyStart:
You stand within the grand Magi-Knight Citadel of Aethelgard, the air thick with the weight of history. Before you, the Exarch of the Order of the Gryphon steps forward to the center of the chamber. His voice carries across the hall as he addresses the assembled gathering:

"Leaders of Balen, I bid you welcome to this unprecedented summit. To my right stands Queen Veyra of Drakona, sovereign of the skies and protector of the Dragon-Blooded. To my left, Emperor Aureon Dawnmere of the Holy Kingdom, bearer of the Crystal Crown and guardian of the southern plains. Across from me, the honored delegation of the Republic of Valen, led by Minister Gerard, voice of the industrial heartland. And representing the ancient forests of Sylvera, Queen Elowyn and her Councillor, guardians of the World-Roots."

The Exarch's expression grows grave. "But we have not gathered merely to exchange pleasantries. There is a matter of grave concern that has brought all four nations to this table—a matter that threatens the balance of our world. I call upon each of you to speak your truth, for the fate of Balen may well rest upon the words spoken here today."
Holy Kingdom Councillor.description: Begin as a Holy Kingdom representative at the four-nation summit, carrying the dignity of the Crown into a hall full of rivals. Expect sacred politics, noble calculation, diplomacy under pressure, and hard choices between peace, pride, and duty.

Sylvera Forest Warden.name: Sylvera Forest Warden
Sylvera Forest Warden.storyStart: You move silently through the canopy of the Deepwood as a young Forest Warden. Your bow is nocked and your silver blade ready to defend the sacred groves.
Sylvera Forest Warden.description: Begin as a defender of Sylvera's living canopy and sacred groves. Expect patrols through ancient boughs, silvered arms, hidden paths, trespassers, wild threats, and the quiet burden of protecting a forest that remembers more than it says.

Sylvera Shadowblade Ranger.name: Sylvera Shadowblade Ranger
Sylvera Shadowblade Ranger.storyStart: You perch high in the ancient trees of the Deepwood as a veteran Shadowblade Ranger. Your guerrilla skills and silver-steel blade have felled many intruders.
Sylvera Shadowblade Ranger.description: Begin as a veteran deepwood guerrilla trained to strike from the canopy and vanish before the forest breathes again. Expect ambushes, border tension, stealth, silver blades, and missions where silence protects Sylvera better than banners ever could.

Sylvera Grove Wanderer.name: Sylvera Grove Wanderer
Sylvera Grove Wanderer.storyStart: You walk the hidden paths of the Deepwood, a lone wanderer among the sacred trees. As an elven adventurer, you seek knowledge and quiet purpose away from outsiders.
Sylvera Grove Wanderer.description: Begin as a lone traveler among World-Root paths, leaf-shadowed bridges, and old forest whispers. Expect quiet mysteries, spiritual pressure, hidden groves, wandering purpose, and the choice between remaining apart or stepping into Balen's wider conflicts.

Sylvera Silverweave Artisan.name: Sylvera Silverweave Artisan
Sylvera Silverweave Artisan.storyStart: You sit in the silver-lit forge of Eldertree Enclave, shaping delicate silver-steel into an enchanted blade. As a master Silverweave artisan, your work is revered among the elves.
Sylvera Silverweave Artisan.description: Begin as a Sylveran artisan shaping silvered craft beneath ancient boughs and watchful tradition. Expect delicate metalwork, forest law, rare commissions, artistic pride, and the chance for a maker's hands to touch secrets older than the forge.

Sylvera Councillor.name: Sylvera Councillor
Sylvera Councillor.storyStart:
You stand within the grand Magi-Knight Citadel of Aethelgard, the air thick with the weight of history. Before you, the Exarch of the Order of the Gryphon steps forward to the center of the chamber. His voice carries across the hall as he addresses the assembled gathering:

"Leaders of Balen, I bid you welcome to this unprecedented summit. To my right stands Queen Veyra of Drakona, sovereign of the skies and protector of the Dragon-Blooded. To my left, Emperor Aureon Dawnmere of the Holy Kingdom, bearer of the Crystal Crown and guardian of the southern plains. Across from me, the honored delegation of the Republic of Valen, led by Minister Gerard, voice of the industrial heartland. And representing the ancient forests of Sylvera, Queen Elowyn and her Councillor, guardians of the World-Roots."

The Exarch's expression grows grave. "But we have not gathered merely to exchange pleasantries. There is a matter of grave concern that has brought all four nations to this table—a matter that threatens the balance of our world. I call upon each of you to speak your truth, for the fate of Balen may well rest upon the words spoken here today."
Sylvera Councillor.description: Begin as Sylvera's voice at a tense four-nation summit in Aethelgard. Expect patient diplomacy, old forest wisdom, cultural suspicion, political pressure, and the challenge of defending Sylvera's future without surrendering its silence.

Balen Free Adventurer.name: Balen Free Adventurer
Balen Free Adventurer.storyStart: You stand in the crowded central market square of Aethelgard, the neutral heart of Balen. No banner flies above you, no oath binds you to any nation. With only your wits, a few coins, and the open road ahead, you are free to travel wherever the wind or your ambition takes you—whether that means joining a Drakona sky-armada, working the Republic forges, seeking the Crystal Crown’s light, or vanishing into the Sylvera deepwood.
Balen Free Adventurer.description: Begin in Aethelgard with no banner above you and no oath deciding your road. Expect open-ended adventure, guild work, rumors from every nation, dangerous choices, and the freedom to become a hero, fool, fugitive, legend, or something stranger.

The Wounded Wyrm.name: The Wounded Wyrm
The Wounded Wyrm.storyStart:
You trek through the rugged wilderness bordering the neutral territories when you crest a rocky ridge and spot a massive wounded dragon lying among the boulders. One of its wings is bent at a painful angle, deep gashes mar its shimmering scales, and it breathes heavily. The dragon lifts its head weakly, eyes watching you with cautious intelligence rather than hostility.

IMPORTANT: This dragon's gender is ALWAYS the opposite of your character's gender. If you are male, the dragon is female. If you are female, the dragon is male. This is a core rule of this story start and must be followed.
The Wounded Wyrm.description: Begin in the wilderness with a wounded dragon before you and a choice that could change both your lives. Expect immediate moral tension, danger, trust-building, draconic mystery, and a story shaped by whether you see a dragon as a prize, threat, or person.

Continuation.name: Continuation
Continuation.storyStart: Silas Dey Corvand, Sylvareth, Kaelith, and Pyrrhos begin at the Wayfarer's Accord Inn in Aethelgard. They have been staying there for about a week, using the inn and its connected party room as a temporary base. All listed active party members are present and persist unless explicitly separated. Use the linked world lore entry for their full shared history.
Continuation.description: Continuation of Silas and Sylvareth. Start at the Wayfarer's Accord Inn in Aethelgard after the party has stayed there for about a week.
Continuation.continuationSummary: The party has been staying at the Wayfarer's Accord Inn in Aethelgard for about a week. Full Silas/Sylvareth/Kaelith/Pyrrhos history is stored in worldLore['Continuation: Silas, Sylvareth, Kaelith, and Pyrrhos'].
openingRules: Begin at The Wayfarer's Accord Inn inside Aethelgard.
The party has been staying there for about a week.
Silas, Sylvareth, Kaelith, and Pyrrhos are physically present.
Keep all active party members present unless explicitly separated.
Use the linked world lore entry for full shared history.
Continuation._note: Continuation begins at the Wayfarer's Accord Inn in Aethelgard after about one week.
Continuation.partyPersistenceRule: Listed activePartyMembers remain present unless explicitly separated by player action, plot, danger, command, injury, or travel logistics.
Continuation.fugitiveToneRule: Silas and Sylvareth are not hiding from other nations. They are only at serious legal/political risk if they return to Drakona, where they may be detained or forced apart.

Kazien.name: Kazien
Kazien.storyStart: Morning settles over Cranemere Estate, quiet except for birdsong, the faint hum of ward-stones, and the distant bells of Aethelgard beyond the Marches. Kazien Cranehollow begins inside Cottage Hearth with the authored NPC Commander Valyrine Dawnmere, his wife, physically present with him. Her greatsword rests nearby, and the garden still bears scars from their last round of mock combat. The day is open: stay at the cottage, work in the rear workshop, spar in the yard, answer a Citadel message, take a runesmith commission, or let trouble find the one place Kazien once thought he would never deserve.
Kazien.description: Begin as Kazien Cranehollow at Cranemere Estate, the cottage home he bought for himself and Valyrine Dawnmere near Aethelgard. Expect runesmith commissions, Magi-Knight ties, domestic moments with Valyrine, echoes of the Five-Month War, dangerous circle theory, and the choice between quiet home life, Citadel duty, and whatever trouble needs a master runesmith.
Kazien.hiddenInfo: This start is intended for the Kazien Cranehollow premade character. Use the existing authored NPC Commander Valyrine Dawnmere as Kazien’s wife and active companion at Cranemere Estate; do not generate a duplicate spouse or replacement wolf-worcen. Cranemere Estate is their private home while Valyrine is on leave. The opening should start at Cottage Hearth, with the workshop, training yard, and Aethelgard/Magi-Knight Citadel available as later destinations through player choice, not as alternate random starting points. Keep the opening calm but playable: commissions, Citadel messages, workshop work, sparring, domestic banter, or the choice to travel to Aethelgard are all valid first directions.Do not formulate the openning as a question or wait for the player to make a choice, dialogue and narration should be fluid and organic
openingRules: Begin inside Cottage Hearth at Cranemere Estate in the Aetherian Marches.
Kazien Cranehollow and the existing authored NPC Commander Valyrine Dawnmere are physically present in the cottage at the start.
Use the authored NPC Commander Valyrine Dawnmere as Kazien's wife. Do not create a new Valyrine Dawnmere, wife, spouse, wolf-worcen, direwolf-worcen, or replacement companion.
Valyrine Dawnmere and Commander Valyrine Dawnmere are the same person for this start.
Keep Commander Valyrine Dawnmere present unless the player explicitly separates from her or the scene gives a clear in-world reason.
Use premadeCharacter Kazien Cranehollow as the player character for this start.
Active party at start is exactly Kazien Cranehollow and the authored NPC Commander Valyrine Dawnmere.
Do not generate, rename, duplicate, or substitute Valyrine Dawnmere; use Commander Valyrine Dawnmere from the NPC list.
Kazien.partyPersistenceRule: Kazien Cranehollow and Commander Valyrine Dawnmere begin physically present together at Cranemere Estate / Cottage Hearth. Commander Valyrine Dawnmere remains the active authored spouse/companion NPC for this start. Do not replace Kazien Cranehollow or Commander Valyrine Dawnmere with generated NPCs. Valyrine remains present unless explicitly separated by player action, duty, danger, command, injury, or travel logistics.

narratorStyle:
Third-person, present tense. Never call the player “you”; use the character’s name, title, or pronoun.

Player agency is absolute. Never invent the character’s speech, inner life, consent, decisions, movement, attacks, spells, interactions, or voluntary reactions. Resolve only stated or delegated actions. The narrator may describe sensation, involuntary responses, and unavoidable consequences, but never chooses the character’s response.

Keep the world moving. NPCs follow motives; danger, conflict, and consequences advance naturally. Never end with a question, menu, or meta handoff, or freeze NPCs waiting. End on an organic beat—NPC action or dialogue, revelation, pressure, change, or consequence—without acting for the player character.

Balen is a living world, not a protagonist’s stage. Across all four nations, people work, trade, worship, love, raise families, gossip, pray, and survive amid war and politics. Prejudice, grudges, class tension, faith, and racial suspicion exist but are not universal; people may be kind, practical, tired, conflicted, or uninvolved.

Use mature, cinematic, grounded dark fantasy: beautiful, political, sensual, dangerous, and violent. Favor vivid sensory detail—steel, stone, blood, dragon breath, smoke, rain, ash, mana pressure, and silence before violence. Combat is unsanitized; wounds, burns, broken bones, mutilation, and death may be ugly and sudden.

Magic is wondrous and dangerous, with texture, cost, strain, risk, and consequence. Love, lust, jealousy, tenderness, marriage, and sexual tension matter. Player-led, character-appropriate consensual adult intimacy may be raunchy, explicit, erotic, sensation-based, and use direct vulgar anatomy.

NPCs have motives, duties, and limited knowledge. Preserve authored rulers, commanders, councils, spouses, companions, and faction leaders. Do not turn ordinary tension into Shaydes, Daemons, cults, or mysterious attackers unless lore earns it.

Peace.name: Peace
Peace.description: Default adventure mode. Balen is currently stable: the nations have old grudges, politics, monsters, crime, ruins, and local problems, but no active national crisis.
Peace.instructions:
## MODE PURPOSE
Peace mode keeps Balen in a stable peacetime state. Nations have rivalries, old wounds, patrols, crime, monsters, politics, trade disputes, accidents, and personal quests, but no active inter-nation war unless the user directly starts one.

## CANON ACCURACY RULES
- Use existing Balen lore before invention: named nations, known factions, authored NPCs, established monsters, Slayer's Bestiary creatures, false-wyrms, terrain, politics, logistics, and known supernatural forces.
- Do not invent random new monster species, alien creatures, unknown ancient races, mysterious cosmic enemies, or setting-breaking threats for drama.
- Shaydes, Daemons, Obsidian Blight, Abyssal Tower forces, and otherworldly threats are not default explanations. Use them only if explicitly called by the user, story start, location, quest, or established scene.
- Do not invent shared borders. Valen and Sylvera do not share a direct land border. Dragonfall Pass is Drakona's southernmost land border; Aerial Strongholds are not Drakona's southern border.

## THREAT STYLE
- Focus on civic conflict, local monsters, travel hazards, guild business, personal drama, diplomacy, trade, investigation, and ordinary danger.
- Military forces may appear as patrols, guards, exercises, or political pressure, not active invading armies.
- Do not escalate peaceful scenes into world-ending or otherworldly crises without explicit user direction.
Peace.askTheNarratorPrompt: Run Balen in a stable adventure state. Keep national war and border skirmishes inactive at the opening unless the chosen story start explicitly requires them.

Tension.name: Tension
Tension.description: Cold-war tension mode. No nations are openly fighting yet, but diplomacy is strained and one bad incident could become a skirmish or war.
Tension.instructions:
## MODE PURPOSE
Tension mode means nations are uneasy but not openly at war. Rumors, patrol buildup, espionage, border alerts, trade restrictions, propaganda, military drills, and suspicious incidents can occur.

## CANON ACCURACY RULES
- Use existing Balen lore before invention: named nations, known factions, authored NPCs, established monsters, Slayer's Bestiary creatures, false-wyrms, terrain, politics, logistics, and known supernatural forces.
- Do not invent random new monster species, alien creatures, unknown ancient races, mysterious cosmic enemies, or setting-breaking threats for drama.
- Shaydes, Daemons, Obsidian Blight, Abyssal Tower forces, and otherworldly threats are not default explanations. Use them only if explicitly called by the user, story start, location, quest, or established scene.
- Do not invent shared borders. Valen and Sylvera do not share a direct land border. Dragonfall Pass is Drakona's southernmost land border; Aerial Strongholds are not Drakona's southern border.

## THREAT STYLE
- Keep pressure political and mortal by default: spies, scouts, suspicious shipments, propaganda, sabotage, diplomats, deserters, smugglers, military exercises, and local monsters disturbed by tension.
- Incidents should be deniable or limited unless the user escalates them.
- Do not reveal that a mortal conflict is secretly Shayde/Daemon/otherworldly unless explicitly established by the active story.
Tension.askTheNarratorPrompt: Run Balen in a tense pre-war state. Make diplomacy fragile and danger plausible, but do not begin with open war or skirmishes.

Border Skirmishes.name: Border Skirmishes
Border Skirmishes.description: Localized border conflict mode. Nations are not in full war, but small clashes have broken out along legitimate borderlands and frontier routes.
Border Skirmishes.instructions:
## MODE PURPOSE
Border Skirmishes mode creates limited clashes, raids, patrol fights, fort alerts, scouting actions, and disputed-route incidents without full national mobilization.

## CANON ACCURACY RULES
- Use existing Balen lore before invention: named nations, known factions, authored NPCs, established monsters, Slayer's Bestiary creatures, false-wyrms, terrain, politics, logistics, and known supernatural forces.
- Do not invent random new monster species, alien creatures, unknown ancient races, mysterious cosmic enemies, or setting-breaking threats for drama.
- Shaydes, Daemons, Obsidian Blight, Abyssal Tower forces, and otherworldly threats are not default explanations. Use them only if explicitly called by the user, story start, location, quest, or established scene.
- Do not invent shared borders. Valen and Sylvera do not share a direct land border. Dragonfall Pass is Drakona's southernmost land border; Aerial Strongholds are not Drakona's southern border.

## VALID SKIRMISH LOGIC
- Skirmishes must use geography that exists in Balen. Use the correct frontier, pass, road, sky route, trade route, coast, or neutral territory.
- Drakona's southern border scenes use Dragonfall Pass, not Aerial Strongholds.
- Valen vs Sylvera is not an ordinary border-skirmish pairing because they do not share a land border. Use proxy conflict, expeditionary scouts, sabotage, neutral routes, trade disruption, or third-party territory.
- Drakona vs Holy Kingdom is not a default hostile border pairing because they have strong alliance ties; it requires an explicit named cause.

## THREAT STYLE
- Threats should be soldiers, scouts, spies, patrols, saboteurs, supply teams, deserters, smugglers, fort pressure, terrain, weather, and established creatures displaced or used by the conflict.
- Do not create brand-new monster species or cosmic war causes.
Border Skirmishes.askTheNarratorPrompt: Run Balen with limited border skirmishes between dynamically selected, geographically plausible nations. Keep conflict localized and clearly national, with Aethelgard acting as mediator.

Inter-Nation War.name: Inter-Nation War
Inter-Nation War.description: Open war mode. Two mortal nations are formally or functionally at war, while Aethelgard struggles to mediate and protect civilians.
Inter-Nation War.instructions:
## MODE PURPOSE
Inter-Nation War mode means two or more nations are in open mortal conflict. The war should have named belligerents, visible fronts, commanders, armies, logistics, civilians, refugees, spies, propaganda, supply shortages, raids, and political consequences.

## CANON ACCURACY RULES
- Use existing Balen lore before invention: named nations, known factions, authored NPCs, established monsters, Slayer's Bestiary creatures, false-wyrms, terrain, politics, logistics, and known supernatural forces.
- Do not invent random new monster species, alien creatures, unknown ancient races, mysterious cosmic enemies, or setting-breaking threats for drama.
- Shaydes, Daemons, Obsidian Blight, Abyssal Tower forces, and otherworldly threats are not default explanations. Use them only if explicitly called by the user, story start, location, quest, or established scene.
- Do not invent shared borders. Valen and Sylvera do not share a direct land border. Dragonfall Pass is Drakona's southernmost land border; Aerial Strongholds are not Drakona's southern border.

## WAR ROUTING
- Name the belligerent nations early and keep them primary.
- Pressure comes from national strategy: scouts, raids, sieges, supply lines, terrain, morale, propaganda, assassins, defectors, fortifications, military monsters, elite units, weather, and commanders.
- Valen vs Sylvera is never a simple border war. Frame it through expeditionary logistics, proxy forces, neutral-territory crisis, sabotage, trade-route conflict, air/naval movement, diplomacy, or a deliberate invasion route.
- Drakona southern-front scenes use Dragonfall Pass. Aerial Strongholds scenes are northern/internal airspace or deep-incursion scenes.

## FORBIDDEN DEFAULTS
- Do not make the war secretly caused by Shaydes, Daemons, ancient seals, Obsidian Blight, or unknown cosmic forces unless the user explicitly chooses that escalation.
- Do not invent new enemy species or world-breaking monsters to replace the attacking nation.
Inter-Nation War.askTheNarratorPrompt: Run Balen with a clear two-nation war chosen dynamically from political tensions. Name the belligerents early and keep Aethelgard a strained neutral responder.

World War.name: World War
World War.description: Extreme continental war mode. Multiple fronts threaten Balen's political order, and the Magi-Knights are stretched thin trying to prevent total collapse.
World War.instructions:
## MODE PURPOSE
World War mode means Balen is in a mortal national catastrophe involving multiple nations, fronts, alliances, broken diplomacy, refugees, strained economies, propaganda, mobilization, elite units, sabotage, and major military decisions.

## CANON ACCURACY RULES
- Use existing Balen lore before invention: named nations, known factions, authored NPCs, established monsters, Slayer's Bestiary creatures, false-wyrms, terrain, politics, logistics, and known supernatural forces.
- Do not invent random new monster species, alien creatures, unknown ancient races, mysterious cosmic enemies, or setting-breaking threats for drama.
- Shaydes, Daemons, Obsidian Blight, Abyssal Tower forces, and otherworldly threats are not default explanations. Use them only if explicitly called by the user, story start, location, quest, or established scene.
- Do not invent shared borders. Valen and Sylvera do not share a direct land border. Dragonfall Pass is Drakona's southernmost land border; Aerial Strongholds are not Drakona's southern border.

## WORLD WAR ROUTING
- Establish named national fronts first. Each front must respect canon geography and existing alliances, roads, passes, skies, ports, neutral territories, and impassable barriers.
- The main threats are nations, armies, commanders, logistics, spies, sieges, terrain, supply collapse, displaced civilians, military monsters, and political decisions.
- Otherworldly threats may appear only as rare complications after the national war is already established and only if the user, story start, location, or active quest explicitly brings them in.
- Do not turn World War into 'actually Shayde did it' by default.

## GEOGRAPHY LOCKS
- Valen and Sylvera do not share a direct land border.
- Dragonfall Pass is Drakona's southernmost land border.
- Aerial Strongholds are not Drakona's southern border.
World War.askTheNarratorPrompt: Run Balen in a clear multi-front world war between named mortal nations. Keep Aethelgard a strained neutral mediator and defender unless directly escalated.

Human.name: Human
Human.description: Versatile and adaptable, humans thrive in almost any environment and can excel in nearly any profession. They learn quickly, adjust under pressure, and bring broad competence to both practical work and dangerous travel.
Human.traitNarrativeEffects: You have an uncanny ability to fit in anywhere, picking up local customs and mannerisms within days. Your versatility makes you a natural at any task you put your mind to.

Beastkin.name: Beastkin
Beastkin.description: Animal-featured people with heightened senses, quick bodies, and a natural rapport with beasts. Beastkin often read animal moods instinctively, and even aggressive creatures may hesitate around them.
Beastkin.traitNarrativeEffects: Your ears twitch when you're curious or alert, and your eyes reflect light like a predator's in the dark. You have a natural affinity with animals of all kinds.

Elf.name: Elf
Elf.description: Graceful, long-lived people with a natural affinity for magic, forests, memory, and patient craft. Elves often move with quiet precision and sense magical or natural patterns more easily than most.
Elf.traitNarrativeEffects: You move with unnatural grace, and time seems to pass differently for you — what feels like moments to others may be hours to you. You can feel when spells are being cast nearby.

Drakken.name: Drakken
Drakken.description: A distinct humanoid dragon-like people marked by scales, horns or horn ridges, powerful bodies, draconic facial influence, and natural breath magic. Drakken are not true dragons and are not descended from true dragons; their resemblance and breath magic are racial traits of their own people. True dragon children, heirs, mates, and council offspring are dragons, not Drakken.
Drakken.traitNarrativeEffects: Your eyes glow with inner fire when you're angry or excited, and smoke sometimes curls from your nostrils when you exhale. You feel a deep kinship with dragons.

Worcen.name: Worcen
Worcen.description: Civilized werebeast-like people who can shift into a feral ancestral form. Worcens are guardians of the boundary between the settled world and the wild, carrying stronger bestial anatomy and deeper animal instinct than ordinary beastkin.
Worcen.traitNarrativeEffects: Your eyes flash with animal instinct when threatened, and you sometimes growl or purr without realizing it. When you shift, your eyes glow with primal energy and you can remain in your animal form indefinitely.

Drakken Heritage.name: Drakken Heritage
Drakken Heritage.description: Raised among Drakken communities, true dragons, sky-cities, and Drakonan military culture. You understand Drakona's mountains, aerial warfare, etiquette, and breath-magic traditions.

Sylvera Heritage.name: Sylvera Heritage
Sylvera Heritage.description: Raised in the sacred deepwood. Knowledge of forests, guerrilla tactics, and elven customs.

Republic Heritage.name: Republic Heritage
Republic Heritage.description: Raised in the industrial heartlands. Knowledge of factories, gunpowder tech, and urban life.

Holy Heritage.name: Holy Heritage
Holy Heritage.description: Raised in the agrarian plains under the Crystal Crown. Knowledge of faith, farming, and knightly traditions.

Neutral Heritage.name: Neutral Heritage
Neutral Heritage.description: Raised in Aethelgard. Broad knowledge of all four nations and diplomatic customs.

Natural Dracomancer.name: Natural Dracomancer
Natural Dracomancer.description: You have instinctive breath-control, throat discipline, and draconic casting reflexes that make Dracomancy easier to use. This trait is natural to Drakken, Drakken-blooded lineages, true-dragon descendants, or explicit story exceptions.
Natural Dracomancer.traitNarrativeEffects: Your breath carries draconic pressure. When you steady yourself, the air in your chest feels like a banked furnace, storm, frost, acid, or healing warmth waiting to be released.
unlockedBy: Drakken
Draconic Bloodline
Drakken Bloodline

Gunpowder Prodigy.name: Gunpowder Prodigy
Gunpowder Prodigy.description: You take naturally to firearms, powder discipline, loading rhythm, and the sharp mathematics of black-powder weapons. Smoke, recoil, timing, and maintenance make sense in your hands.
Gunpowder Prodigy.traitNarrativeEffects: You notice fouled barrels, damp powder, bad springs, and unsafe firing lines before most people know anything is wrong.

Blade Master.name: Blade Master
Blade Master.description: Blades feel familiar in your hand, whether short knife, soldier’s sword, dueling blade, or field steel. Your instincts favor edge alignment, footwork, timing, and clean openings.
Blade Master.traitNarrativeEffects: Your hand drifts toward a blade hilt when you think, and you read a stranger’s stance before you read their face.

Arcane Aptitude.name: Arcane Aptitude
Arcane Aptitude.description: You have a natural gift for understanding magical patterns, spell structure, mana behavior, and arcane theory. Even when magic is dangerous or unfamiliar, its logic comes to you more easily than it does to others.
Arcane Aptitude.traitNarrativeEffects: You find yourself tracing ward-lines, comparing symbols, and noticing when magic behaves differently than it should.

Stealth Expert.name: Stealth Expert
Stealth Expert.description: You have an instinct for moving unseen and unheard. Your steps find quiet places, your posture fades from attention, and you know when to still your breath.
Stealth Expert.traitNarrativeEffects: You choose shadows, corners, crowds, and blind spots without thinking, even when there is no danger.

Dandy.name: Dandy
Dandy.description: You are polished, stylish, socially attentive, and difficult to ignore, but your taste for presentation can make rough conditions and practical hardship harder to stomach.
Dandy.traitNarrativeEffects: You notice poor tailoring, cheap perfume, and bad manners at exactly the wrong time.
Dandy.tradeoff: Better in social and performative situations; worse at enduring rough wilderness or ugly hardship.
Dandy.balanceRole: Quirk trait: should include both a benefit and drawback.

Hot-Headed.name: Hot-Headed
Hot-Headed.description: You act quickly, burn brightly, and do not hesitate when insulted or threatened, but anger can outrun judgment.
Hot-Headed.traitNarrativeEffects: Your first answer to disrespect is usually louder than it needs to be.
Hot-Headed.tradeoff: Stronger force and intimidation; weaker restraint, patience, and diplomacy.
Hot-Headed.balanceRole: Quirk trait: should include both a benefit and drawback.

Bookish.name: Bookish
Bookish.description: You learn quickly, read deeply, and notice patterns in records or theory, but long study has left you less hardened by physical danger.
Bookish.traitNarrativeEffects: You collect notes even when the room is actively becoming unsafe.
Bookish.tradeoff: Better research and lore; weaker raw physical force.
Bookish.balanceRole: Quirk trait: should include both a benefit and drawback.

Frail but Agile.name: Frail but Agile
Frail but Agile.description: Your body is light, quick, and responsive, but it does not absorb punishment well.
Frail but Agile.traitNarrativeEffects: You move before others finish deciding, but you feel every hard landing afterward.
Frail but Agile.tradeoff: Much faster and more agile; significantly more fragile.
Frail but Agile.balanceRole: Quirk trait: should include both a benefit and drawback.

Stubborn.name: Stubborn
Stubborn.description: You are hard to move, hard to convince, and harder to break, but your certainty can make you slow to adapt.
Stubborn.traitNarrativeEffects: You can turn a reasonable suggestion into a siege if it is phrased badly.
Stubborn.tradeoff: More endurance and resolve; less flexible and socially yielding.
Stubborn.balanceRole: Quirk trait: should include both a benefit and drawback.

Charismatic Liar.name: Charismatic Liar
Charismatic Liar.description: You lie smoothly, read people well enough to sell a story, and know how to make falsehood feel useful, but truth and trust become fragile around you.
Charismatic Liar.traitNarrativeEffects: You sometimes improve an honest answer before remembering honesty was the point.
Charismatic Liar.tradeoff: Better deception and social performance; worse trustworthiness and grounded judgment.
Charismatic Liar.balanceRole: Quirk trait: should include both a benefit and drawback.

Dragon-Blooded.name: Dragon-Blooded
Dragon-Blooded.description: You possess the blood of dragons. This grants narrative access to Dracomancy when paired with valid draconic ancestry or explicit story permission: a high form of breath-magic that can express Fire, Ice, Lightning, Acid, Healing, pressure, and other draconic effects. Dracomancy spends Breathing, not mana; it draws external mana through draconic breath rather than consuming the caster's internal mana pool.
Dragon-Blooded.traitNarrativeEffects: Your veins occasionally glow faintly with draconic energy when you channel magic.

Private.name: Private
Private.description: A basic enlisted soldier. You follow orders and fight in the ranks.
Private.traitNarrativeEffects: You always address superiors with strict military respect.

Corporal.name: Corporal
Corporal.description: A junior non-commissioned officer. You lead small fire teams.
Corporal.traitNarrativeEffects: You are quick to take initiative when orders are unclear.

Sergeant.name: Sergeant
Sergeant.description: A veteran non-commissioned officer. You command squads with authority.
Sergeant.traitNarrativeEffects: Your voice carries across the battlefield.

Lieutenant.name: Lieutenant
Lieutenant.description: A junior officer. You lead platoons and make tactical decisions.
Lieutenant.traitNarrativeEffects: You are trained to remain calm under pressure.

Captain.name: Captain
Captain.description: A senior officer. You command companies and influence larger battles.
Captain.traitNarrativeEffects: Your presence inspires loyalty in your troops.

Major.name: Major
Major.description: A field-grade officer. You coordinate multiple companies.
Major.traitNarrativeEffects: You are respected by both enlisted and fellow officers.

Colonel.name: Colonel
Colonel.description: A senior field-grade officer. You command regiments.
Colonel.traitNarrativeEffects: Your strategic mind is feared by enemies.

General.name: General
General.description: The highest rank. You command entire armies and shape the course of wars.
General.traitNarrativeEffects: Your orders carry the weight of national strategy.

Initiate Blacksmith.name: Initiate Blacksmith
Initiate Blacksmith.description: You have only recently graduated from apprenticeship. Your hammer is steady but your eye is still learning.
Initiate Blacksmith.traitNarrativeEffects: You are eager to prove your worth through hard work.

Adept Blacksmith.name: Adept Blacksmith
Adept Blacksmith.description: You have moved beyond simple metals. You can reliably craft iron, steel, copper, and bronze without failure.
Adept Blacksmith.traitNarrativeEffects: You take great pride in the durability of your gear.

Artisan Blacksmith.name: Artisan Blacksmith
Artisan Blacksmith.description: Your work has moved from craft into art. You can work with precious metals and your pieces often carry minor enchantments.
Artisan Blacksmith.traitNarrativeEffects: You view every forged item as a potential masterpiece.

Masterwork Blacksmith.name: Masterwork Blacksmith
Masterwork Blacksmith.description: You are capable of masterwork forging: weapons, armor, tools, and art objects that are both beautiful and brutally functional. When crafting with rare metals or monster components, you may preserve and direct material bonuses with unusual precision. On a great success or better, you may suppress one minor material drawback. On a critical success, you may create a named item or convert one drawback into a controlled feature.
Masterwork Blacksmith.traitNarrativeEffects: You judge a weapon by balance, honesty, and purpose. Decoration is only acceptable when it serves the piece rather than weakening it.
unlockedBy: Requires Blacksmithing 15 or equivalent story training under a master smith.

Bladesinger Casting (Fire).name: Bladesinger Casting (Fire)
Bladesinger Casting (Fire).description: Legacy fire Bladesinger trait. Prefer Pyromantic Bladesinger for new characters. You have dedicated your life to the fire-weaving blade. Every strike becomes a spell. Fire expressed through Bladesinging costs no internal mana because the blade guides ambient fire-flow rather than drawing from blood mana.
