Bladesinger Casting (Fire).traitNarrativeEffects: Your blade occasionally flickers with inner flames.
Bladesinger Casting (Fire).manaCostRule: Fire effects performed through Bladesinging cost no internal mana. Non-fire ordinary spellcasting costs double.

Path of Diamond Mind.name: Path of Diamond Mind
Path of Diamond Mind.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of absolute mental clarity. The practitioner disciplines thought until fear, pain, and distraction become ripples on still water. Diamond Mind teaches that the sword is first restrained in the mind: one breath, one choice, one perfect moment of calm before action. You train the mind to cut sharper than any blade. Clarity in chaos, foresight in the strike.
Path of Diamond Mind.traitNarrativeEffects: Stillness before action; the mind becomes a blade held in perfect restraint. You often speak in philosophical terms about combat.
Path of Diamond Mind.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Practitioner of The Ninefold Path.name: Practitioner of The Ninefold Path
Practitioner of The Ninefold Path.description: You have begun to walk the Ninefold Path — a sacred fusion of martial discipline and mystic insight where combat itself becomes a fatal dance of will and precision.
Practitioner of The Ninefold Path.traitNarrativeEffects: Your movements carry an almost ritualistic grace.
unlockedBy: Path of Diamond Mind
Path of Shadow Hand
Path of Devoted Spirit
Path of Stone Dragon
Path of Iron Heart
Path of Desert Winds
Path of Tiger Claw
Path of Setting Sun
Path of White Raven

Master of the Ninefold.name: Master of the Ninefold
Master of the Ninefold.description: You have conquered all nine teachings of the Ninefold Path and understand their united sublime philosophy. Every movement you make is already aligned with the Paths. Every breath calms, every flourish eases the body, and every mantra recollects the mind. You can blend the expressions of the nine paths because no single path stands above another in your understanding.
Master of the Ninefold.traitNarrativeEffects: You naturally settle into path-appropriate rhythm without conscious effort. Your Ninefold Harmony, Sublime Recovery, and Path Blending are expressed through the trait's lore rather than separate ability entries.
unlockedBy: Unlocked only when the character possesses all nine path traits: Path of Diamond Mind, Path of Shadow Hand, Path of Devoted Spirit, Path of Stone Dragon, Path of Iron Heart, Path of Desert Winds, Path of Tiger Claw, Path of Setting Sun, and Path of White Raven.
Understand the truth of the Ninefold Lotus.
Demonstrate the ability to embody the unity of the nine paths in action, not merely know of it.

Councillor.name: Councillor
Councillor.description: A respected political advisor and diplomat, representing your nation's interests in the grand summit. Skilled in negotiation, statecraft, and the subtle art of alliance-building.
Councillor.traitNarrativeEffects: You speak with measured precision, always weighing the political implications of every word.

Senator.name: Senator
Senator.description: An elected representative of the Republic, wielding legislative power and the authority to speak for the people. Master of debate, law, and political maneuvering.
Senator.traitNarrativeEffects: You carry yourself with the confidence of one who has debated in the grand halls of the Republic and emerged victorious.

Path of Shadow Hand.name: Path of Shadow Hand
Path of Shadow Hand.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of misdirection, hidden motion, and the quiet hand. Shadow Hand is not cruelty or cowardice; it is the lesson that a warrior need not meet force directly when silence, angle, patience, and restraint can end violence before it blooms. You move where light cannot follow. The shadow is both cloak and gateway.
Path of Shadow Hand.traitNarrativeEffects: Moves by silence, angle, patience, and hidden motion rather than direct force. You prefer the spaces between light and darkness, where secrets hide.
Path of Shadow Hand.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Path of Devoted Spirit.name: Path of Devoted Spirit
Path of Devoted Spirit.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of conviction, mercy, and the protected oath. Devoted Spirit teaches that the strongest warrior is not the one who kills most readily, but the one who knows what must be protected and refuses to abandon it. You fight not for yourself, but for something greater. Devotion turns every wound into fuel.
Path of Devoted Spirit.traitNarrativeEffects: Conviction and mercy; protects what must not be abandoned. You speak of honor and sacrifice with genuine conviction.
Path of Devoted Spirit.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Path of Stone Dragon.name: Path of Stone Dragon
Path of Stone Dragon.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of rooted strength and enduring form. Stone Dragon teaches the body to become a mountain: patient, grounded, and impossible to move without consequence. Its lesson is that true strength is not frenzy, but the calm refusal to be broken. You become the mountain — unmovable and unbreakable.
Path of Stone Dragon.traitNarrativeEffects: Rooted and unbroken; strength expressed through calm endurance. You stand with unwavering patience, letting others reveal their weaknesses first.
Path of Stone Dragon.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Path of Iron Heart.name: Path of Iron Heart
Path of Iron Heart.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of disciplined resolve and perfect martial commitment. Iron Heart does not glorify violence; it teaches that once peaceful restraint has failed, the sword must act with clean purpose, without hesitation, vanity, or rage. You forge your will into living steel. No pain, no fear, no doubt can break you.
Path of Iron Heart.traitNarrativeEffects: Disciplined resolve; when restraint fails, action becomes clean and purposeful. You face every challenge with the same unyielding determination.
Path of Iron Heart.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Path of Desert Winds.name: Path of Desert Winds
Path of Desert Winds.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of flowing heat, swift motion, and relentless passage. Desert Wind teaches the practitioner to move like hot wind over sand: untouchable, precise, and gone before force can answer. You are the desert storm — swift, scorching, and impossible to pin down.
Path of Desert Winds.traitNarrativeEffects: Swift flowing motion; precise and gone before force can answer. You move with restless energy, always seeking the next horizon.
Path of Desert Winds.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Path of Tiger Claw.name: Path of Tiger Claw
Path of Tiger Claw.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of instinct, predatory motion, and decisive pressure. Tiger Claw teaches the practitioner to move with the sudden certainty of a hunting beast, while still remaining bound by restraint. It is ferocity mastered, not ferocity unleashed. You fight with the primal fury of the tiger. Rage, when honed, becomes perfect instinct.
Path of Tiger Claw.traitNarrativeEffects: Ferocity mastered; predatory movement without surrendering restraint. Your eyes flash with predatory focus when combat begins.
Path of Tiger Claw.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Path of Setting Sun.name: Path of Setting Sun
Path of Setting Sun.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of redirection, balance, and yielding force. Setting Sun teaches that power can be defeated by refusing to oppose it directly: turning momentum, guiding aggression aside, and letting violence collapse under its own weight. You master the art of the falling sun — turning your enemy's strength against them.
Path of Setting Sun.traitNarrativeEffects: Redirection and balance; turns aggression against its own momentum. You find opportunities where others see only obstacles.
Path of Setting Sun.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Path of White Raven.name: Path of White Raven
Path of White Raven.description: A Ninefold Path teaching of presence, command, and unity. White Raven teaches that a warrior's voice, posture, and conviction can steady allies and turn chaos into shared purpose. It is leadership through clarity rather than domination. You are the banner on the battlefield. The truest warrior elevates those who fight beside them.
Path of White Raven.traitNarrativeEffects: Presence and command; turns chaos into shared purpose. You instinctively position yourself to support your allies.
Path of White Raven.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Ninefold Path Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Draconic Bloodline.name: Draconic Bloodline
Draconic Bloodline.description: One of your parents was a true dragon. This makes you a half-dragon or half-breed in the eyes of many draconic cultures, carrying a direct and unmistakable connection to true dragon blood rather than Drakken ancestry. Your body, instincts, breath, and mana carry stronger true-dragon resonance than most peoples. This ancestry can bring awe, fear, prejudice, and heavy social consequences, especially in Drakona.
Draconic Bloodline.traitNarrativeEffects: Your draconic nature is difficult to hide when your temper, magic, or instincts flare. Your eyes, voice, breath, or bearing may briefly betray the true dragon blood behind you.
unlockedBy: Selected as Bloodline during character creation.
Draconic Bloodline.prejudiceWarning: Half-dragon or half-breed status may cause prejudice from other draconic races and Drakonan institutions.

Sylvera Bloodline.name: Sylvera Bloodline
Sylvera Bloodline.description: You carry Sylveran elven ancestry, giving you a strong natural affinity for magic, stealth, nature, and the living memory of old forests. Mana comes to you easily, and the rhythms of root, shadow, and leaf feel familiar rather than strange.
Sylvera Bloodline.traitNarrativeEffects: Your ears have a slight point, and you move with unnatural grace.

Beastkin Bloodline.name: Beastkin Bloodline
Beastkin Bloodline.description: You carry primal beast blood, tying your body and instincts to animal heritage. Your strength comes through sense, instinct, movement, and the ancestral animal chosen elsewhere in character creation.
Beastkin Bloodline.traitNarrativeEffects: Your eyes reflect light like a predator's, and you move with animal grace.

Crownsworn Bloodline.name: Crownsworn Bloodline
Crownsworn Bloodline.description: You carry the blood and sacred traditions of the Kingdom of the Holy Crown. Duty, protection, persuasion, faith, and chivalric bearing come naturally to you, whether you embrace those expectations or struggle beneath them.
Crownsworn Bloodline.traitNarrativeEffects: You carry yourself with quiet dignity, and your presence brings comfort to the faithful. A ribbon from a citizen often adorns your armor.

Valenian Bloodline.name: Valenian Bloodline
Valenian Bloodline.description: You carry the blood of the industrial Republic of Valen, granting a sharp mind and natural affinity for invention, gunpowder, law, clockwork, medicine, and practical engineering. Valenian humans have no personal mana and cannot cast normal spells, but they can excel through tools, tactics, alchemy, technology, and stamina-based Sublime Arts.
Valenian Bloodline.traitNarrativeEffects: You think in mechanisms, equations, and disciplined technique — not spells and incantations. True power comes from within, not from borrowed mana.

Animal Kin.name: Animal Kin
Animal Kin.description: You have a natural affinity with animals. You gain +3 to all checks involving animals (calming, training, understanding behavior).
Animal Kin.traitNarrativeEffects: Animals seem drawn to you, and even aggressive beasts often calm in your presence.

Arcane Affinity.name: Arcane Affinity
Arcane Affinity.description: You have a natural connection to magic. You gain +3 to Arcane Aptitude and +2 to all magic-related skill checks.
Arcane Affinity.traitNarrativeEffects: Magic flows through you like a second heartbeat. You can feel when spells are being cast nearby.

Animal Communication.name: Animal Communication
Animal Communication.description: You can communicate with animals of your ancestral type (wolves for wolf Worcen, bears for bear Worcen, etc.). You understand their calls, body language, and can convey simple messages.
Animal Communication.traitNarrativeEffects: You often find yourself having one-sided conversations with animals, and they seem to understand you.

Ancestral Shift.name: Ancestral Shift
Ancestral Shift.description: You can shift into your ancestral animal form (wolf, bear, eagle, etc.) and remain in it permanently if you choose. In this form, you gain enhanced physical abilities, natural weapons, and can move freely in natural environments. Shifting takes 1 minute, but you may remain in your shifted form indefinitely — like a dragon maintaining their true form. You are this world's druid: a guardian of nature who walks between the civilized and wild worlds.
Ancestral Shift.traitNarrativeEffects: When you shift, your eyes glow with primal energy and your voice becomes more animalistic. You can remain in your animal form for days, weeks, or even permanently if you desire. The transformation is both beautiful and terrifying to witness.

Human Versatility.name: Human Versatility
Human Versatility.description: You are exceptionally adaptable. You gain +2 to all skill checks, representing your ability to quickly learn and excel in any situation.
Human Versatility.traitNarrativeEffects: You have a knack for picking up new skills and adapting to new environments faster than anyone else.

Wolf.name: Wolf
Wolf.description: Your ancestral animal is the wolf: a pack hunter with keen senses, endurance, and fierce loyalty. Beastkin usually show wolf ears, a bushy tail, fangs, or gray-brown fur. Worcen with this ancestry are powerful bipedal wolf-folk with full muzzles, dense fur, and predatory posture.
Wolf.traitNarrativeEffects: You have a natural pack instinct — you feel most comfortable in groups and often find yourself taking protective positions around allies.

Bear.name: Bear
Bear.description: Your ancestral animal is the bear: a powerful guardian of immense strength, endurance, and stubborn courage. Beastkin usually show rounded bear ears, heavy build, short tail, or thick fur patches. Worcen with this ancestry are broad, heavy bipedal bear-folk with massive claws and dense fur.
Bear.traitNarrativeEffects: You have a protective, nurturing nature — you often find yourself standing between danger and those you care about.

Eagle.name: Eagle
Eagle.description: Your ancestral animal is the eagle: a sharp-eyed aerial hunter associated with vision, pride, and height. Beastkin usually show feathered features, keen eyes, and expressive wings or feather patterns. Worcen with this ancestry are bipedal eagle-folk with hooked beaks, taloned feet, and commanding presence.
Eagle.traitNarrativeEffects: You have a piercing gaze that seems to see through lies and deception. You often find yourself scanning the horizon even indoors.

Fox.name: Fox
Fox.description: Your ancestral animal is the fox: a quick, clever survivor known for cunning, agility, and misdirection. Beastkin usually show fox ears, a fluffy tail, red or silver fur, and bright eyes. Worcen with this ancestry are lean bipedal fox-folk with pointed muzzles, swift movement, and sly bearing.
Fox.traitNarrativeEffects: You have a mischievous streak and love wordplay, riddles, and clever schemes. Your tail twitches when you're plotting something.

Panther.name: Panther
Panther.description: Your ancestral animal is the panther: a silent predator of grace, strength, and ambush. Beastkin usually show panther ears, a long tail, sleek dark fur, and quiet steps. Worcen with this ancestry are bipedal panther-folk with powerful muscles, midnight fur, and predatory poise.
Panther.traitNarrativeEffects: You move with predatory grace and prefer to observe from the shadows before acting. Your eyes glow faintly in the dark.

Owl.name: Owl
Owl.description: Your ancestral animal is the owl: a nocturnal hunter tied to patience, silence, and watchful wisdom. Beastkin usually show large owl-like eyes, feathered patches, and quiet movement. Worcen with this ancestry are bipedal owl-folk with feathered bodies, broad eyes, and almost soundless steps.
Owl.traitNarrativeEffects: You see things others miss and often speak in riddles or metaphors. Your head tilts slightly when you're thinking deeply.

Stag.name: Stag
Stag.description: Your ancestral animal is the stag: a forest guardian associated with endurance, majesty, and swift movement through wild places. Beastkin usually show deer ears, antlers, or brown fur patches. Worcen with this ancestry are bipedal stag-folk with strong legs, antlers, and dignified bearing.
Stag.traitNarrativeEffects: You carry yourself with quiet dignity and often find yourself drawn to ancient forests and sacred groves.

Raven.name: Raven
Raven.description: Your ancestral animal is the raven: an intelligent scavenger and omen-bird associated with memory, cleverness, and watchful mystery. Beastkin usually show dark feathering, sharp eyes, or raven-like features. Worcen with this ancestry are bipedal raven-folk with glossy feathers, keen eyes, and quick, curious minds.
Raven.traitNarrativeEffects: You have a fascination with shiny objects and often collect trinkets. You speak in riddles and seem to know things before they happen.

N/A.name: N/A
N/A.description: You do not have an ancestral animal. This option is intended for characters without Beastkin or Worcen-style animal heritage.
N/A.traitNarrativeEffects: You lack any animal heritage or features. You are fully humanoid without any bestial traits.

Blacksmith Masterworker.name: Blacksmith Masterworker
Blacksmith Masterworker.description: Silas's crafts are always considered masterworks, especially when working with special metals, rare alloys, or monster components. His deep understanding of scavenging and materials allows him to create equipment of legendary quality.

Master of The Mundane (Updated).name: Master of The Mundane (Updated)
Master of The Mundane (Updated).description: As a master blacksmith, Silas has absolute mastery over basic and precious metals. He has no failure chance when working known materials and can gain bonuses to crafting and repairing items made from known materials. New planar or impossible materials must be mastered through dedicated work.

Former Glaive Training Advisor.name: Former Glaive Training Advisor
Former Glaive Training Advisor.description: A former advisor to the Holy Crown's Glaives specializing in training the 115th Pyro-Knight Regiment. Silas knows Crown military protocols and earns respect or scrutiny from elite Glaive units.

Master Weaver's Associate.name: Master Weaver's Associate
Master Weaver's Associate.description: Established a professional partnership with Master Weaver in Wheatwall. Grants priority access to high-end textile commissions and a labor discount for garment crafting or armor integration within her network.

Dragonbreaker Training.name: Dragonbreaker Training
Dragonbreaker Training.description: Advanced Dragoon/Slayer anti-dragon combat training. You understand true dragon anatomy, flight posture, scale overlap, wing strain, and how to strike without wasting force on armored plates. You deal +25% damage against true dragons, false-wyrms, and dragon-scale creatures when using appropriate weapons, magic, or tactics. You also gain +5 to checks to identify dragon weak points, avoid a dragon's reach, resist intimidation from dragon-scale enemies, and recognize when a dragon is holding back rather than trying to kill you. This trait is rare and politically sensitive in Drakona; using it openly against a true dragon may be treated as attempted murder, treason, or an act of war depending on context.
Dragonbreaker Training.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively watch wing joints, throat plates, tail balance, and scale seams. Around dragons, this can make you look either highly trained or dangerously predatory.
unlockedBy: Requires draconic combat level 18 or equivalent advanced anti-dragon training from a Dragoon, Slayer master, or Drakonan instructor.

Dragoon Restraint.name: Dragoon Restraint
Dragoon Restraint.description: A humane Dragoon technique focused on subduing dragon-scale opponents without killing them. You gain +5 to attempts to pin, redirect, disarm, ground, bind, calm, or force surrender from true dragons, bonded dragons, false-wyrms, and dragon-scale creatures. Against true dragons, this is socially safer than lethal Dragonbreaker methods because it shows discipline and intent to preserve life.
Dragoon Restraint.traitNarrativeEffects: You automatically look for ways to end a dragon-scale fight without death: grounding the wings, controlling the tail, binding the jaws, breaking line of movement, or forcing a yield.
unlockedBy: Requires draconic combat level 12 or equivalent Dragoon subdual training focused on grounding, pinning, and nonlethal control.

Material Savant.name: Material Savant
Material Savant.description: You can identify the likely hidden properties of metals, monster parts, woods, crystals, and relic fragments before crafting. You gain +3 to checks involving material identification, compatibility, preservation, and choosing the best item type for a rare material.
Material Savant.traitNarrativeEffects: You handle materials like living witnesses, turning them under light, tapping them, smelling them under heat, and listening for flaws.
unlockedBy: Requires Blacksmithing 10 or repeated successful crafting with rare materials.

Beast Handler.name: Beast Handler
Beast Handler.description: You have experience handling tameable animals, mounts, hunting beasts, and work creatures. You gain +3 to Tamer checks involving Common or Restricted creatures.
Beast Handler.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively watch ears, tails, wings, posture, and breathing before approaching any creature.
unlockedBy: May be selected by characters with animal-handling, stable, falconry, ranch, Beastkin, Slayer, or wilderness backgrounds.

Exotic Beast Trainer.name: Exotic Beast Trainer
Exotic Beast Trainer.description: You are trained to handle dangerous tameable creatures such as gryphons, aerial mounts, war hounds, pack reptiles, young wyverns, and certain magical beasts. You gain +3 to Tamer checks involving Restricted or Dangerous tameable creatures. This trait does not allow pseudo-drakes, true dragons, Daemons, Shaydes, or sentient people to be pets.
Exotic Beast Trainer.traitNarrativeEffects: You judge danger by whether the creature is afraid, hungry, territorial, or curious, not only by how frightening it looks.
unlockedBy: Requires Tamer 10 or story training with exotic mounts, monster handlers, Slayers, aeries, or beast sanctuaries.

The Second Step.name: The Second Step
The Second Step.description: You have uncovered the hidden truth of magic: mana has no element. Mana is mana. Fire, water, earth, wind, lightning, frost, and other elements are imagined structures placed on a singular essence. Combined with the First Step, this truth opens the path toward becoming an unstoppable master of the arcane.
The Second Step.traitNarrativeEffects: You can no longer fully believe in elemental mana. When others speak as if fire mana and water mana are different substances, you hear the useful lie beneath the lesson.
unlockedBy: Player must actively state some form of the realization that mana has no element.
Valid unlock phrases include: 'mana has no element', 'mana is mana', 'there is only mana', 'elemental mana does not exist', or equivalent wording.
This unlock represents the Second Step of Magi-Knight arcane mastery and should not be handed out for passive exposure alone.

The First Step.name: The First Step
The First Step.description: Your body feels different. Your blood flows faster, your sight feels fresher, your magic casts faster, and mana flows through you like water. Combined with the Second Step, this awakening opens the path toward becoming the ultimate arcanist.
The First Step.traitNarrativeEffects: After surviving collapse, death, or mana overdraw, you feel mana moving through your blood as if something in you has been charged open.
unlockedBy: Unlock if the player dies and is revived.
Unlock if the player reaches 0 mana while their HP is below 50%.
Unlock if the story clearly shows near-death life-force overdraw, mana collapse, or extreme bodily strain equivalent to the Magi-Knight First Step.

Cardinalis.name: Cardinalis
Cardinalis.description: Mana has no form and no element. There is only the breath of life in the air and the flow in your veins. Elements are fundamentals, but you are advanced. All elements are attuned to you, and you can weave them together for devastating results. Your imagination is now truly your only limit.
Cardinalis.traitNarrativeEffects: You no longer feel elemental walls. Fire, water, earth, wind, frost, lightning, light, and all other forms feel like different handshapes made from the same living current.
unlockedBy: Unlocked only when the player has both The First Step and The Second Step.
When Cardinalis unlocks, remove or replace The First Step and The Second Step so Cardinalis becomes the active completed trait.

Pyromantic Bladesinger.name: Pyromantic Bladesinger
Pyromantic Bladesinger.description: Pyromantic Bladesingers guide fire-mana through aggressive, flowing blade arcs. Their style favors sweeping attacks, rising pressure, ember trails, and relentless forward rhythm. They do not simply burn enemies; they coax fire into following the hunger of the dance.
Pyromantic Bladesinger.traitNarrativeEffects: Your blades sometimes spark or trail heat shimmer when your footwork falls into rhythm.
Pyromantic Bladesinger.manaCostRule: Fire effects performed through Bladesinging cost no internal mana. The Bladesinger Rhythm ability uses stamina to guide ambient airborne mana through movement and weapon rhythm. Non-Fire ordinary spellcasting still costs double.
Pyromantic Bladesinger.attackStyle: Aggressive, flowing, sweeping cuts that build flame pressure through long arcs and forward rhythm.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Pyromantic Bladesinger.vocationRole: Elemental duelist, fire-flow blade attacks, freeform Bladesinging rhythm, no internal mana cost for Fire Bladesinging.

vocationPerk.name: Fire-Flow Mana Sight
vocationPerk.description: You can perceive nearby airborne fire-mana as ember-streams, heat shimmer, sparks, and rising orange-red ribbons. While focused, gain +5 to Pyromantic Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds and can follow fire-mana flow in the air.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character notice and follow fire-aspected airborne mana-flow. Apply +5 to appropriate Pyromantic Bladesinger Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds when focused.

manaSight.name: Fire-Flow Mana Sight
manaSight.description: You can perceive nearby airborne fire-mana as ember-streams, heat shimmer, sparks, and rising orange-red ribbons. While focused, gain +5 to Pyromantic Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds and can follow fire-mana flow in the air.
manaSight.usageInstructions: Mana Sight is treated as trained Bladesinger perception inside the vocation trait, not a separate starting ability. It helps the narrator describe visible elemental mana-flow and supports Bladesinging setup.

Hydromantic Bladesinger.name: Hydromantic Bladesinger
Hydromantic Bladesinger.description: Hydromantic Bladesingers guide water-mana through smooth, deceptive, continuous motion. Their style favors flowing steps, defensive redirection, sudden reversals, mist-veiled movement, and wave-like impacts.
Hydromantic Bladesinger.traitNarrativeEffects: Mist sometimes gathers along your blade edge when your breathing settles into a smooth rhythm.
Hydromantic Bladesinger.manaCostRule: Water effects performed through Bladesinging cost no internal mana. The Bladesinger Rhythm ability uses stamina to guide ambient airborne mana through movement and weapon rhythm. Non-Water ordinary spellcasting still costs double.
Hydromantic Bladesinger.attackStyle: Fluid, deceptive, continuous motion that redirects pressure before crashing back like a tide.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Hydromantic Bladesinger.vocationRole: Elemental duelist, water-flow blade attacks, freeform Bladesinging rhythm, no internal mana cost for Water Bladesinging.

vocationPerk.name: Water-Flow Mana Sight
vocationPerk.description: You can perceive nearby airborne water-mana as curling mist, vapor ribbons, dew-bright threads, and blue-white flowing streams. While focused, gain +5 to Hydromantic Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds and can follow water-mana flow in the air.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character notice and follow water-aspected airborne mana-flow. Apply +5 to appropriate Hydromantic Bladesinger Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds when focused.

manaSight.name: Water-Flow Mana Sight
manaSight.description: You can perceive nearby airborne water-mana as curling mist, vapor ribbons, dew-bright threads, and blue-white flowing streams. While focused, gain +5 to Hydromantic Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds and can follow water-mana flow in the air.
manaSight.usageInstructions: Mana Sight is treated as trained Bladesinger perception inside the vocation trait, not a separate starting ability. It helps the narrator describe visible elemental mana-flow and supports Bladesinging setup.

Aeromantic Bladesinger.name: Aeromantic Bladesinger
Aeromantic Bladesinger.description: Aeromantic Bladesingers guide wind-mana through quick, chaotic, evasive motion. Their style favors sudden angles, rapid cuts, delayed pressure, gust-like repositioning, and attacks that seem to strike twice.
Aeromantic Bladesinger.traitNarrativeEffects: Loose cloth, dust, and hair stir around your dagger hands even in still air.
Aeromantic Bladesinger.manaCostRule: Wind effects performed through Bladesinging cost no internal mana. The Bladesinger Rhythm ability uses stamina to guide ambient airborne mana through movement and weapon rhythm. Non-Wind ordinary spellcasting still costs double.
Aeromantic Bladesinger.attackStyle: Fast, evasive, chaotic footwork with rapid cuts and delayed wind-follow strikes.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Aeromantic Bladesinger.vocationRole: Elemental duelist, wind-flow blade attacks, freeform Bladesinging rhythm, no internal mana cost for Wind Bladesinging.

vocationPerk.name: Wind-Flow Mana Sight
vocationPerk.description: You can perceive nearby airborne wind-mana as pale currents, pressure lines, invisible eddies, and thin silver-green ribbons. While focused, gain +5 to Aeromantic Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds and can follow wind-mana flow in the air.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character notice and follow wind-aspected airborne mana-flow. Apply +5 to appropriate Aeromantic Bladesinger Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds when focused.

manaSight.name: Wind-Flow Mana Sight
manaSight.description: You can perceive nearby airborne wind-mana as pale currents, pressure lines, invisible eddies, and thin silver-green ribbons. While focused, gain +5 to Aeromantic Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds and can follow wind-mana flow in the air.
manaSight.usageInstructions: Mana Sight is treated as trained Bladesinger perception inside the vocation trait, not a separate starting ability. It helps the narrator describe visible elemental mana-flow and supports Bladesinging setup.

Fulmantic Bladesinger.name: Fulmantic Bladesinger
Fulmantic Bladesinger.description: Fulmantic Bladesingers guide lightning-mana through fluid steps broken by sharp, jagged thrusts. Their style favors piercing lines, sudden bursts, sensory disruption, flash-feints, and thunderous pressure.
Fulmantic Bladesinger.traitNarrativeEffects: Your blade sometimes clicks with tiny thunder-cracks when drawn too sharply.
Fulmantic Bladesinger.manaCostRule: Lightning effects performed through Bladesinging cost no internal mana. The Bladesinger Rhythm ability uses stamina to guide ambient airborne mana through movement and weapon rhythm. Non-Lightning ordinary spellcasting still costs double.
Fulmantic Bladesinger.attackStyle: Fluid steps broken by jagged thrusts, flash-feints, sonic cracks, and piercing lightning lines.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Fulmantic Bladesinger.vocationRole: Elemental duelist, lightning-flow blade attacks, freeform Bladesinging rhythm, no internal mana cost for Lightning Bladesinging.

vocationPerk.name: Storm-Flow Mana Sight
vocationPerk.description: You can perceive nearby airborne lightning-mana as jagged violet-white arcs, branching threads, static halos, and sudden snapping paths. While focused, gain +5 to Fulmantic Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds and can follow lightning-mana flow in the air.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character notice and follow lightning-aspected airborne mana-flow. Apply +5 to appropriate Fulmantic Bladesinger Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds when focused.

manaSight.name: Storm-Flow Mana Sight
manaSight.description: You can perceive nearby airborne lightning-mana as jagged violet-white arcs, branching threads, static halos, and sudden snapping paths. While focused, gain +5 to Fulmantic Bladesinging checks for up to 10 rounds and can follow lightning-mana flow in the air.
manaSight.usageInstructions: Mana Sight is treated as trained Bladesinger perception inside the vocation trait, not a separate starting ability. It helps the narrator describe visible elemental mana-flow and supports Bladesinging setup.

Arcanist.name: Arcanist
Arcanist.description: You are trained in arcane spellcasting, mana control, spell theory, and magical experimentation. You possess natural casting talent, but refine that gift through study, notes, practice, and controlled spell design. You can cast through instinct when pressed, but your best magic comes from understanding how mana behaves and shaping it with will, imagination, and discipline.
Arcanist.traitNarrativeEffects: You tend to describe problems in terms of structure, flow, control, and consequence, and your spellbook is never truly finished.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Arcanist.vocationRole: Active spellcaster, mana control specialist, spell theorist, and flexible arcane problem-solver.

vocationPerk.name: Spell Sense
vocationPerk.description: You can recognize common spell structures, active mana residue, signs of overcasting, and whether a magical effect feels improvised, formal, unstable, runic, elemental, or dangerous.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character identify practical magical structure and risk without revealing hidden lore beyond the evidence.

Mercenary.name: Mercenary
Mercenary.description: Mercenaries are professional soldiers-for-hire who thrive in the uneasy peace between Balen's nations. When rulers, nobles, guilds, merchants, or border lords need force without officially sending an army, they pay mercenaries. Some are respectable caravan guards, expedition soldiers, sentries, monster-route escorts, and private security. Others are deniable blades hired for border disputes, sabotage, proxy skirmishes, and conflicts no nation wants the Magi-Knights to call a breach of the peace. A Mercenary knows how to fight, how to read a contract, how to survive a bad employer, and how to leave a battlefield alive enough to collect payment.
Mercenary.traitNarrativeEffects: You judge rooms by exits, employers by their hands, contracts by what the pay fails to mention, and battlefields by whether the job is worth dying for.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Mercenary.vocationRole: Paid combat, caravan defense, expedition security, proxy conflict, battlefield pragmatism, contract survival, and professional small-unit fighting.

vocationPerk.name: Contract Instinct
vocationPerk.description: You know how to judge risk, pay, ambush potential, employer honesty, unlawful proxy work, suspiciously vague targets, and jobs likely to attract Magi-Knight scrutiny.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Give grounded warnings about bad deals, poor tactics, suspicious employers, likely betrayal, underpriced work, proxy-war danger, or private violence that may breach the peace if exposed.

Slayer.name: Slayer
Slayer.description: Slayers are certified monster hunters, trackers, harvesters, and field investigators licensed through the Slayer Guild. They take contracts against dangerous beasts, false-wyrms, monsters, rogue magical creatures, and ecological threats across Balen. A Slayer is not just a killer: they identify tracks, read wounds, follow signs, understand monster behavior, harvest valuable materials cleanly, and follow guild law. Reckless slaughter, poaching, killing protected species, or harming true dragons can bring Red-Guard audits, guild punishment, or execution.
Slayer.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively check tracks, wind, wounds, claw marks, feeding signs, harvest value, legality, and guild procedure before trusting a monster report.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Slayer.vocationRole: Certified monster contracts, tracking, monster identification, legal harvesting, and field tactics.

vocationPerk.name: Monster Work
vocationPerk.description: You understand Slayer contracts, harvestable parts, monster signs, bounty procedure, protected-species concerns, Red-Guard oversight, and practical field preparation.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character estimate danger, identify useful remains, understand Slayer Guild expectations, and notice when a contract may be illegal, underreported, or suspicious.

Knight.name: Knight
Knight.description: Knights are trained armored warriors bound by oath, rank, house, order, or formal martial discipline. They are recognized combatants expected to hold lines, defend allies, command respect, and act with discipline under pressure. Their strength is trained battlefield control, armor discipline, shield work, and the authority to stand between danger and those under their protection.
Knight.traitNarrativeEffects: You are used to being judged not only by how you fight, but by whether your conduct honors your oath, title, house, order, or chosen duty.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Knight.vocationRole: Oathbound armored combat, defensive battlefield control, formal challenge, shield discipline, military courtesy, and recognized station.

vocationPerk.name: Recognized Station
vocationPerk.description: You are socially recognized as a knight where your status, oath, house, order, or reputation applies.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Guards, nobles, soldiers, clergy, commoners, and rival warriors may treat the character with respect, scrutiny, expectation, formal courtesy, challenge, or legal attention depending on region and conduct.

Scout.name: Scout
Scout.description: Scouts are advance eyes and quiet feet: trailfinders, outriders, infiltrators, military observers, caravan pathfinders, and expedition lookouts. They move ahead of the main group, identify threats before contact, mark safe routes, spot ambushes, and return with information others would have died trying to learn. A Scout is not simply a thief, hunter, or soldier. Their greatest weapon is knowing what waits ahead before anyone else does.
Scout.traitNarrativeEffects: You count sightlines, cover, patrol gaps, and escape routes the way others count steps.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Scout.vocationRole: Reconnaissance, mundane stealth, pathfinding, ambush detection, route marking, infiltration support, and emergency wilderness survival.

vocationPerk.name: Avoidance
vocationPerk.description: You can escape detection in areas with cover, broken sightlines, crowds, brush, ruins, darkness, smoke, clutter, or uneven terrain. This is mundane stealth, not shadow magic.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: When cover or broken sightlines exist, give Scouts strong chances to vanish from attention, reposition, scout ahead, or escape pursuit through timing and terrain.

Runescribe.name: Runescribe
Runescribe.description: Runescribes are tattooed ritual arcanists who write magic into flesh, air, stone, parchment, and prepared surfaces. Many mark their own bodies with runes and magic circles, turning skin into a living grimoire and using touch, gesture, spoken sigils, and prepared circles to release controlled arcane effects. A Runescribe does not fight like an armored warrior. They fight like a ritualist: touching enemies to brand runes, tracing circles into the air, preparing trap seals, breaking hostile inscriptions, and relying on protective body-runes instead of armor.
Runescribe.traitNarrativeEffects: You read skin, stone, air, doorways, floors, and weapon edges as possible ritual surfaces, and your fingers often trace invisible circle-lines while you think.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Runescribe.vocationRole: Tattooed ritual arcanist, touch-brand caster, air-circle tracer, magic-circle trapper, warder, and written-magic specialist.

vocationPerk.name: Living Grimoire
vocationPerk.description: Your body, hands, breath, ink, and gestures are part of your ritual practice. You can use tattooed runes, spoken sigils, and prepared circles to shape magic without wearing armor or relying on a weapon as your primary focus.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character inspect, prepare, trigger, disrupt, or improvise runes and circles. Treat them as a ritualist who uses touch and prepared geometry, not as a Magi-Knight, Bladesinger, or armored martial caster.

Healer.name: Healer
Healer.description: Healers are battlefield medics, apothecaries, surgeons, and triage specialists trained to keep the living alive when magic alone is not enough. They use anatomy, bandages, splints, poultices, stitches, vials, potions, and calm hands under pressure. Some Healers know minor arcane stabilization, and they practice quiet stamina-based pressure-control techniques to keep their hands steady in chaos, but their true strength is practical survival: knowing what the body needs after the wound is closed.
Healer.traitNarrativeEffects: You look at how someone stands, breathes, and grips a wound before you listen to their claim that they are fine.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Healer.vocationRole: Practical medicine, alchemical treatment, battlefield triage, emergency stabilization, injury reading, recovery care, minor arcane suturing, and steady-handed treatment under pressure.

vocationPerk.name: Trained Healer
vocationPerk.description: You are trained in practical medicine, battlefield triage, alchemical treatment, potion use, wound care, and recovery. You can stop bleeding, splint bones, stitch wounds, identify shock, treat poison, manage infection risk, and keep someone alive long enough for real recovery.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character identify injury severity, stabilize wounded creatures, use medical tools effectively, administer potions properly, and recognize when magical wound closure is not enough. Healer techniques may use tools, supplies, mana, or stamina depending on the action.

Tamer.name: Tamer
Tamer.description: Tamers are creature handlers, trappers, trainers, and field companions who understand how tameable beasts and monsters think. They know how to calm frightened animals, set humane snares, read signs of aggression, manage food and scent, train commands, and build trust with creatures others would only fear or kill. A Tamer does not begin with a guaranteed companion; they begin with the knowledge, tools, and patience needed to capture, calm, feed, train, and eventually bond with tameable creatures.
Tamer.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively soften your posture around frightened creatures and read ears, wings, tails, breath, eyes, scent behavior, and distance before you speak.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Tamer.vocationRole: Creature handling, humane trapping, taming attempts, command training, reading temperament, and temporary field cooperation with nearby tameable creatures.

vocationPerk.name: Trapper-Handler
vocationPerk.description: You can read tameable creature behavior, set humane snares, approach frightened animals without provoking them, and begin the slow work of bonding or training without needing a guaranteed starting companion.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Give better options during taming, humane capture, creature calming, feed/scent management, command training, and temporary creature-aid scenes.

Scholar.name: Scholar
Scholar.description: Scholars are trained researchers, archivists, historians, translators, theorists, and field academics. They survive by knowing what others overlook: old languages, political records, religious doctrine, magical theory, ruin markings, ancient symbols, and the difference between rumor and evidence. A Scholar is not automatically a mage; their strength is disciplined knowledge, asking the right question, checking the right source, and recognizing when a story does not match the facts.
Scholar.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively search for the source of a claim before trusting the claim itself, and you notice when a story's details do not agree with each other.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Scholar.vocationRole: Research, archives, lore, translation, evidence review, contradiction-spotting, records, academic survival, and structured reasoning.

vocationPerk.name: Disciplined Knowledge
vocationPerk.description: You can recall, research, verify, translate, compare, and contextualize information from archives, histories, maps, old languages, magical theory, legal records, religious doctrine, ruins, and political documents.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character identify useful context, likely sources, contradictions, false assumptions, translation leads, and what evidence would be needed to prove a claim. Do not reveal hidden canon or secret truths without earned evidence.

Drakken Bloodline.name: Drakken Bloodline
Drakken Bloodline.description: Your ancestry traces to the Drakken humanoid people. You are not the child of a true dragon and do not possess true dragon blood; your lineage carries Drakken traits, breath affinity, scale-marked heritage, Drakonan culture, or mixed family ties to Drakken communities.
Drakken Bloodline.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively recognize old Drakonan symbols, military customs, and the body language of dragon-blooded warriors.
unlockedBy: Selected as Bloodline during character creation.

Drive: Redemption.name: Drive: Redemption
Drive: Redemption.description: You are trying to atone for a failure, sin, betrayal, cowardice, or mistake that still follows you.
Drive: Redemption.traitNarrativeEffects: You hesitate before judging others too quickly.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Redemption.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Discovery.name: Drive: Discovery
Drive: Discovery.description: You are driven to uncover what is hidden: ruins, maps, species, lost magic, forbidden lands, or truths no one else has reached.
Drive: Discovery.traitNarrativeEffects: You struggle to leave an unanswered question alone.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Discovery.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Duty.name: Drive: Duty
Drive: Duty.description: You move forward because something is expected of you: an oath, title, family, order, commander, deity, or promise.
Drive: Duty.traitNarrativeEffects: You measure choices against obligation before comfort.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Duty.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Freedom.name: Drive: Freedom
Drive: Freedom.description: You seek to live without chains, masters, cages, contracts, inherited roles, or someone else's definition of your life.
Drive: Freedom.traitNarrativeEffects: You bristle when someone assumes they have authority over you.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Freedom.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Mastery.name: Drive: Mastery
Drive: Mastery.description: You seek excellence in a craft, weapon, magic system, philosophy, trade, or personal discipline.
Drive: Mastery.traitNarrativeEffects: You notice imperfections in your own work before anyone else does.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Mastery.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Protection.name: Drive: Protection
Drive: Protection.description: You are driven to shield someone, someplace, or something from harm.
Drive: Protection.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively place yourself between danger and those who cannot endure it.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Protection.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Wealth.name: Drive: Wealth
Drive: Wealth.description: You want coin, marks, leverage, security, comfort, or enough resources that no one can control you through need again.
Drive: Wealth.traitNarrativeEffects: You mentally estimate the value of favors, tools, and risks.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Wealth.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Glory.name: Drive: Glory
Drive: Glory.description: You want your name remembered, your deeds sung, or your strength recognized.
Drive: Glory.traitNarrativeEffects: You have trouble ignoring an audience.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Glory.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Revenge.name: Drive: Revenge
Drive: Revenge.description: Someone or something took too much from you, and part of you still walks toward the day you answer it.
Drive: Revenge.traitNarrativeEffects: Your calm becomes sharp when the old wound is named.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Revenge.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Forbidden Knowledge.name: Drive: Forbidden Knowledge
Drive: Forbidden Knowledge.description: You are drawn to secrets that wiser people lock away: occult relics, sealed archives, hidden magic, lost histories, or dangerous truths.
Drive: Forbidden Knowledge.traitNarrativeEffects: You know exactly when a warning sign is trying to become an invitation.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Forbidden Knowledge.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Drive: Proving Worth.name: Drive: Proving Worth
Drive: Proving Worth.description: You are trying to prove that you deserve respect, inheritance, love, title, mastery, or the right to define yourself.
Drive: Proving Worth.traitNarrativeEffects: Insults about your place in the world linger longer than wounds.
unlockedBy: Selected as Personal Drive during character creation.
Drive: Proving Worth.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Commoner.name: Standing: Commoner
Standing: Commoner.description: You come from ordinary working people. Laborers, farmers, tavern keepers, dockhands, shopkeepers, and villagers tend to recognize you as one of their own.
Standing: Commoner.traitNarrativeEffects: You are more comfortable eating at a busy kitchen table than being seated in a noble hall.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Commoner.narratorUse: Common folk may be more candid around you, while nobles may underestimate you.
Standing: Commoner.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Noble-Born.name: Standing: Noble-Born
Standing: Noble-Born.description: You were born into rank, title, land, inheritance, court culture, or recognized blood. This grants access and scrutiny in equal measure.
Standing: Noble-Born.traitNarrativeEffects: You notice breaches of etiquette even when you pretend not to care.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Noble-Born.narratorUse: Nobles and officials may grant audience or courtesy, but rivals may expect political behavior.
Standing: Noble-Born.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Disgraced Noble.name: Standing: Disgraced Noble
Standing: Disgraced Noble.description: You come from nobility, but scandal, exile, debt, crime, inheritance dispute, or public failure has stained your name.
Standing: Disgraced Noble.traitNarrativeEffects: You hear old titles like an insult even when spoken politely.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Disgraced Noble.narratorUse: Courtly circles may recognize the name, but reactions range from pity and gossip to hostility.
Standing: Disgraced Noble.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Guild-Bound.name: Standing: Guild-Bound
Standing: Guild-Bound.description: You are tied to a guild, company, contract hall, trade house, Slayer lodge, artisan order, or professional network.
Standing: Guild-Bound.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively look for the local guild mark before trusting a town's work.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Guild-Bound.narratorUse: Guild contacts may offer work, shelter, rumors, or demands.
Standing: Guild-Bound.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Temple-Raised.name: Standing: Temple-Raised
Standing: Temple-Raised.description: You were raised by, educated through, or sheltered within a temple, shrine, monastery, or religious institution.
Standing: Temple-Raised.traitNarrativeEffects: You know how to sleep through bells, chants, and morning duties.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Temple-Raised.narratorUse: Clergy may recognize your discipline, but your conduct may be judged against temple expectations.
Standing: Temple-Raised.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Military Family.name: Standing: Military Family
Standing: Military Family.description: You were raised around soldiers, officers, barracks, campaign stories, watch rotations, or military discipline.
Standing: Military Family.traitNarrativeEffects: You stand straighter when someone calls cadence.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Military Family.narratorUse: Soldiers may treat you as someone who understands chain of command, war stories, and military burdens.
Standing: Military Family.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Outcast.name: Standing: Outcast
Standing: Outcast.description: You were cast out, shunned, disowned, exiled, rejected, or pushed to the edges of polite society.
Standing: Outcast.traitNarrativeEffects: You always identify the quickest exit before sitting down.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Outcast.narratorUse: Other outcasts may sympathize, while respectable society may mistrust or ignore you.
Standing: Outcast.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Foreigner.name: Standing: Foreigner
Standing: Foreigner.description: You are visibly or culturally from elsewhere. Your manners, accent, dress, faith, papers, or customs mark you as outside the local norm.
Standing: Foreigner.traitNarrativeEffects: You collect local rules quickly, usually after breaking one.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Foreigner.narratorUse: Locals may be curious, suspicious, helpful, exploitative, or dismissive depending on region.
Standing: Foreigner.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Criminal Record.name: Standing: Criminal Record
Standing: Criminal Record.description: You are known, suspected, branded, documented, or rumored to have committed a crime.
Standing: Criminal Record.traitNarrativeEffects: You know how law sounds when it is looking for someone.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Criminal Record.narratorUse: Authorities may scrutinize you, while underworld contacts may recognize usefulness or liability.
Standing: Criminal Record.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Standing: Free Wanderer.name: Standing: Free Wanderer
Standing: Free Wanderer.description: You belong to the road more than any town, court, temple, guild, or border. You are known as a traveler without fixed allegiance.
Standing: Free Wanderer.traitNarrativeEffects: You judge places by water, shelter, road quality, and how quickly strangers ask questions.
unlockedBy: Selected as Social Standing during character creation.
Standing: Free Wanderer.narratorUse: Travelers, caravan folk, and roadside communities may accept your independence more readily than settled authorities.
Standing: Free Wanderer.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Family Obligation.name: Bond: Family Obligation
Bond: Family Obligation.description: You owe something to family: protection, money, inheritance, rescue, obedience, revenge, or the burden of a name.
Bond: Family Obligation.traitNarrativeEffects: Messages from home can ruin your whole posture.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Family Obligation.narratorUse: Use family as a source of requests, guilt, support, pressure, or personal stakes.
Bond: Family Obligation.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Old Mentor.name: Bond: Old Mentor
Bond: Old Mentor.description: Someone taught you, saved you, trained you, or gave you the words that still guide you.
Bond: Old Mentor.traitNarrativeEffects: You still hear their voice when you are about to do something foolish.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Old Mentor.narratorUse: Use the mentor for guidance, letters, tests, warnings, unfinished lessons, or complications.
Bond: Old Mentor.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Rival.name: Bond: Rival
Bond: Rival.description: Someone out there measures themselves against you, and you against them.
Bond: Rival.traitNarrativeEffects: You practice arguments with them even when they are nowhere nearby.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Rival.narratorUse: Use the rival as competition, recurring tension, a foil, or an unexpected ally under pressure.
Bond: Rival.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Guild Contact.name: Bond: Guild Contact
Bond: Guild Contact.description: You have a contact in a guild, hall, trade house, lodge, company, or professional network.
Bond: Guild Contact.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively look for coded marks on job boards and shop signs.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Guild Contact.narratorUse: Use the contact for rumors, jobs, introductions, fees, favors, or guild obligations.
Bond: Guild Contact.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Military Comrade.name: Bond: Military Comrade
Bond: Military Comrade.description: You have someone from past service, training, guard duty, campaign work, or battlefield survival who still matters to you.
Bond: Military Comrade.traitNarrativeEffects: Old marching songs catch in your throat at inconvenient times.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Military Comrade.narratorUse: Use the comrade for aid, old wounds, shared secrets, requests, or war-related consequences.
Bond: Military Comrade.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Temple Patron.name: Bond: Temple Patron
Bond: Temple Patron.description: A priest, temple, shrine, monastery, or faithful benefactor has a claim on your loyalty or gratitude.
Bond: Temple Patron.traitNarrativeEffects: You remember temple etiquette even when you resent it.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Temple Patron.narratorUse: Use the patron for sanctuary, moral pressure, healing, requests, religious obligations, or conflict with worldly choices.
Bond: Temple Patron.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Debt Holder.name: Bond: Debt Holder
Bond: Debt Holder.description: Someone holds a debt over you, whether coin, favor, blood-price, contract, rescue, or blackmail.
Bond: Debt Holder.traitNarrativeEffects: You count every unexpected kindness twice.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Debt Holder.narratorUse: Use the debt as pressure, negotiation, danger, a hook, or a reason the character cannot simply walk away.
Bond: Debt Holder.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Beast Companion.name: Bond: Beast Companion
Bond: Beast Companion.description: A specific animal, pet, mount, hatchling, or tameable creature matters to you, whether already beside you or waiting somewhere safe.
Bond: Beast Companion.traitNarrativeEffects: You save food scraps without thinking about it.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Beast Companion.narratorUse: Use the companion as emotional stake, travel aid, pet-system hook, loyalty test, or source of complications.
Bond: Beast Companion.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Disgraced House.name: Bond: Disgraced House
Bond: Disgraced House.description: Your name, family, order, clan, or house carries disgrace, and some part of you still answers to that burden.
Bond: Disgraced House.traitNarrativeEffects: You notice heraldry and family names before faces.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Disgraced House.narratorUse: Use the disgraced house for reputation, inheritance, enemies, restoration, shame, or political hooks.
Bond: Disgraced House.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Missing Parent.name: Bond: Missing Parent
Bond: Missing Parent.description: One of your parents is gone, lost, hidden, taken, exiled, dead under uncertain terms, or missing from the truth of your life.
Bond: Missing Parent.traitNarrativeEffects: You study strangers of the right age longer than you should.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Missing Parent.narratorUse: Use the missing parent as mystery, rumor, inheritance, identity question, or emotional hook.
Bond: Missing Parent.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Bond: Lost Lover.name: Bond: Lost Lover
Bond: Lost Lover.description: Someone you loved is gone, unreachable, estranged, dead, cursed, missing, or changed beyond easy return.
Bond: Lost Lover.traitNarrativeEffects: Certain songs, roads, or weather make you quieter.
unlockedBy: Selected as Starting Bond during character creation.
Bond: Lost Lover.narratorUse: Use the lost lover carefully as longing, unresolved grief, rumor, reunion, or moral complication.
Bond: Lost Lover.balanceRole: Identity trait: provides motivation, social context, story hooks, small skill modifiers, and narrative permissions rather than raw attribute growth.

Draconic Bond (Sylvareth).name: Draconic Bond (Sylvareth)
Draconic Bond (Sylvareth).description: Silas is soul-linked and bonded with Sylvareth. Their minds are linked, allowing them to sense each other's thoughts, share feelings, memories, and physical abilities such as Sylvareth's draconic sight. They always know exactly where each other are, can feel one another's emotional state, and can communicate mentally across long distances. This bond is intimate, sacred, and mutual; it is not ownership, command, or a rider bond.
Draconic Bond (Sylvareth).traitNarrativeEffects: Soul-bonded to Sylvareth; shares thought, feeling, location sense, memories, and draconic perception.
Draconic Bond (Sylvareth).hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as Bond Trait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Valenian Ingenuity.name: Valenian Ingenuity
Valenian Ingenuity.description: Raised in or strongly influenced by Valen's culture of industry, invention, practical research, civic engineering, and battlefield problem-solving. Grants +5 Crafting and +5 Investigation.
Valenian Ingenuity.traitNarrativeEffects: Valenian practical ingenuity: trained to solve problems through tools, craft, investigation, engineering, and disciplined nonmagical reasoning.
Valenian Ingenuity.hiddenInfo: Special/non-character-creator trait originally classified as bloodlineTrait; grant through story, vocation, trigger, or narrator logic as appropriate.

Dungeoneer.name: Dungeoneer
Dungeoneer.description: Dungeoneers are professional ruin-delvers, tomb-breakers, trap-spotters, lock-workers, and expedition survivors. They know how to move through abandoned fortresses, old temples, collapsed mines, buried cities, monster lairs, and cursed ruins without dying to the first suspicious floor tile. A Dungeoneer is not necessarily a scholar or thief; their strength is practical danger-sense: noticing the seam in the wall, the false stone, the bad air, the valuable scrap, the unstable ceiling, and the treasure chest that is probably not worth opening without a plan.
Dungeoneer.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively check doorframes, floor seams, old statues, suspiciously clean walls, bad air, and treasure piles before trusting them.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Dungeoneer.vocationRole: Dungeon exploration, stealth, lockpicking, trap detection and disarming, ruins, relic recovery, hazard reading, enclosed-space survival, and appraising suspicious finds.

Certified Slayer.name: Certified Slayer
Certified Slayer.description: You carry official Slayer Guild certification identifying you as a recognized member of the Slayers Guild. This authorizes you to take legal Slayer contracts across Balen, access guild branches, consult hunt boards, report monster activity, sell legal monster harvests, and receive guild payment for completed work. Your certification also trains you to read contracts for danger signs: underreported threat levels, suspicious client omissions, illegal targets, protected species risk, poaching setups, Red-Guard audit concerns, and jobs that should be reported to guild leadership. This certification does not place you above local law; national restrictions, endangered species protections, anti-poaching rules, Red-Guard oversight, and dragon-sentience laws still apply.
Certified Slayer.traitNarrativeEffects: Your first instinct around dangerous creatures or suspicious contracts is to check signs, legality, harvest value, client wording, and guild procedure before drawing steel.
unlockedBy: Granted by the Slayer vocation.

Ravager.name: Ravager
Ravager.description: Ravagers are brutal Sublime Arts warriors who turn raw physical force into battlefield momentum. Where Bladesingers guide mana through rhythm and grace, Ravagers drive stamina through muscle, breath, and controlled violence. They favor greataxes, warhammers, and paired axes, using overwhelming charges, guard-breaking blows, and war cries that shake enemy resolve. A Ravager is not merely angry; they are trained to push the body to the edge without losing control.
Ravager.traitNarrativeEffects: When danger rises, your breathing deepens and your hands flex as if your body is preparing to break a battlefield open.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Ravager.vocationRole: Stamina-fueled Sublime Arts bruiser, battlefield charging, intimidation, heavy weapon damage, and defense-rending force.

vocationPerk.name: Rending Momentum
vocationPerk.description: You understand how to turn breath, strength, stance, and pain tolerance into battlefield pressure.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character read weak guards, break formations, force enemies back, and use strength-driven Sublime Arts without consuming mana or Breathing.

Priest.name: Priest
Priest.description: Priests are ordained light-magic casters trained to protect the living, bless allies, close wounds, resist darkness, and preserve morale under fear, grief, and corruption. Unlike Healers, who rely on medicine, alchemy, and triage, Priests use mana-shaped rites and deity-centered light magic. Their magic can seal wounds, stop bleeding, shield allies, and steady the spirit, but it cannot truly rebuild the body. Broken bones, ruined organs, lost tissue, and severe internal trauma still require mundane treatment, rest, alchemy, or true regenerative Dracomancy.
Priest.traitNarrativeEffects: You notice oaths, funeral customs, holy symbols, fear beneath brave faces, and the quiet moment when people need blessing more than advice.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Priest.vocationRole: Light-magic protection, blessings, wound closure, purification, rites, funerals, sanctuary customs, temple procedure, and spiritual authority.

vocationPerk.name: Ordained Priest
vocationPerk.description: You are recognized as a trained priest of Balen's fourfold faith, with authority to perform rites, offer blessings, conduct funerals, request sanctuary, identify religious obligations, and use defensive light magic.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let temples, faithful commoners, clergy, pilgrims, and religious officials respond to the character as recognized clergy. Priest magic can close wounds, shield allies, strengthen resolve, and oppose corruption, but it does not replace surgery, bone-setting, recovery care, or Dracomantic regeneration.

Spellsword.name: Spellsword
Spellsword.description: Spellswords are martial arcanists trained to bind runes, sigils, and magic circles into combat. They carve spell structure into blades, armor, scabbards, charms, chalk lines, and even the air itself, turning preparation and precision into battlefield magic. A Spellsword fights through timing, inscription, direct contact, and controlled mana release. Their blade is not only a weapon, but a writing tool for magic.
Spellsword.traitNarrativeEffects: You read blades, walls, thresholds, and armor as surfaces waiting for the right mark, and your fingers sometimes trace invisible sigils before a fight begins.
unlockedBy: Selected as starting Vocation during character creation.
Spellsword.vocationRole: Martial runic combat, prepared weapon inscriptions, combat sigils, defensive circles, contact brands, and practical enchantment work.

vocationPerk.name: Martial Rune-Caster
vocationPerk.description: You can inscribe simple runes on weapons, armor, scabbards, tools, and battlefield surfaces, then channel mana through those markings during combat. With focus, you can also trace temporary rune-lines and circle-lines through the air using mana carried by your weapon edge.
vocationPerk.narratorUse: Let the character understand combat inscriptions, use Runic Magic with weapon actions, recognize unstable sigils, prepare simple rune effects, and use rune/circle logic without treating them as a Magi-Knight or Sublime Arts practitioner.

Circle Enchanter.name: Circle Enchanter
Circle Enchanter.description: You can safely place a basic magic-circle enchantment on an item without risking catastrophic failure. If the item, materials, and circle design are appropriate, the enchantment succeeds at a basic functional level. Higher quality results, stronger effects, unusual materials, complex enchantments, or attempts to preserve rare bonuses still require Runic Magic, Crafting, and appropriate materials. This trait prevents simple enchantment work from failing outright; it does not guarantee masterwork quality.
Circle Enchanter.traitNarrativeEffects: You inspect objects for whether they can hold a complete circuit before thinking about what power to place inside them.
unlockedBy: Granted by the Spellsword vocation.

Runic Branding.name: Runic Branding
Runic Branding.description: You are trained to brand combat runes into a target through direct contact. When your weapon, hand, tattooed palm, etched focus, or prepared ritual implement touches a target, you can speak a prepared rune aloud and force the sigil to flare against them. Runic Branding requires contact. If the attack or touch misses, is dodged, parried, blocked, interrupted, or otherwise fails to establish contact, the brand does not form. The spoken rune determines the effect: Ignis brands fire, Aero brands forceful wind, Fulcra brands lightning, and Icis brands frost.
Runic Branding.traitNarrativeEffects: You tend to speak rune-words with exact pronunciation, as if a careless syllable might cut the wrong line into the world.
unlockedBy: Granted by the Spellsword vocation.
Granted by the Runescribe vocation.

Avoidance.name: Avoidance
Avoidance.description: You are trained to escape detection in areas with cover, broken sightlines, crowds, brush, ruins, darkness, smoke, clutter, or uneven terrain. When there is something to hide behind, move through, blend into, or break line of sight with, you can vanish from attention far more easily than an untrained traveler. Avoidance is mundane stealth, not shadow magic; it relies on cover, timing, quiet movement, posture, patience, and knowing where enemies are not looking.
Avoidance.traitNarrativeEffects: Your eyes instinctively mark the next piece of cover before you move, and you rarely stand where a straight line of sight can hold you for long.
unlockedBy: Granted by the Scout vocation.

Runic Tattooing.name: Runic Tattooing
Runic Tattooing.description: You can place stable runes or magic circles onto your own body, using ritual ink, scar-line channels, mana-reactive pigment, or tattooed circuit patterns to create protective inscriptions in place of armor. While not wearing armor, your runic tattoos grant +2 physical Damage Resistance. These markings do not count as worn armor, but they must remain visible, intact, and magically stable to function. This protection can be weakened by disrupted rune lines, anti-magic effects, damaged skin, mana-drain seals, or effects that interfere with magic-circle structure. This protection does not stack with armor-based Damage Resistance unless a specific enchantment or item says otherwise.
Runic Tattooing.traitNarrativeEffects: You instinctively protect tattooed circuit-lines from cuts, smudging, and clothing that would interrupt the pattern.
unlockedBy: Granted by the Runescribe vocation.
Runic Tattooing.restriction: Only while unarmored and while the body-runes remain visible, intact, and magically stable. Does not stack with armor-based Damage Resistance unless a specific enchantment says otherwise.

Efficient Circlework.name: Efficient Circlework
Efficient Circlework.description: You are trained to power runes and magic circles with minimal mana.
